documents with a filter from a node (used for shard migration).\n304\t    async fn fetch_documents(\n305\t        &self,\n306\t        _node: &NodeId,\n307\t        _address: &str,\n308\t        _request: &FetchDocumentsRequest,\n309\t    ) -> std::result::Result<FetchDocumentsResponse, NodeError> {\n310\t        Ok(FetchDocumentsResponse {\n311\t            results: Vec::new(),\n312\t            limit: _request.limit,\n313\t            offset: _request.offset,\n314\t            total: 0,\n315\t        })\n316\t    }\n317\t}\n318\t\n319\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n320\tpub enum NodeError {\n321\t    Timeout,\n322\t    HttpError { status: u16, body: String },\n323\t    NetworkError(String),\n324\t}\n325\t\n326\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n327\tpub struct SearchRequest {\n328\t    pub index_uid: String,\n329\t    pub query: Option<String>,\n330\t    pub offset: usize,\n331\t    pub limit: usize,\n332\t    pub filter: Option<Value>,\n333\t    pub facets: Option<Vec<String>>,\n334\t    pub ranking_score: bool,\n335\t    pub body: Value,\n336\t    /// Global IDF data from the preflight phase (OP#4).\n337\t    pub global_idf: Option<GlobalIdf>,\n338\t}\n339\t\n340\timpl SearchRequest {\n341\t    /// Build the request body for sending to a node.\n342\t    ///\n343\t    /// Injects `showRankingScore: true` unconditionally so the merger can global-sort.\n344\t    /// Each node receives `offset + limit` results to ensure the coordinator has enough\n345\t    /// data to apply pagination.\n346\t    pub fn to_node_body(&self) -> Value {\n347\t        let mut body = self.body.clone();\n348\t\n349\t        // Inject showRankingScore: true unconditionally for global sorting\n350\t        body[\"showRankingScore\"] = serde_json::json!(true);\n351\t\n352\t        // Set limit to offset + limit so we get enough results for pagination\n353\t        // (coordinator applies final offset/limit after merging)\n354\t        body[\"limit\"] = serde_json::json!(self.offset + self.limit);\n355\t\n356\t        // Set offset to 0 on individual nodes (coordinator handles offset)\n357\t        body[\"offset\"] = serde_json::json!(0);\n358\t\n359\t        // Ensure query is set\n360\t        if let Some(q) = &self.query {\n361\t            body[\"q\"] = serde_json::json!(q);\n362\t        }\n363\t\n364\t        // Ensure filter is set if provided\n365\t        if let Some(filter) = &self.filter {\n366\t            body[\"filter\"] = filter.clone();\n367\t        }\n368\t\n369\t        // Ensure facets are set if provided\n370\t        if let Some(facets) = &self.facets {\n371\t            body[\"facets\"] = serde_json::json!(facets);\n372\t        }\n373\t\n374\t        body\n375\t    }\n376\t}\n377\t\n378\t#[derive(Debug)]\n379\tpub struct ScatterResult {\n380\t    pub shard_pages: Vec<ShardHitPage>,\n381\t    pub failed_shards: HashMap<u32, NodeError>,\n382\t    pub partial: bool,\n383\t    pub deadline_exceeded: bool,\n384\t}\n385\t\n386\t#[instrument(skip_all, fields(query_seq, rf, shard_count))]\n387\tpub async fn plan_search_scatter(\n388\t    topology: &Topology,\n389\t    query_seq: u64,\n390\t    rf: usize,\n391\t    shard_count: u32,\n392\t    replica_selector: Option<&ReplicaSelector>,\n393\t) -> ScatterPlan {\n394\t    let chosen_group = query_group(query_seq, topology.replica_group_count());\n395\t\n396\t    let group = match topology.group(chosen_group) {\n397\t        Some(g) => g,\n398\t        None => {\n399\t            return ScatterPlan {\n400\t                chosen_group, target_shards: Vec::new(),\n401\t                shard_to_node: HashMap::new(), deadline_ms: 0, hedging_eligible: false,\n402\t            };\n403\t        }\n404\t    };\n405\t\n406\t    let _covering = covering_set(shard_count, group, rf, query_seq);\n407\t\n408\t    let mut shard_to_node = HashMap::new();\n409\t    for shard_id in 0..shard_count {\n410\t        let replicas = crate::router::assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, group.nodes(), rf);\n411\t\n412\t        let selected = if let Some(selector) = replica_selector {\n413\t            match selector.select(&replicas, chosen_group).await {\n414\t                Some(node) => node,\n415\t                None => replicas[(query_seq as usize) % replicas.len()].clone(),\n416\t            }\n417\t        } else {\n418\t            replicas[(query_seq as usize) % replicas.len()].clone()\n419\t        };\n420\t\n421\t        shard_to_node.insert(shard_id, selected);\n422\t    }\n423\t\n424\t    ScatterPlan {\n425\t        chosen_group,\n426\t        target_shards: (0..shard_count).collect(),\n427\t        shard_to_node,\n428\t        deadline_ms: 5000,\n429\t        hedging_eligible: group.node_count() > 1,\n430\t    }\n431\t}\n432\t\n433\t/// Plan search scatter with settings version floor filtering (plan §13.5).\n434\t///\n435\t/// Excludes nodes whose settings version for the given index is below `floor`.\n436\t/// Returns None if no covering set can be assembled (caller should return 503).\n437\tpub async fn plan_search_scatter_with_version_floor(\n438\t    topology: &Topology,\n439\t    query_seq: u64,\n440\t    rf: usize,\n441\t    shard_count: u32,\n442\t    index: &str,\n443\t    floor: u64,\n444\t    version_checker: &impl Fn(&str, &str) -> u64,\n445\t    replica_selector: Option<&ReplicaSelector>,\n446\t) -> Option<ScatterPlan> {\n447\t    let chosen_group = query_group(query_seq, topology.replica_group_count());\n448\t\n449\t    let group = topology.group(chosen_group)?;\n450\t\n451\t    let covering = covering_set_with_version_floor(\n452\t        shard_count,\n453\t        group,\n454\t        rf,\n455\t        query_seq,\n456\t        index,\n457\t        floor,\n458\t        version_checker,\n459\t    )?;\n460\t\n461\t    let mut shard_to_node = HashMap::new();\n462\t    for shard_id in 0..shard_count {\n463\t        let replicas = crate::router::assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, group.nodes(), rf);\n464\t        // Filter by version floor\n465\t        let eligible: Vec<_> = replicas\n466\t            .iter()\n467\t            .filter(|node_id| {\n468\t                let version = version_checker(index, node_id.as_str());\n469\t                version >= floor\n470\t            })\n471\t            .collect();\n472\t\n473\t        if eligible.is_empty() {\n474\t            return None;\n475\t        }\n476\t\n477\t        let selected = if let Some(selector) = replica_selector {\n478\t            // Convert Vec<&NodeId> to Vec<NodeId> for selector\n479\t            let eligible_owned: Vec<NodeId> = eligible.iter().map(|&n| n.clone()).collect();\n480\t            match selector.select(&eligible_owned, chosen_group).await {\n481\t                Some(node) => node,\n482\t                None => eligible[query_seq as usize % eligible.len()].clone(),\n483\t            }\n484\t        } else {\n485\t            eligible[query_seq as usize % eligible.len()].clone()\n486\t        };\n487\t\n488\t        shard_to_node.insert(shard_id, selected.clone());\n489\t    }\n490\t\n491\t    Some(ScatterPlan {\n492\t        chosen_group,\n493\t        target_shards: (0..shard_count).collect(),\n494\t        shard_to_node,\n495\t        deadline_ms: 5000,\n496\t        hedging_eligible: group.node_count() > 1,\n497\t    })\n498\t}\n499\t\n500\t/// Plan search scatter for a specific replica group (plan §13.6 session pinning).\n501\t///\n502\t/// Used when a session has a pending write and needs to read from the pinned group\n503\t/// to ensure read-your-writes consistency.\n504\tpub async fn plan_search_scatter_for_group(\n505\t    topology: &Topology,\n506\t    query_seq: u64,\n507\t    rf: usize,\n508\t    shard_count: u32,\n509\t    pinned_group: u32,\n510\t    replica_selector: Option<&ReplicaSelector>,\n511\t) -> Option<ScatterPlan> {\n512\t    let group = topology.group(pinned_group)?;\n513\t\n514\t    let mut shard_to_node = HashMap::new();\n515\t    for shard_id in 0..shard_count {\n516\t        let replicas = crate::router::assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, group.nodes(), rf);\n517\t        if replicas.is_empty() {\n518\t            continue;\n519\t        }\n520\t\n521\t        let selected = if let Some(selector) = replica_selector {\n522\t            match selector.select(&replicas, pinned_group).await {\n523\t                Some(node) => node,\n524\t                None => replicas[query_seq as usize % replicas.len()].clone(),\n525\t            }\n526\t        } else {\n527\t            replicas[query_seq as usize % replicas.len()].clone()\n528\t        };\n529\t\n530\t        shard_to_node.insert(shard_id, selected);\n531\t    }\n532\t\n533\t    Some(ScatterPlan {\n534\t        chosen_group: pinned_group,\n535\t        target_shards: (0..shard_count).collect(),\n536\t        shard_to_node,\n537\t        deadline_ms: 5000,\n538\t        hedging_eligible: group.node_count() > 1,\n539\t    })\n540\t}\n541\t\n542\t/// Plan search scatter using adaptive replica selection (plan §13.3).\n543\t///\n544\t/// Uses EWMA-based scoring to select the best replica for each shard,\n545\t/// falling back to round-robin for shards with no metrics data.\n546\t#[instrument(skip_all, fields(query_seq, rf, shard_count))]\n547\tpub async fn plan_search_scatter_adaptive(\n548\t    topology: &Topology,\n549\t    query_seq: u64,\n550\t    rf: usize,\n551\t    shard_count: u32,\n552\t    replica_selector: &ReplicaSelector,\n553\t) -> ScatterPlan {\n554\t    let chosen_group = query_group(query_seq, topology.replica_group_count());\n555\t\n556\t    let group = match topology.group(chosen_group) {\n557\t        Some(g) => g,\n558\t        None => {\n559\t            return ScatterPlan {\n560\t                chosen_group,\n561\t                target_shards: Vec::new(),\n562\t                shard_to_node: HashMap::new(),\n563\t                deadline_ms: 0,\n564\t                hedging_eligible: false,\n565\t            };\n566\t        }\n567\t    };\n568\t\n569\t    let mut shard_to_node = HashMap::new();\n570\t    for shard_id in 0..shard_count {\n571\t        let replicas = crate::router::assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, group.nodes(), rf);\n572\t        if replicas.is_empty() {\n573\t            continue;\n574\t        }\n575\t\n576\t        // Use adaptive selection to pick the best replica\n577\t        let selected = match replica_selector.select(&replicas, chosen_group).await {\n578\t            Some(node) => node,\n579\t            None => {\n580\t                // Fallback to round-robin if selector returns None\n581\t                replicas[(query_seq as usize) % replicas.len()].clone()\n582\t            }\n583\t        };\n584\t\n585\t        shard_to_node.insert(shard_id, selected);\n586\t    }\n587\t\n588\t    ScatterPlan {\n589\t        chosen_group,\n590\t        target_shards: (0..shard_count).collect(),\n591\t        shard_to_node,\n592\t        deadline_ms: 5000,\n593\t        hedging_eligible: group.node_count() > 1,\n594\t    }\n595\t}\n596\t\n597\t#[instrument(skip_all, fields(node_count))]\n598\tpub async fn execute_scatter<C: NodeClient>(\n599\t    plan: ScatterPlan,\n600\t    client: &C,\n601\t    req: SearchRequest,\n602\t    topology: &Topology,\n603\t    policy: UnavailableShardPolicy,\n604\t) -> Result<ScatterResult> {\n605\t    let mut node_to_shards: HashMap<NodeId, Vec<u32>> = HashMap::new();\n606\t    for (&shard_id, node_id) in &plan.shard_to_node {\n607\t        if plan.target_shards.contains(&shard_id) {\n608\t            node_to_shards.entry(node_id.clone()).or_default().push(shard_id);\n609\t        }\n610\t    }\n611\t    tracing::Span::current().record(\"node_count\", node_to_shards.len());\n612\t\n613\t    let mut shard_pages = Vec::new();\n614\t    let mut failed_shards = HashMap::new();\n615\t    let mut deadline_exceeded = false;\n616\t\n617\t    let mut tasks = Vec::new();\n618\t    for (node_id, shards) in node_to_shards {\n619\t        let node = match topology.node(&node_id) {\n620\t            Some(n) => n.clone(),\n621\t            None => {\n622\t                for shard_id in shards {\n623\t                    failed_shards.insert(shard_id, NodeError::NetworkError(\"node not in topology\".to_string()));\n624\t                }\n625\t                continue;\n626\t            }\n627\t        };\n628\t        let client_ref = client;\n629\t        let req_clone = req.clone();\n630\t        let node_id_clone = node_id.clone();\n631\t        let shard_count = shards.len();\n632\t        // Create a span for this node's scatter call\n633\t        let span = info_span!(\n634\t            \"scatter_node\",\n635\t            node_id = %node_id_clone,\n636\t            address = %node.address,\n637\t            shard_count = shard_count,\n638\t        );\n639\t        tasks.push(async move {\n640\t            let result = client_ref.search_node(&node_id_clone, &node.address, &req_clone).await;\n641\t            (node_id_clone, shards, result)\n642\t        }.instrument(span));\n643\t    }\n644\t\n645\t    let results = futures_util::future::join_all(tasks).await;\n646\t\n647\t    for (_node_id, shards, result) in results {\n648\t        match result {\n649\t            Ok(body) => {\n650\t                for _shard_id in shards {\n651\t                    shard_pages.push(ShardHitPage { body: body.clone() });\n652\t                }\n653\t            }\n654\t            Err(NodeError::Timeout) => {\n655\t                deadline_exceeded = true;\n656\t                for shard_id in shards { failed_shards.insert(shard_id, NodeError::Timeout); }\n657\t            }\n658\t            Err(e) => {\n659\t                for shard_id in shards { failed_shards.insert(shard_id, e.clone()); }\n660\t            }\n661\t        }\n662\t    }\n663\t\n664\t    let partial = !failed_shards.is_empty();\n665\t\n666\t    match policy {\n667\t        UnavailableShardPolicy::Error => {\n668\t            if !failed_shards.is_empty() {\n669\t                return Err(crate::error::MiroirError::Routing(format!(\"{} shard(s) unavailable\", failed_shards.len())));\n670\t            }\n671\t        }\n672\t        UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial => {}\n673\t        UnavailableShardPolicy::Fallback => {\n674\t            // Group-unavailability fallback: try other replica groups for failed shards\n675\t            if !failed_shards.is_empty() {\n676\t                let mut fallback_pages = Vec::new();\n677\t                let mut remaining_failed = HashMap::new();\n678\t\n679\t                for (&shard_id, error) in &failed_shards {\n680\t                    let mut fallback_succeeded = false;\n681\t\n682\t                    // Try each other replica group\n683\t                    for group_id in 0..topology.replica_group_count() {\n684\t                        if group_id == plan.chosen_group {\n685\t                            continue; // Skip the already-tried group\n686\t                        }\n687\t\n688\t                        if let Some(group) = topology.group(group_id) {\n689\t                            let replicas = crate::router::assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, group.nodes(), topology.rf());\n690\t                            if replicas.is_empty() {\n691\t                                continue;\n692\t                            }\n693\t\n694\t                            // Try each replica in the fallback group\n695\t                            for node_id in replicas {\n696\t                                if let Some(node) = topology.node(&node_id) {\n697\t                                    match client.search_node(&node_id, &node.address, &req).await {\n698\t                                        Ok(body) => {\n699\t                                            fallback_pages.push(ShardHitPage { body });\n700\t                                            fallback_succeeded = true;\n701\t                                            break;\n702\t                                        }\n703\t                                        Err(_) => {\n704\t                                            continue; // Try next replica\n705\t                                        }\n706\t                                    }\n707\t                                }\n708\t                            }\n709\t\n710\t                            if fallback_succeeded {\n711\t                                break; // Found a working replica\n712\t                            }\n713\t                        }\n714\t                    }\n715\t\n716\t                    if !fallback_succeeded {\n717\t                        remaining_failed.insert(shard_id, error.clone());\n718\t                    }\n719\t                }\n720\t\n721\t                // Merge fallback results\n722\t                shard_pages.extend(fallback_pages);\n723\t\n724\t                // Update failed_shards with only those that truly failed\n725\t                if remaining_failed.is_empty() {\n726\t                    // All shards succeeded via fallback\n727\t                    return Ok(ScatterResult {\n728\t                        shard_pages,\n729\t                        failed_shards: HashMap::new(),\n730\t                        partial: false,\n731\t                        deadline_exceeded,\n732\t                    });\n733\t                } else {\n734\t                    // Some shards still failed\n735\t                    failed_shards = remaining_failed;\n736\t                }\n737\t            }\n738\t        }\n739\t    }\n740\t\n741\t    Ok(ScatterResult { shard_pages, failed_shards, partial, deadline_exceeded })\n742\t}\n743\t\n744\t#[instrument(skip_all, fields(index = %req.index_uid))]\n745\tpub async fn scatter_gather_search<C: NodeClient>(\n746\t    plan: ScatterPlan,\n747\t    client: &C,\n748\t    req: SearchRequest,\n749\t    topology: &Topology,\n750\t    policy: UnavailableShardPolicy,\n751\t    strategy: &dyn MergeStrategy,\n752\t) -> Result<MergedSearchResult> {\n753\t    let scatter_result = execute_scatter(plan, client, req.clone(), topology, policy).await?;\n754\t\n755\t    let mut shard_pages = scatter_result.shard_pages;\n756\t    if scatter_result.partial {\n757\t        for shard_id in scatter_result.failed_shards.keys() {\n758\t            shard_pages.push(ShardHitPage {\n759\t                body: serde_json::json!({\"success\": false, \"message\": format!(\"shard {} unavailable\", shard_id)}),\n760\t            });\n761\t        }\n762\t    }\n763\t\n764\t    // Collect failed shard IDs for the X-Miroir-Degraded header\n765\t    let failed_shards: Vec<u32> = scatter_result.failed_shards.keys().copied().collect();\n766\t\n767\t    let merge_input = MergeInput {\n768\t        shard_hits: shard_pages,\n769\t        offset: req.offset,\n770\t        limit: req.limit,\n771\t        client_requested_score: req.ranking_score,\n772\t        facets: req.facets.clone(),\n773\t        failed_shards,\n774\t    };\n775\t\n776\t    // Span for the merge operation\n777\t    let _span = info_span!(\n778\t        \"merge\",\n779\t        shard_count = merge_input.shard_hits.len(),\n780\t        offset = req.offset,\n781\t        limit = req.limit,\n782\t    ).entered();\n783\t\n784\t    strategy.merge(merge_input)\n785\t}\n786\t\n787\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n788\t// OP#4: Global-IDF preflight execution\n789\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n790\t\n791\t/// Extract unique query terms from a search query string.\n792\tpub fn extract_query_terms(query: &Option<String>) -> Vec<String> {\n793\t    match query {\n794\t        Some(q) if !q.is_empty() => {\n795\t            let mut seen = std::collections::HashSet::new();\n796\t            let mut terms = Vec::new();\n797\t            for term in q.split_whitespace() {\n798\t                let lower = term.to_lowercase();\n799\t                if seen.insert(lower.clone()) { terms.push(lower); }\n800\t            }\n801\t            terms\n802\t        }\n803\t        _ => Vec::new(),\n804\t    }\n805\t}\n806\t\n807\t/// Execute the preflight phase: gather term frequencies from all shards.\n808\t#[instrument(skip_all, fields(node_count, term_count = req.terms.len()))]\n809\tpub async fn execute_preflight<C: NodeClient>(\n810\t    plan: &ScatterPlan,\n811\t    client: &C,\n812\t    req: &PreflightRequest,\n813\t    topology: &Topology,\n814\t) -> Result<GlobalIdf> {\n815\t    if req.terms.is_empty() {\n816\t        return Ok(GlobalIdf { total_docs: 0, avg_doc_length: 0.0, terms: HashMap::new() });\n817\t    }\n818\t\n819\t    let mut node_to_shards: HashMap<NodeId, Vec<u32>> = HashMap::new();\n820\t    for (&shard_id, node_id) in &plan.shard_to_node {\n821\t        if plan.target_shards.contains(&shard_id) {\n822\t            node_to_shards.entry(node_id.clone()).or_default().push(shard_id);\n823\t        }\n824\t    }\n825\t\n826\t    let mut tasks = Vec::new();\n827\t    for (node_id, _) in node_to_shards {\n828\t        let node = match topology.node(&node_id) {\n829\t            Some(n) => n.clone(),\n830\t            None => continue,\n831\t        };\n832\t        let client_ref = client;\n833\t        let req_clone = req.clone();\n834\t        let nid = node_id.clone();\n835\t        tasks.push(async move { client_ref.preflight_node(&nid, &node.address, &req_clone).await });\n836\t    }\n837\t\n838\t    let results = futures_util::future::join_all(tasks).await;\n839\t    let responses: Vec<PreflightResponse> = results.into_iter().filter_map(|r| r.ok()).collect();\n840\t    Ok(GlobalIdf::from_preflight_responses(&responses))\n841\t}\n842\t\n843\t/// Execute a full dfs_query_then_fetch search (OP#4 global-IDF preflight).\n844\t#[instrument(skip_all, fields(index = %req.index_uid))]\n845\tpub async fn dfs_query_then_fetch_search<C: NodeClient>(\n846\t    plan: ScatterPlan,\n847\t    client: &C,\n848\t    req: SearchRequest,\n849\t    topology: &Topology,\n850\t    policy: UnavailableShardPolicy,\n851\t    strategy: &dyn MergeStrategy,\n852\t) -> Result<MergedSearchResult> {\n853\t    let preflight_req = PreflightRequest {\n854\t        index_uid: req.index_uid.clone(),\n855\t        terms: extract_query_terms(&req.query),\n856\t        filter: req.filter.clone(),\n857\t    };\n858\t    let global_idf = execute_preflight(&plan, client, &preflight_req, topology).await?;\n859\t    let mut search_req = req;\n860\t    search_req.global_idf = Some(global_idf);\n861\t    scatter_gather_search(plan, client, search_req, topology, policy, strategy).await\n862\t}\n863\t\n864\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n865\t// Mock }\n962\t\n963\t        Ok(stored)\n964\t    }\n965\t\n966\t    fn get_task_status(\n967\t        &self,\n968\t        node: &NodeId,\n969\t        _address: &str,\n970\t        _request: = make_test_topology();\n1162\t        let plan = plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, 2, 64, None).await;\n1163\t        let mut c = MockNodeClient::default();\n1164\t        c.responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-0\".into()), serde_json::json!({\"hits\": [{\"id\": \"a\", \"_rankingScore\": 0.9}], \"estimatedTotalHits\": 1, \"processingTimeMs\": 5}));\n1165\t        c.preflight_responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-0\".into()), PreflightResponse {\n1166\t            total_docs: 50000, avg_doc_length: 50.0,\n1167\t            term_stats:  }\n1174\t\n1175\t    /// Integration test for dfs_query_then_fetch with severely skewed shard distribution.\n1176\t    ///\n1177\t    /// This test simulates the scenario described in miroir-yio:\n1178\t    /// - Shard 0: Normal (1,000 docs, term df = 100)\n1179\t    /// - Shard 1: 100x normal (100,000 docs, term df = 10,000)\n1180\t    /// - Shard 2: Near-empty (10 docs, term df = 1)\n1181\t    ///\n1182\t    /// Without global IDF preflight, each shard would compute different local IDF values:\n1183\t    /// - Shard 0: IDF ≈ log((1000 - 100 + 0.5) / (100 + 0.5)) + 1 ≈ 2.3\n1184\t    /// - Shard 1: IDF ≈ log((100000 - 10000 + 0.5) / (10000 + 0.5)) + 1 ≈ 2.3\n1185\t    /// - Shard 2: IDF ≈ log((10 - 1 + 0.5) / (1 + 0.5)) + 1 ≈ 2.8\n1186\t    ///\n1187\t    /// With global IDF preflight, all shards use the same IDF:\n1188\t    /// - Global: N = 101,010, df = 10,101\n1189\t    /// - IDF ≈ log((101010 - 10101 + 0.5) / (10101 + 0.5)) + 1 ≈ 2.3\n1190\t    ///\n1191\t    /// This ensures scores are comparable across shards, enabling correct score-based merge.\n1192\t    #[tokio::test]\n1193\t    async fn test_dfs_skewed_shards_global_idf_aggregation() {\n1194\t        let mut topo = Topology::new(3, 1, 1);\n1195\t        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-0\".into()), \"http://node-0:7700\".into(), 0));\n1196\t        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-1\".into()), \"http://node-1:7700\".into(), 0));\n1197\t        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-2\".into()), \"http://node-2:7700\".into(), 0));\n1198\t\n1199\t        let plan = plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, 1, 3, None).await;\n1200\t\n1201\t        // Simulate severely skewed shard distribution\n1202\t        let mut c = MockNodeClient::default();\n1203\t\n1204\t        // Shard 0: Normal distribution\n1205\t        c.preflight_responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-0\".into()), PreflightResponse {\n1206\t            total_docs: 1000,\n1207\t            avg_doc_length: 50.0,\n1208\t            term_stats: HashMap::from([\n1209\t                (\"machine\".into(), TermStats { df: 100 }),\n1210\t                (\"learning\".into(), TermStats { df: 80 }),\n1211\t            ]),\n1212\t        });\n1213\t\n1214\t        // Shard 1: 100x normal (severely skewed)\n1215\t        c.preflight_responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-1\".into()), PreflightResponse {\n1216\t            total_docs: 100_000,\n1217\t            avg_doc_length: 55.0,\n1218\t            term_stats: HashMap::from([\n1219\t                (\"machine\".into(), TermStats { df: 10_000 }),\n1220\t                (\"learning\".into(), TermStats { df: 8_000 }),\n1221\t            ]),\n1222\t        });\n1223\t\n1224\t        // Shard 2: Near-empty\n1225\t        c.preflight_responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-2\".into()), PreflightResponse {\n1226\t            total_docs: 10,\n1227\t            avg_doc_length: 45.0,\n1228\t            term_stats: HashMap::from([\n1229\t                (\"machine\".into(), TermStats { df: 1 }),\n1230\t                (\"learning\".into(), TermStats { df: 1 }),\n1231\t            ]),\n1232\t        });\n1233\t\n1234\t        // Execute preflight to get global IDF\n1235\t        let preflight_req = PreflightRequest {\n1236\t            index_uid: \"test\".into(),\n1237\t            terms: vec![\"machine\".into(), \"learning\".into()],\n1238\t            filter: None,\n1239\t        };\n1240\t\n1241\t        let global_idf = execute_preflight(&plan, &c, &preflight_req, &topo).await.unwrap();\n1242\t\n1243\t        // Verify global aggregation\n1244\t        assert_eq!(global_idf.total_docs, 101_010);\n1245\t        assert_eq!(global_idf.terms.get(\"machine\").unwrap().df, 10_101);\n1246\t        assert_eq!(global_idf.terms.get(\"learning\").unwrap().df, 8_081);\n1247\t\n1248\t        // Verify global IDF is the same for all shards\n1249\t        // Expected IDF for \"machine\": log((101010 - 10101 + 0.5) / (10101 + 0.5)) + 1\n1250\t        let expected_idf_machine: f64 = ((101010.0_f64 - 10101.0 + 0.5) / (10101.0 + 0.5)).ln() + 1.0;\n1251\t        let actual_idf_machine = global_idf.terms.get(\"machine\").unwrap().idf;\n1252\t        assert!((actual_idf_machine - expected_idf_machine).abs() < 0.001);\n1253\t\n1254\t        // Expected IDF for \"learning\": log((101010 - 8081 + 0.5) / (8081 + 0.5)) + 1\n1255\t        let expected_idf_learning: f64 = ((101010.0_f64 - 8081.0 + 0.5) / (8081.0 + 0.5)).ln() + 1.0;\n1256\t        let actual_idf_learning = global_idf.terms.get(\"learning\").unwrap().idf;\n1257\t        assert!((actual_idf_learning - expected_idf_learning).abs() < 0.001);\n1258\t\n1259\t        // Verify that without global IDF, local IDF values would differ significantly\n1260\t        // Shard 0 local IDF for \"machine\": log((1000 - 100 + 0.5) / (100 + 0.5)) + 1 ≈ 3.19\n1261\t        // Shard 1 local IDF for \"machine\": log((100000 - 10000 + 0.5) / (10000 + 0.5)) + 1 ≈ 3.20\n1262\t        // Shard 2 local IDF for \"machine\": log((10 - 1 + 0.5) / (1 + Ok(0)\n206\t    }\n207\t\n208\t    /// Update the overall task status based on node task statuses.\n209\t    pub async fn update_overall_status(&self, miroir_id: &str) -> Result<bool> {\n210\t        let mut tasks = self.tasks.write().await;\n211\t        let task = match tasks.get(miroir_id) {\n212\t            Some(t) => t.clone(),\n213\t            None => return Ok(false),\n214\t        };\n215\t\n216\t        // Determine overall status from node tasks\n217\t        let mut all_succeeded = true;\n218\t        let mut any_failed = false;\n219\t        let mut all_terminal = true;\n220\t\n221\t        for (_node_id, node_task) in &task.node_tasks {\n222\t            match node_task.status {\n223\t                NodeTaskStatus::Enqueued | NodeTaskStatus::Processing => {\n224\t                    all_terminal = false;\n225\t                    all_succeeded = false;\n226\t                }\n227\t                NodeTaskStatus::Succeeded => {}\n228\t                NodeTaskStatus::Failed => {\n229\t                    any_failed = true;\n230\t                }\n231\t            }\n232\t        }\n233\t\n234\t        let new_status = if any_failed {\n235\t            TaskStatus::Failed\n236\t        } else if all_terminal && all_succeeded {\n237\t            TaskStatus::Succeeded\n238\t        } else if !all_terminal {\n239\t            TaskStatus::Processing\n240\t        } else {\n241\t            TaskStatus::Enqueued\n242\t        };\n243\t\n244\t        if new_status != task.status {\n245\t            if let Some(t) = tasks.get_mut(miroir_id) {\n246\t                t.status = new_status;\n247\t            }\n248\t            Ok(true)\n249\t        } else {\n250\t            Ok(false)\n251\t        }\n252\t    }\n253\t\n254\t    /// Poll node tasks to update the overall Miroir task status.\n255\t    /// Uses exponential backoff: 25ms → 50 → 100 → ... → 1s cap.\n256\t    ///\n257\t    /// Phase 2: Simulates node polling (tasks complete after ~500ms)\n258\t    /// Phase 3: Will poll actual nodes via HttpClient using topology\n259\t    async fn poll_task_status_simulated(&self, miroir_id: &str) {\n260\t        let mut delay_ms = 25u64;\n261\t        let max_delay_ms = 1000u64;\n262\t\n263\t        loop {\n264\t            // Get the current task state\n265\t            let task = self.get_async(miroir_id).await;\n266\t\n267\t            let task = match task {\n268\t                Some(t) => t,\n269\t                None => return, // Task was deleted\n270\t            };\n271\t\n272\t            // Check if we've reached a terminal state\n273\t            if matches!(task.status, TaskStatus::Succeeded | TaskStatus::Failed | TaskStatus::Canceled) {\n274\t                return;\n275\t            }\n276\t\n277\t            // In a real implementation, we would query the nodes here\n278\t            // For Phase 2, we simulate status progression\n279\t            // Phase 3 will add actual node polling via HttpClient\n280\t\n281\t            // Check each node task's status\n282\t            let mut all_terminal = true;\n283\t            for (_node_id, node_task) in &task.node_tasks {\n284\t                match node_task.status {\n285\t                    NodeTaskStatus::Enqueued | NodeTaskStatus::Processing => {\n286\t                        all_terminal = false;\n287\t                    }\n288\t                    NodeTaskStatus::Succeeded | NodeTaskStatus::Failed => {}\n289\t                }\n290\t            }\n291\t\n292\t            // For testing purposes, simulate tasks completing\n293\t            // In production, this would poll actual nodes\n294\t            if !all_terminal && delay_ms >= 500 {\n295\t                // Simulate completion for testing\n296\t                let mut tasks = self.tasks.write().await;\n297\t                if let Some(t) = tasks.get_mut(miroir_id) {\n298\t                    for (_node_id, node_task) in &mut t.node_tasks {\n299\t                        if matches!(node_task.status, NodeTaskStatus::Enqueued | NodeTaskStatus::Processing) {\n300\t                            node_task.status = NodeTaskStatus::Succeeded;\n301\t                        }\n302\t                    }\n303\t                    // Update overall status\n304\t                    let mut all_succeeded = true;\n305\t                    let mut any_failed = false;\n306\t                    for (_node_id, node_task) in &t.node_tasks {\n307\t                        match node_task.status {\n308\t                            NodeTaskStatus::Succeeded => {}\n309\t                            NodeTaskStatus::Failed => any_failed = true,\n310\t                            NodeTaskStatus::Enqueued | NodeTaskStatus::Processing => {\n311\t                                all_succeeded = false;\n312\t                            }\n313\t                        }\n314\t                    }\n315\t                    if any_failed {\n316\t                        t.status = TaskStatus::Failed;\n317\t                    } else if all_succeeded {\n318\t                        t.status = TaskStatus::Succeeded;\n319\t                    } else {\n320\t                        t.status = TaskStatus::Processing;\n321\t                    }\n322\t                    // Set finished timestamp for terminal states\n323\t                    if matches!(t.status, TaskStatus::Succeeded | TaskStatus::Failed | TaskStatus::Canceled) {\n324\t                        t.finished_at = Some(std::time::SystemTime::now()\n325\t                            .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)\n326\t                            .unwrap_or_default()\n327\t                            .as_millis() as u64);\n328\t                    }\n329\t                }\n330\t                return;\n331\t            }\n332\t\n333\t            // Exponential backoff with cap\n334\t            tokio::time::sleep(std::time::Duration::from_millis(delay_ms)).await;\n335\t            delay_ms = (delay_ms * 2).min(max_delay_ms);\n336\t        }\n337\t    }\n338\t\n339\t    /// Poll node tasks to update the overall Miroir task status, using real node polling.\n340\t    /// Uses exponential backoff: 25ms → 50 → 100 → ... → 1s cap.\n341\t    async fn poll_task_status_with_poller<P: NodePoller>(&self, miroir_id: &str, poller: Arc<P>) {\n342\t        let mut delay_ms = 25u64;\n343\t        let max_delay_ms = 1000u64;\n344\t\n345\t        loop {\n346\t            // Get the current task state\n347\t            let task = self.get_async(miroir_id).await;\n348\t\n349\t            let task = match task {\n350\t                Some(t) => t,\n351\t                None => return, // Task was deleted\n352\t            };\n353\t\n354\t            // Check if we've reached a terminal state\n355\t            if matches!(task.status, TaskStatus::Succeeded | TaskStatus::Failed | TaskStatus::Canceled) {\n356\t                return;\n357\t            }\n358\t\n359\t            // Collect node IDs and task UIDs to poll\n360\t            let node_polls: Vec<(NodeId, u64)> = task.node_tasks\n361\t                .iter()\n362\t                .filter(|(_, nt)| !matches!(nt.status, NodeTaskStatus::Succeeded | NodeTaskStatus::Failed))\n363\t                .map(|(node_id, nt)| (NodeId::new(node_id.clone()), nt.task_uid))\n364\t                .collect();\n365\t\n366\t            if node_polls.is_empty() {\n367\t                // All node tasks are terminal, update overall status\n368\t                let mut tasks = self.tasks.write().await;\n369\t                if let Some(t) = tasks.get_mut(miroir_id) {\n370\t                    let mut all_succeeded = true;\n371\t                    let mut any_failed = false;\n372\t                    for (_node_id, node_task) in &t.node_tasks {\n373\t                        match node_task.status {\n374\t                            NodeTaskStatus::Succeeded => {}\n375\t                            NodeTaskStatus::Failed => any_failed = true,\n376\t                            NodeTaskStatus::Enqueued | NodeTaskStatus::Processing => {\n377\t                                all_succeeded = false;\n378\t                            }\n379\t                        }\n380\t                    }\n381\t                    if any_failed {\n382\t                        t.status = TaskStatus::Failed;\n383\t                    } else if all_succeeded {\n384\t                        t.status = TaskStatus::Succeeded;\n385\t                    } else {\n386\t                        t.status = TaskStatus::Processing;\n387\t                    }\n388\t                    // Set finished timestamp for terminal states\n389\t                    if matches!(t.status, TaskStatus::Succeeded | TaskStatus::Failed | TaskStatus::Canceled) {\n390\t                        t.finished_at = Some(std::time::SystemTime::now()\n391\t                            .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)\n392\t                            .unwrap_or_default()\n393\t                            .as_millis() as u64);\n394\t                    }\n395\t                }\n396\t                return;\n397\t            }\n398\t\n399\t            // Poll each node for status\n400\t            let mut node_statuses = HashMap::new();\n401\t            for (node_id, task_uid) in &node_polls {\n402\t                // Get node address from topology (would need topology here)\n403\t                // For now, use a mock address - in production, this would come from the topology\n404\t                let address = format!(\"http://{}\", node_id.as_str());\n405\t\n406\t                match poller.poll_node_task(&node_id, &address, *task_uid).await {\n407\t                    Ok(status) => {\n408\t                        node_statuses.insert(node_id.clone(), status);\n409\t                    }\n410\t                    Err(e) => {\n411\t                        tracing::warn!(\"Failed to poll node {} for task {}: {}\", node_id, task_uid, e);\n412\t                        // On poll failure, keep the current status but mark for potential degradation\n413\t                    }\n414\t                }\n415\t            }\n416\t\n417\t            // Update node task statuses\n418\t            {\n419\t                let mut tasks = self.tasks.write().await;\n420\t                if let Some(t) = tasks.get_mut(miroir_id) {\n421\t                    for (node_id, status) in node_statuses {\n422\t                        if let Some(node_task) = t.node_tasks.get_mut(node_id.as_str()) {\n423\t                            node_task.status = status;\n424\t                        }\n425\t                    }\n426\t\n427\t                    // Update started_at timestamp if moving to processing\n428\t                    if t.status == TaskStatus::Enqueued {\n429\t                        let any_processing = t.node_tasks.values().any(|nt| {\n430\t                            matches!(nt.status, NodeTaskStatus::Processing)\n431\t                        });\n432\t                        if any_processing && t.started_at.is_none() {\n433\t                            t.started_at = Some(std::time::SystemTime::now()\n434\t                                .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)\n435\t                                .unwrap_or_default()\n436\t                                .as_millis() as u64);\n437\t                            t.status = TaskStatus::Processing;\n438\t                        }\n439\t                    }\n440\t                }\n441\t            }\n442\t\n443\t            // Exponential backoff with cap\n444\t            tokio::time::sleep(std::time::Duration::from_millis(delay_ms)).await;\n445\t            delay_ms = (delay_ms * 2).min(max_delay_ms);\n446\t        }\n447\t    }\n448\t\n449\t    /// List tasks with optional filtering (Meilisearch-compatible).\n450\t    pub async fn list_async(&self, filter: &TaskFilter) -> Result<Vec<MiroirTask>> {\n451\t        let guard = self.tasks.read().await;\n452\t        let mut result: Vec<MiroirTask> = guard.values().cloned().collect();\n453\t\n454\t        // Apply status filter\n455\t        if let Some(status) = filter.status {\n456\t            result.retain(|t| t.status == status);\n457\t        }\n458\t\n459\t        // Apply index_uid filter\n460\t        if let Some(index_uid) = &filter.index_uid {\n461\t            result.retain(|t| t.index_uid.as_ref().map_or(false, |uid| uid == index_uid));\n462\t        }\n463\t\n464\t        // Apply task_type filter\n465\t        if let Some(task_type) = &filter.task_type {\n466\t            result.retain(|t| t.task_type.as_ref().map_or(false, |ty| ty == task_type));\n467\t        }\n468\t\n469\t        // Apply offset\n470\t        if let Some(offset) = filter.offset {\n471\t            if offset < result.len() {\n472\t                result = result[offset..].to_vec();\n473\t            } else {\n474\t                result.clear();\n475\t            }\n476\t        }\n477\t\n478\t        // Apply limit\n479\t        if let Some(limit) = filter.limit {\n480\t            result.truncate(limit);\n481\t        }\n482\t\n483\t        Ok(result)\n484\t    }\n485\t}\n486\t\n487\timpl }\n540\t\n541\t    fn update_node_task(\n542\t        &self,\n543\t        miroir_id: &str,\n544\t        node_id: &str,\n545\t        node_status: NodeTaskStatus,\n546\t    ) -> Result<()> {\n547\t ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n591\t// TaskRegistryImpl — runtime-selected backend (Phase 3)\n592\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n593\t\n594\t/// Task status as lowercase string for store serialization.\n595\tfn status_to_str(s: TaskStatus) -> &'static str {\n596\t    match s {\n597\t        TaskStatus::Enqueued => \"enqueued\",\n598\t        TaskStatus::Processing => \"processing\",\n599\t        TaskStatus::Succeeded => \"succeeded\",\n600\t        TaskStatus::Failed => \"failed\",\n601\t        TaskStatus::Canceled => \"canceled\",\n602\t    }\n603\t}\n604\t\n605\t/// Parse task status from store string.\n606\tfn str_to_status(s: &str) -> TaskStatus {\n607\t    match s {\n608\t        \"succeeded\" => TaskStatus::Succeeded,\n609\t        \"failed\" => TaskStatus::Failed,\n610\t        \"processing\" => TaskStatus::Processing,\n611\t        \"canceled\" => TaskStatus::Canceled,\n612\t        _ => TaskStatus::Enqueued,\n613\t    }\n614\t}\n615\t\n616\t/// Convert a store `TaskRow` to a `MiroirTask`.\n617\t///\n618\t/// Per-node status is not persisted; all nodes are set to `Enqueued`.\n619\t/// The GET /tasks/{id} endpoint polls nodes to reconstruct per-node status.\n620\tfn row_to_task(row: crate::task_store::TaskRow) -> MiroirTask {\n621\t    let node_tasks: HashMap<String, NodeTask> = row\n622\t        .node_tasks\n623\t        .into_iter()\n624\t        .map(|(node_id, task_uid)| {\n625\t            (\n626\t                node_id,\n627\t                NodeTask {\n628\t                    task_uid,\n629\t                    status: NodeTaskStatus::Enqueued,\n630\t                },\n631\t            )\n632\t        })\n633\t        .collect();\n634\t\n635\t    MiroirTask {\n636\t        miroir_id: row.miroir_id,\n637\t        created_at: row.created_at as u64,\n638\t        started_at: row.started_at.map(|t| t as u64),\n639\t        finished_at: row.finished_at.map(|t| t as u64),\n640\t        status: str_to_status(&row.status),\n641\t        index_uid: row.index_uid,\n642\t        task_type: row.task_type,\n643\t        node_tasks,\n644\t        error: row.error,\n645\t        node_errors: row.node_errors,\n646\t    }\n647\t}\n648\t\n649\t/// Convert a route `TaskFilter` to a store `TaskFilter`.\n650\tfn filter_to_store(f: &TaskFilter) -> crate::task_store::TaskFilter {\n651\t    crate::task_store::TaskFilter {\n652\t        status: f.status.map(|s| status_to_str(s).to_string()),\n653\t        index_uid: f.index_uid.clone(),\n654\t        task_type: f.task_type.clone(),\n655\t        limit: f.limit,\n656\t        offset: f.offset,\n657\t    }\n658\t}\n659\t\n660\t/// Runtime-selected task registry backend.\n661\t///\n662\t/// - `InMemory`: Phase 2 behaviour, tasks lost on restart (default for sqlite without path).\n663\t/// - `Sqlite`: persisted via `SqliteTaskStore`, survives restarts.\n664\t/// - `Redis`: persisted via `RedisTaskStore`, required for multi-replica HPA.\n665\t#[derive(Clone)]\n666\tpub enum TaskRegistryImpl {\n667\t    InMemory(InMemoryTaskRegistry),\n668\t    Sqlite(Arc<crate::task_store::SqliteTaskStore>),\n669\t    #[cfg(feature = \"redis-store\")]\n670\t    Redis(Arc<crate::task_store::RedisTaskStore>),\n671\t}\n672\t\n673\timpl TaskRegistryImpl {\n674\t    /// Open a SQLite-backed registry at the given path.\n675\t    pub fn sqlite(path: &str) ->  }\n680\t\n681\t    /// Open a Redis-backed registry at the given URL.\n682\t    #[cfg(feature = \"redis-store\")]\n683\t    pub fn redis(url: &str) -> Result<Self> {\n684\t        let store = Self::InMemory(InMemoryTaskRegistry::new())\n695\t    }\n696\t\n697\t    /// Return a reference to the underlying `SqliteTaskStore`, if this is the Sqlite variant.\n698\t    pub fn as_sqlite(&self) -> }\n704\t\n705\t    /// Return a reference to the underlying `RedisTaskStore`, if this is the Redis variant.\n706\t    #[cfg(feature = \"redis-store\")]\n707\t    pub fn as_redis(&self) ->    }\n722\t    }\n723\t\n724\t    /// Register a new task with metadata (sync, compatible with `TaskRegistry` trait).\n725\t    pub fn register_with_metadata(\n726\t        &self,\n727\t )\n736\t            }\n737\t            TaskRegistryImpl::Sqlite(store) => {\n738\t                let miroir_id = format!(\"mtask-{}\", Uuid::new_v4());\n739\t                let created_at = created_at: new_task.created_at as u64,\n762\t                    started_at: None,\n763\t                    finished_at: None,\n764\t                    status: TaskStatus::Enqueued,\n765\t                    index_uid: new_task.index_uid,\n766\t                    task_type: new_task.task_type,\n767\t                    node_tasks: new_task\n768\t                        .node_tasks\n769\t                        .into_iter()\n770\t                        .map(|(nid, uid)| (nid, NodeTask { task_uid: uid, status: NodeTaskStatus::Enqueued }))\n771\t                        .collect(),\n772\t                    error: None,\n773\t                    node_errors: HashMap::new(),\n774\t                })\n775\t            }\n776\t            #[cfg(feature = \"redis-store\")]\n777\t            TaskRegistryImpl::Redis(store) => {\n778\t                let miroir_id = format!(\"mtask-{}\", Uuid::new_v4());\n779\t                let created_at = std::time::SystemTime::now()\n780\t                    .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)\n781\t                    .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Task(format!(\"clock error: {}\", e)))?\n782\t                    .as_millis() as i64;\n783\t\n784\t                let new_task = crate::task_store::NewTask {\n785\t                    miroir_id: miroir_id.clone(),\n786\t                    created_at,\n787\t                    status: \"enqueued\".to_string(),\n788\t                    node_tasks,\n789\t                    error: None,\n790\t                    started_at: None,\n791\t                    finished_at: None,\n792\t                    index_uid,\n793\t                    task_type,\n794\t                    node_errors: HashMap::new(),\n795\t                };\n796\t\n797\t                store.insert_task(&new_task)?;\n798\t\n799\t                Ok(MiroirTask {\n800\t                    miroir_id: new_task.miroir_id,\n801\t                    created_at: new_task.created_at as u64,\n802\t                    started_at: None,\n803\t                    finished_at: None,\n804\t                    status: TaskStatus::Enqueued,\n805\t                    index_uid: new_task.index_uid,\n806\t                    task_type: new_task.task_type,\n807\t                    node_tasks: new_task\n808\t                        .node_tasks\n809\t                        .into_iter()\n810\t                        .map(|(nid, uid)| (nid, NodeTask { task_uid: uid, status: NodeTaskStatus::Enqueued }))\n811\t                        .collect(),\n812\t                    error: None,\n813\t                    node_errors: HashMap::new(),\n814\t                })\n815\t            }\n816\t        }\n817\t    }\n818\t\n819\t    /// Get a task by Miroir ID.\n820\t    pub fn get(&self, miroir_id: &str) -> Result<Option<MiroirTask>> {\n821\t        match self {\n822\t            TaskRegistryImpl::InMemory(r) => crate::task::TaskRegistry::get(r, miroir_id),\n823\t            TaskRegistryImpl::Sqlite(store) => {\n824\t                Ok(store.get_task(miroir_id)?.map(row_to_task))\n825\t            }\n826\t            #[cfg(feature = \"redis-store\")]\n827\t            TaskRegistryImpl::Redis(store) => {\n828\t                Ok(store.get_task(miroir_id)?.map(row_to_task))\n829\t            }\n830\t        }\n831\t    }\n832\t\n833\t    /// Update the overall task status.\n834\t    pub fn update_status(&self, miroir_id: &str, status: TaskStatus) -> Result<()> {\n835\t        match self {\n836\t            TaskRegistryImpl::InMemory(r) => {\n837\t                crate::task::TaskRegistry::update_status(r, miroir_id, status)\n838\t            }\n839\t            TaskRegistryImpl::Sqlite(store) => {\n840\t                store.update_task_status(miroir_id, ///\n853\t    /// For persistent backends this is a no-op for per-node status since it is\n854\t    /// reconstructed from polling on each GET /tasks/{id}. The store only persists\n855\t    /// the overall task status and node task UIDs.\n856\t    pub fn update_node_task(\n857\t        &self,\n858\t        miroir_id: &str,\n859\t        node_id: &str,\n860\t        node_status: NodeTaskStatus,\n861\t    ) -> Result<()> {\n862\t        match self {\n863\t            TaskRegistryImpl::InMemory(r) => {\n864\t                crate::task::TaskRegistry::update_node_task(r, miroir_id, node_id, node_status)\n865\t            }\n866\t            // Per-node status is reconstructed from polling on each GET; not persisted.\n867\t            #[cfg(feature = \"redis-store\")]\n868\t            TaskRegistryImpl::Redis(_) => Ok(()),\n869\t            TaskRegistryImpl::Sqlite(_) => Ok(()),\n870\t        }\n871\t    }\n872\t\n873\t    /// List tasks with optional filtering.\n874\t    pub fn list(&self, filter: TaskFilter) -> Result<Vec<MiroirTask>> {\n875\t        match self {\n876\t #[cfg(feature = \"redis-store\")]\n883\t            TaskRegistryImpl::Redis(store) => {\n884\t                let store_filter = filter_to_store(&filter);\n885\t                let rows = `Request.extensions().get::<RequestId>()`.\n28\t#[derive(Clone, Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]\n29\tpub struct RequestId(pub String);\n30\t\n31\timpl RequestId {\n32\t    /// Create a new RequestId from a UUIDv7.\n33\t    ///\n34\t    /// Hashes the full UUIDv7 to produce an 8-character hex ID that is unique\n35\t    /// even for consecutive calls within the same millisecond.\n36\t    pub fn new() -> Self {\n37\t        let uuid = Uuid::now_v7();\n38\t        let bytes = uuid.as_bytes();\n39\t        // Hash the full UUID to ensure uniqueness even within the same millisecond\n40\t        let mut hasher = DefaultHasher::new();\n41\t        hasher.write(bytes);\n42\t        let hash = hasher.finish();\n43\t        // Take first 8 hex chars of 64-bit hash (32 bits is sufficient entropy)\n44\t        Self(format!(\"{:08x}\", hash as u32))\n45\t    }\n46\t\n47\t    /// Get the inner request ID string.\n48\t    pub fn as_str(&self) -> &str {\n49\t        &self.0\n50\t    }\n51\t\n52\t    ///   } else {\n57\t            None\n58\t        }\n59\t    }\n60\t}\n61\t\n62\t/// Session ID wrapper type for read-your-writes session pinning (plan §13.6).\n63\t///\n64\t/// Extracted from the {\n88\t            None\n89\t        }\n90\t    }\n91\t}\n92\t\n93\tpub async fn request_id_middleware(\n94\t    mut req: Request,\n95\t    next: Next,\n96\t) -> Response {\n97\t    // Check for response.headers_mut().insert(\"x-request-id\", val);\n119\t    }\n120\t\n121\t    response\n122\t}\n123\t\n124\t/// Session pinning middleware (plan §13.6).\n125\t///\n126\t/// Extracts the `X-Miroir-Session` header and stores it in request extensions\n127\t/// for handlers to access via `Request.extensions().get::<SessionId>()`.\n128\tpub async fn session_pinning_middleware(\n129\t    mut req: Request,\n130\t    next: Next,\n131\t) -> Response {\n132\t    // Extract session ID from header if present\n133\t    let session_id = req\n134\t        .headers()\n135\t        .get(\"x-miroir-session\")\n136\t        .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())\n137\t        .and_then(|s| SessionId::parse(s.to_string()));\n138\t\n139\t    // Store in request extensions for handler access\n140\t    if let Some(sid) = session_id {\n141\t        req.extensions_mut().insert(sid);\n142\t    }\n143\t\n144\t    next.run(req).await\n145\t}\n146\t\n147\t\n148\t/// Telemetry state Gauge,\n185\t\n186\t    // ── Scatter-gather metrics ──\n187\t    scatter_fan_out_size: Histogram,\n188\t    scatter_partial_responses: Counter,\n189\t    scatter_retries: Counter,\n190\t\n191\t    // ── Rebalancer metrics ──\n192\t    rebalance_in_progress: Gauge,\n193\t    rebalance_documents_migrated: Counter,\n194\t    rebalance_duration: Histogram,\n195\t\n196\t    // ── §13.11 Multi-search metrics (feature-gated) ──\n197\t    multisearch_queries_per_batch: Option<Histogram>,\n198\t    multisearch_batches_total: Option<Counter>,\n199\t    multisearch_partial_failures_total: Option<Counter>,\n200\t    multisearch_tenant_session_pin_override_total: Option<CounterVec>,\n201\t\n202\t    // ── §13.12 Vector search metrics (feature-gated) ──\n203\t    vector_search_over_fetched_total: Option<Counter>,\n204\t    vector_merge_strategy: Option<CounterVec>,\n205\t    vector_embedder_drift_total: Option<Counter>,\n206\t\n207\t    // ── §13.13 CDC metrics (feature-gated) ──\n208\t    cdc_events_published_total: Option<CounterVec>,\n209\t    cdc_lag_seconds: Option<GaugeVec>,\n210\t    cdc_buffer_bytes: Option<GaugeVec>,\n211\t    cdc_dropped_total: Option<CounterVec>,\n212\t    cdc_events_suppressed_total: Option<CounterVec>,\n213\t\n214\t    // ── §13.14 TTL metrics (feature-gated) ──\n215\t    ttl_documents_expired_total: Option<CounterVec>,\n216\t    ttl_sweep_duration_seconds: Option<HistogramVec>,\n217\t    ttl_pending_estimate: Option<GaugeVec>,\n218\t\n219\t    // ── §13.15 Tenant affinity metrics (feature-gated) ──\n220\t    tenant_queries_total: Option<CounterVec>,\n221\t    tenant_pinned_groups: Option<GaugeVec>,\n222\t    tenant_fallback_total: Option<CounterVec>,\n223\t\n224\t    // ── §13.16 Shadow traffic metrics (feature-gated) ──\n225\t    shadow_diff_total: Option<CounterVec>,\n226\t    shadow_kendall_tau: Option<Gauge>,\n227\t    shadow_latency_delta_seconds: Option<Histogram>,\n228\t    shadow_errors_total: Option<CounterVec>,\n229\t\n230\t    // ── §13.17 ILM metrics (feature-gated) ──\n231\t    rollover_events_total: Option<CounterVec>,\n232\t    rollover_active_indexes: Option<GaugeVec>,\n233\t    rollover_documents_expired_total: Option<CounterVec>,\n234\t    rollover_last_action_seconds: Option<GaugeVec>,\n235\t\n236\t    // ── §13.18 Canary metrics (feature-gated) ──\n237\t    canary_runs_total: Option<CounterVec>,\n238\t    canary_latency_ms: Option<HistogramVec>,\n239\t    canary_assertion_failures_total: Option<CounterVec>,\n240\t\n241\t    // ── §13.19 Admin UI metrics (feature-gated) ──\n242\t    admin_ui_sessions_total: Option<Counter>,\n243\t    admin_ui_action_total: Option<CounterVec>,\n244\t    admin_ui_destructive_action_total: Option<CounterVec>,\n245\t\n246\t    // ── §13.20 Explain metrics (feature-gated) ──\n247\t    explain_requests_total: Option<Counter>,\n248\t    explain_warnings_total: Option<CounterVec>,\n249\t    explain_execute_total: Option<Counter>,\n250\t\n251\t    // ── §13.21 Search UI metrics (feature-gated) ──\n252\t    search_ui_sessions_total: Option<Counter>,\n253\t    search_ui_queries_total: Option<CounterVec>,\n254\t    search_ui_zero_hits_total: Option<CounterVec>,\n255\t    search_ui_click_through_total: Option<CounterVec>,\n256\t    search_ui_p95_ms: Option<GaugeVec>,\n257\t\n258\t    // ── §14.9 Resource-pressure metrics (always present) ──\n259\t    memory_pressure: Gauge,\n260\t    cpu_throttled_seconds_total: Counter,\n261\t    request_queue_depth: Gauge,\n262\t    background_queue_depth: GaugeVec,\n263\t    peer_pod_count: Gauge,\n264\t    leader: GaugeVec,\n265\t    owned_shards_count: Gauge,\n266\t\n267\t    // ── Admin session sealing metrics (always present) ──\n268\t    admin_session_key_generated: Gauge,\n269\t    admin_session_revoked_total: Counter,\n270\t\n271\t    // ── §13.5 Two-phase settings broadcast metrics (always present) ──\n272\t    settings_broadcast_phase: GaugeVec,\n273\t    settings_hash_mismatch_total: Counter,\n274\t    settings_drift_repair_total: CounterVec,\n275\t    settings_version: GaugeVec,\n276\t    settings_divergence_alert_total: Counter,\n277\t    frozen_indexes: GaugeVec,\n278\t\n279\t    // ── §13.7 Alias metrics (always present) ──\n280\t    alias_resolutions_total: CounterVec,\n281\t    alias_flips_total: CounterVec,\n282\t\n283\t    // ── §13.6 Session pinning metrics (always present) ──\n284\t    session_active_count: Gauge,\n285\t    session_pin_enforced_total: CounterVec,\n286\t    session_wait_duration_seconds: Histogram,\n287\t    session_wait_timeout_total: CounterVec,\n288\t\n289\t    // ── §13.8 Anti-entropy metrics (always present) ──\n290\t    antientropy_shards_scanned_total: Counter,\n291\t    antientropy_mismatches_found_total: Counter,\n292\t    antientropy_docs_repaired_total: Counter,\n293\t    antientropy_last_scan_completed_seconds: Gauge,\n294\t\n295\t    // ── §13.3 Adaptive replica selection metrics (always present) ──\n296\t    replica_selection_score: GaugeVec,\n297\t    replica_selection_exploration_total: Counter,\n298\t\n299\t    // ── §13.10 Idempotency metrics (always present) ──\n300\t    idempotency_hits_total: CounterVec,\n301\t    idempotency_cache_size: Gauge,\n302\t\n303\t    // ── §13.10 Query coalescing metrics (always present) ──\n304\t    query_coalesce_subscribers_total: Counter,\n305\t    query_coalesce_hits_total: Counter,\n306\t}\n307\t\n308\timpl Clone for Metrics {\n309\t Self::new(&MiroirConfig::default())\n408\t    }\n409\t}\n410\t\n411\timpl Metrics {\n412\t    pub fn new(config: &MiroirConfig) -> Self {\n413\t        let registry = Registry::new();\n414\t\n415\t        // ── Request metrics ──\n416\t        let request_duration = HistogramVec::new(\n417\t            HistogramOpts::new(\"miroir_request_duration_seconds\", \"Request latency in seconds\")\n418\t                .buckets(vec![0.005, 0.01, 0.025, 0.05, 0.1, 0.25, 0.5, 1.0, 2.5, 5.0, 10.0]),\n419\t            &[\"method\", \"path_template\", \"status\"],\n420\t        )\n421\t        .expect(\"failed to create request_duration histogram\");\n422\t\n423\t        let requests_total = CounterVec::new(\n424\t            Opts::new(\"miroir_requests_total\", \"Total number of requests\"),\n425\t            &[\"method\", \"path_template\", \"status\"],\n426\t        )\n427\t        .expect(\"failed to create requests_total counter\");\n428\t\n429\t        let requests_in_flight = Gauge::with_opts(\n430\t            Opts::new(\"miroir_requests_in_flight\", \"Number of requests currently being processed\"),\n431\t        )\n432\t        .expect(\"failed to create requests_in_flight gauge\");\n433\t\n434\t        // ── Node health metrics ──\n435\t        let node_healthy = GaugeVec::new(\n436\t            Opts::new(\"miroir_node_healthy\", \"Health status of backend nodes (1=healthy, 0=unhealthy)\"),\n437\t            &[\"node_id\"],\n438\t        )\n439\t        .expect(\"failed to create node_healthy gauge\");\n440\t\n441\t        let node_request_duration = HistogramVec::new(\n442\t            HistogramOpts::new(\"miroir_node_request_duration_seconds\", \"Latency of individual node requests\")\n443\t                .buckets(vec![0.001, 0.005, 0.01, 0.025, 0.05, 0.1, 0.5, 1.0]),\n444\t            &[\"node_id\", \"operation\"],\n445\t        )\n446\t        .expect(\"failed to create node_request_duration histogram\");\n447\t\n448\t        let node_errors = CounterVec::new(\n449\t            Opts::new(\"miroir_node_errors_total\", \"Number of errors from backend nodes\"),\n450\t            &[\"node_id\", \"error_type\"],\n451\t        )\n452\t        .expect(\"failed to create node_errors counter\");\n453\t\n454\t        // ── Shard metrics ──\n455\t        let shard_coverage = Gauge::with_opts(\n456\t            Opts::new(\"miroir_shard_coverage\", \"Fraction of shards with at least one healthy replica\"),\n457\t        )\n458\t        .expect(\"failed to create shard_coverage gauge\");\n459\t\n460\t        let degraded_shards = Gauge::with_opts(\n461\t            Opts::new(\"miroir_degraded_shards_total\", \"Number of shards with reduced replica availability\"),\n462\t        )\n463\t        .expect(\"failed to create degraded_shards gauge\");\n464\t\n465\t        let shard_distribution = GaugeVec::new(\n466\t            Opts::new(\"miroir_shard_distribution\", \"Number of shards assigned to each node\"),\n467\t            &[\"node_id\"],\n468\t        )\n469\t        .expect(\"failed to create shard_distribution gauge\");\n470\t\n471\t        // ── Task metrics ──\n472\t        let task_processing_age = Histogram::with_opts(\n473\t            HistogramOpts::new(\"miroir_task_processing_age_seconds\", \"Time between task creation and processing start\")\n474\t                .buckets(vec![0.01, 0.05, 0.1, 0.25, 0.5, 1.0, 2.5, 5.0, 10.0, 30.0, 60.0]),\n475\t        )\n476\t        .expect(\"failed to create task_processing_age histogram\");\n477\t\n478\t        let tasks_total = CounterVec::new(\n479\t            Opts::new(\"miroir_tasks_total\", \"Total number of tasks by status\"),\n480\t            &[\"status\"],\n481\t        )\n482\t        .expect(\"failed to create tasks_total counter\");\n483\t\n484\t        let task_registry_size = Gauge::with_opts(\n485\t            Opts::new(\"miroir_task_registry_size\", \"Current number of tasks in the registry\"),\n486\t        )\n487\t        .expect(\"failed to create task_registry_size gauge\");\n488\t\n489\t        // ── Scatter-gather metrics ──\n490\t        let scatter_fan_out_size = Histogram::with_opts(\n491\t            HistogramOpts::new(\"miroir_scatter_fan_out_size\", \"Number of nodes in scatter operations\")\n492\t                .buckets(vec![1.0, 2.0, 3.0, 5.0, 10.0, 20.0, 50.0]),\n493\t        )\n494\t        .expect(\"failed to create scatter_fan_out_size histogram\");\n495\t\n496\t        let scatter_partial_responses = Counter::with_opts(\n497\t            Opts::new(\"miroir_scatter_partial_responses_total\", \"Number of scatter responses that were partial (some nodes failed)\"),\n498\t        )\n499\t        .expect(\"failed to create scatter_partial_responses counter\");\n500\t\n501\t        let scatter_retries = Counter::with_opts(\n502\t            Opts::new(\"miroir_scatter_retries_total\", \"Number of scatter retry attempts due to node failures\"),\n503\t        )\n504\t        .expect(\"failed to create scatter_retries counter\");\n505\t\n506\t        // ── Rebalancer metrics ──\n507\t        let rebalance_in_progress = Gauge::with_opts(\n508\t            Opts::new(\"miroir_rebalance_in_progress\", \"Whether a rebalance is currently running (1=yes, 0=no)\"),\n509\t        )\n510\t        .expect(\"failed to create rebalance_in_progress gauge\");\n511\t\n512\t        let rebalance_documents_migrated = Counter::with_opts(\n513\t            Opts::new(\"miroir_rebalance_documents_migrated_total\", \"Total number of documents migrated during rebalance\"),\n514\t        )\n515\t        .expect(\"failed to create rebalance_documents_migrated counter\");\n516\t\n517\t        let rebalance_duration = Histogram::with_opts(\n518\t            HistogramOpts::new(\"miroir_rebalance_duration_seconds\", \"Duration of rebalance operations\")\n519\t                .buckets(vec![1.0, 5.0, 10.0, 30.0, 60.0, 300.0, 600.0, 1800.0, 3600.0]),\n520\t        )\n521\t        .expect(\"failed to create rebalance_duration histogram\");\n522\t\n523\t        // Register all metrics\n524\t        macro_rules! reg {\n525\t            ($m:expr) => {\n526\t                registry.register(Box::new($m.clone())).expect(concat!(\"failed to register \", stringify!($m)));\n527\t            };\n528\t        }\n529\t\n530\t        reg!(request_duration);\n531\t        reg!(requests_total);\n532\t        reg!(requests_in_flight);\n533\t        reg!(node_healthy);\n534\t        reg!(node_request_duration);\n535\t        reg!(node_errors);\n536\t        reg!(shard_coverage);\n537\t        reg!(degraded_shards);\n538\t        reg!(shard_distribution);\n539\t        reg!(task_processing_age);\n540\t        reg!(tasks_total);\n541\t        reg!(task_registry_size);\n542\t        reg!(scatter_fan_out_size);\n543\t        reg!(scatter_partial_responses);\n544\t        reg!(scatter_retries);\n545\t        reg!(rebalance_in_progress);\n546\t        reg!(rebalance_documents_migrated);\n547\t        reg!(rebalance_duration);\n548\t\n549\t        // ── §13.11 Multi-search metrics (cardinality cap: top 100 tenants, rest bucketed) ──\n550\t        let (\n551\t            multisearch_queries_per_batch,\n552\t            multisearch_batches_total,\n553\t            multisearch_partial_failures_total,\n554\t            multisearch_tenant_session_pin_override_total,\n555\t        ) = if config.multi_search.enabled {\n556\t            let q = Histogram::with_opts(\n557\t                HistogramOpts::new(\"miroir_multisearch_queries_per_batch\", \"Number of queries in each multi-search batch\")\n558\t                    .buckets(vec![1.0, 2.0, 5.0, 10.0, 25.0, 50.0, 100.0]),\n559\t            ).expect(\"create multisearch_queries_per_batch\");\n560\t            let b = Counter::with_opts(\n561\t                Opts::new(\"miroir_multisearch_batches_total\", \"Total number of multi-search batches processed\"),\n562\t            ).expect(\"create multisearch_batches_total\");\n563\t            let p = Counter::with_opts(\n564\t                Opts::new(\"miroir_multisearch_partial_failures_total\", \"Number of multi-search batches with at least one query failure\"),\n565\t            ).expect(\"create multisearch_partial_failures_total\");\n566\t            let t = CounterVec::new(\n567\t                Opts::new(\"miroir_tenant_session_pin_override_total\", \"Session pin overrides triggered by multi-search tenant routing\"),\n568\t                &[\"tenant\"],\n569\t            ).expect(\"create multisearch_tenant_session_pin_override_total\");\n570\t            reg!(q); reg!(b); reg!(p); reg!(t);\n571\t            (Some(q), Some(b), Some(p), Some(t))\n572\t        } else {\n573\t            (None, None, None, None)\n574\t        };\n575\t\n576\t        // ── §13.12 Vector search metrics ──\n577\t        let (\n578\t            vector_search_over_fetched_total,\n579\t            vector_merge_strategy,\n580\t            vector_embedder_drift_total,\n581\t        ) = if config.vector_search.enabled {\n582\t            let o = Counter::with_opts(\n583\t                Opts::new(\"miroir_vector_search_over_fetched_total\", \"Number of vector searches that over-fetched candidates\"),\n584\t            ).expect(\"create vector_search_over_fetched_total\");\n585\t            let m = CounterVec::new(\n586\t                Opts::new(\"miroir_vector_merge_strategy\", \"Count of hybrid merge strategy selections\"),\n587\t                &[\"strategy\"],\n588\t            ).expect(\"create vector_merge_strategy\");\n589\t            let d = Counter::with_opts(\n590\t                Opts::new(\"miroir_vector_embedder_drift_total\", \"Number of embedder drift detections\"),\n591\t            ).expect(\"create vector_embedder_drift_total\");\n592\t            reg!(o); reg!(m); reg!(d);\n593\t            (Some(o), Some(m), Some(d))\n594\t        } else {\n595\t            (None, None, None)\n596\t query_coalesce_subscribers_total,\n1092\t            query_coalesce_hits_total,\n1093\t        }\n1094\t    }\n1095\t\n1096\t    pub fn encode_metrics(&self) -> Result<String, prometheus::Error> {\n1097\t        let encoder = TextEncoder::new();\n1098\t        let metric_families = self.registry.gather();\n1099\t        let mut buffer = Vec::new();\n1100\t        encoder.encode(&metric_families, &mut buffer)?;\n1101\t        Ok(String::from_utf8(buffer).map_err(|e| {\n1102\t            prometheus::Error::Msg(format!(\"failed to convert metrics to UTF-8: {}\", e))\n1103\t        })?)\n1104\t    }\n1105\t\n1106\t    pub fn admin_session_key_generated(&self) -> Gauge {\n1107\t        self.admin_session_key_generated.clone()\n1108\t    }\n1109\t\n1110\t    pub fn admin_session_revoked_total(&self) -> Counter {\n1111\t        self.admin_session_revoked_total.clone()\n1112\t    }\n1113\t}\n1114\t\n1115\t/// Generate a short request ID from UUIDv7.\n1116\t///\n1117\t/// UUIDv7 provides time-ordered unique IDs. We take the first 8 hex characters,\n1118\t/// hash them, and encode as hex for a short, URL-safe identifier.\n1119\tpub fn generate_request_id() -> String {\n1120\t    let uuid = Uuid::now_v7();\n1121\t    let uuid_str = uuid.simple().to_string();\n1122\t    // Take first 8 chars (enough entropy for uniqueness)\n1123\t    let prefix = &uuid_str[..8];\n1124\t\n1125\t    // Hash to get a consistent short representation\n1126\t    let mut hasher = DefaultHasher::new();\n1127\t    prefix.hash(&mut hasher);\n1128\t    let hash = hasher.finish();\n1129\t\n1130\t    // Encode as hex (16 chars = 64 bits)\n1131\t    format!(\"{:016x}\", hash)\n1132\t}\n1133\t\n1134\t/// Extension trait to add request ID extraction utilities.\n1135\tpub trait RequestIdExt {\n1136\t    fn get_request_id(&self) -> Option<String>;\n1137\t    fn set_request_id(&mut self, id: &str);\n1138\t}\n1139\t\n1140\timpl RequestIdExt for HeaderMap {\n1141\t    fn get_request_id(&self) -> Option<String> {\n1142\t        self.get(\"x-request-id\")\n1143\t            .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())\n1144\t            .map(|s| s.to_string())\n1145\t    }\n1146\t\n1147\t    fn set_request_id(&mut self, id: &str) {\n1148\t        if let Ok(val) = HeaderValue::from_str(id) {\n1149\t            self.insert(\"x-request-id\", val);\n1150\t        }\n1151\t    }\n1152\t}\n1153\t\n1154\t/// Guard that decrements the in-flight gauge when dropped.\n1155\t///\n1156\t/// This ensures that even if the handler panics, the in-flight count\n1157\t/// is accurately decremented.\n1158\tstruct InFlightGuard {\n1159\t    metrics: Metrics,\n1160\t    request_id: String,\n1161\t}\n1162\t\n1163\timpl InFlightGuard {\n1164\t    fn new(metrics: Metrics, request_id: String) -> Self {\n1165\t        metrics.requests_in_flight.inc();\n1166\t        tracing::trace!(\n1167\t            request_id = %request_id,\n1168\t            requests_in_flight = metrics.requests_in_flight.get(),\n1169\t            \"request started\"\n1170\t        );\n1171\t        Self { metrics, request_id }\n1172\t    }\n1173\t}\n1174\t\n1175\timpl Drop for InFlightGuard {\n1176\t    fn drop(&mut self) {\n1177\t        self.metrics.requests_in_flight.dec();\n1178\t        tracing::trace!(\n1179\t            request_id = %self.request_id,\n1180\t            requests_in_flight = self.metrics.requests_in_flight.get(),\n1181\t            \"request completed\"\n1182\t        );\n1183\t    }\n1184\t}\n1185\t\n1186\t/// Extract the path template from the matched route.\n1187\t///\n1188\t/// Axum's MatchedPath extractor provides the route template (e.g., \"/indexes/{uid}/search\")\n1189\t/// instead of the actual path (e.g., \"/indexes/products/search\"), avoiding high-cardinality labels.\n1190\tfn extract_path_template(request: &Request) -> String {\n1191\t    request\n1192\t        .extensions()\n1193\t        .get::<axum::extract::MatchedPath>()\n1194\t        .map(|mp| mp.as_str())\n1195\t        .unwrap_or_else(|| request.uri().path())\n1196\t        .to_string()\n1197\t}\n1198\t\n1199\t/// Main middleware that combines request ID injection, structured logging, and Prometheus metrics.\n1200\t///\n1201\t/// IMPORTANT: This middleware must be applied AFTER request_id_middleware in the layer stack\n1202\t/// (i.e., its layer() call must come BEFORE request_id_middleware's layer() call).\n1203\t/// This ensures the request_id header is already set when this middleware runs.\n1204\tpub async fn telemetry_middleware(\n1205\t    State(telemetry): State<TelemetryState>,\n1206\t    mut req: Request,\n1207\t    next: Next,\n1208\t) -> Response {\n1209\t    let start = Instant::now();\n1210\t    let method = req.method().clone();\n1211\t    let path_template = extract_path_template(&req);\n1212\t    let metrics = telemetry.metrics.clone();\n1213\t    let pod_id = telemetry.pod_id.clone();\n1214\t\n1215\t    // Extract request ID from header (set by request_id_middleware)\n1216\t    // The header must already exist because request_id_middleware runs first.\n1217\t    let request_id = req\n1218\t        .headers()\n1219\t        .get_request_id()\n1220\t        .expect(\"request_id header must be set by request_id_middleware\");\n1221\t    req.headers_mut().set_request_id(&request_id);\n1222\t\n1223\t    // Create span for structured logging with pod_id included.\n1224\t    // Note: raw path is intentionally omitted to avoid logging index names\n1225\t    // (which may contain customer identifiers). Use path_template instead.\n1226\t    let span = info_span!(\n1227\t        \"request\",\n1228\t        request_id = %request_id,\n1229\t        pod_id = %pod_id,\n1230\t        method = %method,\n1231\t        path_template = %path_template,\n1232\t    );\n1233\t\n1234\t    let _guard = span.enter();\n1235\t\n1236\t    // Track in-flight requests\n1237\t    let in_flight = InFlightGuard::new(metrics.clone(), request_id.clone());\n1238\t\n1239\t    let response = next.run(req).await;\n1240\t\n1241\t    drop(in_flight);\n1242\t\n1243\t    let status = response.status();\n1244\t    let status_u16 = status.as_u16();\n1245\t    let duration = start.elapsed();\n1246\t\n1247\t    // Record Prometheus metrics\n1248\t    metrics\n1249\t        .request_duration\n1250\t        .with_label_values(&[method.as_str(), &path_template, &status_u16.to_string()])\n1251\t        .observe(duration.as_secs_f64());\n1252\t    metrics\n1253\t        .requests_total\n1254\t        .with_label_values(&[method.as_str(), &path_template, &status_u16.to_string()])\n1255\t        .inc();\n1256\t\n1257\t    // Structured log entry (plan §10 shape)\n1258\t    // Base fields: timestamp (from tracing-subscriber), level, message, duration_ms\n1259\t    // Additional fields (index, node_count, estimated_hits, degraded)\n1260\t    // are added by request handlers via the tracing span.\n1261\t    let message = format!(\"{} {}\", method, status);\n1262\t    if status.is_server_error() {\n1263\t        tracing::error!(\n1264\t            target: \"miroir.request\",\n1265\t            pod_id = %pod_id,\n1266\t            request_id = %request_id,\n1267\t            message = %message,\n1268\t            duration_ms = duration.as_millis() as u64,\n1269\t            status = status_u16,\n1270\t            method = %method,\n1271\t            path_template = %path_template,\n1272\t        );\n1273\t    } else if status.is_client_error() {\n1274\t        tracing::warn!(\n1275\t            target: \"miroir.request\",\n1276\t            pod_id = %pod_id,\n1277\t            request_id = %request_id,\n1278\t            message = %message,\n1279\t            duration_ms = duration.as_millis() as u64,\n1280\t            status = status_u16,\n1281\t            method = %method,\n1282\t            path_template = %path_template,\n1283\t        );\n1284\t    } else {\n1285\t        tracing::info!(\n1286\t            target: \"miroir.request\",\n1287\t            pod_id = %pod_id,\n1288\t            request_id = %request_id,\n1289\t            message = %message,\n1290\t            duration_ms = duration.as_millis() as u64,\n1291\t            status = status_u16,\n1292\t            method = %method,\n1293\t            path_template = %path_template,\n1294\t        );\n1295\t    }\n1296\t\n1297\t    // Ensure request ID is in response headers\n1298\t    let mut response = response;\n1299\t    if !response.headers().contains_key(\"x-request-id\") {\n1300\t        if let Ok(val) = HeaderValue::from_str(&request_id) {\n1301\t            response.headers_mut().insert(\"x-request-id\", val);\n1302\t        }\n1303\t    }\n1304\t\n1305\t    response\n1306\t}\n1307\t\n1308\t/// Create the metrics router for the :9090 server.\n1309\tpub fn metrics_router() -> Router<Metrics> {\n1310\t    Router::new().route(\"/metrics\", get(metrics_handler))\n1311\t}\n1312\t\n1313\t/// Handler that returns Prometheus metrics in text exposition format.\n1314\tasync fn metrics_handler(State(metrics): State<Metrics>) -> Response {\n1315\t    let body = match metrics.encode_metrics() {\n1316\t        Ok(metrics) => metrics,\n1317\t        Err(e) => {\n1318\t            tracing::error!(error = %e, \"failed to encode metrics\");\n1319\t            format!(\"# ERROR: failed to encode metrics: {}\\n\", e)\n1320\t        }\n1321\t    };\n1322\t\n1323\t    let mut response = Response::new(axum::body::Body::from(body));\n1324\t    response.headers_mut().insert(\n1325\t        \"content-type\",\n1326\t        HeaderValue::from_static(\"text/plain; version=0.0.4; charset=utf-8\"),\n1327\t    );\n1328\t    response\n1329\t}\n1330\t\n1331\t/// Accessor methods for metrics that can be used by other parts of the application.\n1332\timpl Metrics {\n1333\t    // ── Idempotency keys and query coalescing (plan §13.10).\n2\t//!\n3\t//! This module implements:\n4\t//! - Write deduplication via idempotency keys\n5\t//! - Read query coalescing for identical concurrent searches\n6\t\n7\tuse crate::error::{MiroirError, Result};\n8\tuse
