RwLock};\n13\t\n14\t/// Idempotency cache entry.\n15\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n16\tpub struct IdempotencyEntry {\n17\t    /// SHA256 hash of the request body.\n18\t    pub body_hash: String,\n19\t    /// Miroir task ID returned to the client.\n20\t    pub mtask_id: String,\n21\t    /// Expiration timestamp.\n22\t    pub expires_at: u64,\n23\t}\n24\t\n25\t/// Idempotency cache for write deduplication.\n26\tpub struct IdempotencyCache {\n27\t    /// key -> (body_hash, mtask_id, expires_at)\n28\t    cache: Arc<RwLock<HashMap<String, IdempotencyEntry>>>,\n29\t    /// Maximum entries (LRU cap).\n30\t    max_entries: usize,\n31\t    /// TTL in seconds.\n32\t    ttl_secs: u64,\n33\t}\n34\t\n35\timpl IdempotencyCache {\n36\t    /// Create a new idempotency cache.\n37\t    pub fn new(max_entries: usize, ttl_secs: u64) -> Self {\n38\t        Self {\n39\t            cache: Arc::new(RwLock::new(HashMap::new())),\n40\t            max_entries,\n41\t            ttl_secs,\n42\t        }\n43\t    }\n44\t\n45\t    /// Check for a duplicate write.\n46\t    ///\n47\t    /// Returns `Some(mtask_id)` if the key exists and body hash matches.\n48\t    /// Returns `Err` if the key exists but body hash differs.\n49\t    /// Returns `None` if the key is not found (caller should process).\n50\t    pub async fn check(&self, key: &str, body_hash: &str) -> Result<Option<String>> {\n51\t        let mut cache = self.cache.write().await;\n52\t        self.prune_expired_locked(&mut cache);\n53\t\n54\t        if let Some(entry) = cache.get(key) {\n55\t            if entry.body_hash == body_hash {\n56\t                return Ok(Some(entry.mtask_id.clone()));\n57\t            } else {\n58\t                return Err(MiroirError::IdempotencyKeyReused);\n59\t            }\n60\t        }\n61\t        Ok(None)\n62\t    }\n63\t\n64\t    /// Insert a new idempotency entry.\n65\t    pub async fn insert(&self, key: String, body_hash: String, mtask_id: String) {\n66\t        let mut cache = self.cache.write().await;\n67\t        self.prune_expired_locked(&mut cache);\n68\t\n69\t        // Enforce max entries (simple FIFO for now)\n70\t        if cache.len() >= self.max_entries {\n71\t            // Remove oldest entry (first key)\n72\t            if let Some(k) = cache.keys().next().cloned() {\n73\t                cache.remove(&k);\n74\t            }\n75\t        }\n76\t\n77\t        let now = SystemTime::now()\n78\t            .duration_since(SystemTime::UNIX_EPOCH)\n79\t            .unwrap_or_default()\n80\t            .as_secs();\n81\t        let expires_at = now + self.ttl_secs;\n82\t\n83\t        cache.insert(key, IdempotencyEntry {\n84\t            body_hash,\n85\t            mtask_id,\n86\t            expires_at,\n87\t        });\n88\t    }\n89\t\n90\t    /// Prune expired entries (must hold write lock).\n91\t    fn prune_expired_locked(&self, cache: &mut HashMap<String, IdempotencyEntry>) {\n92\t        let now = SystemTime::now()\n93\t            .duration_since(SystemTime::UNIX_EPOCH)\n94\t            .unwrap_or_default()\n95\t            .as_secs();\n96\t        cache.retain(|_, v| v.expires_at > now);\n97\t    }\n98\t\n99\t    /// Get current cache size.\n100\t    pub async fn size(&self) -> usize {\n101\t        self.cache.read().await.len()\n102\t    }\n103\t}\n104\t\n105\t/// Fingerprint of a query for coalescing.\n106\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]\n107\tpub struct QueryFingerprint {\n108\t    /// Index UID.\n109\t    pub index: String,\n110\t    /// Canonical JSON of search query.\n111\t    pub query_json: String,\n112\t    /// Settings version (include to avoid coalescing across settings changes).\n113\t    pub settings_version: u64,\n114\t}\n115\t\n116\t/// Pending query state for coalescing.\n117\tpub struct PendingQuery {\n118\t    /// Response broadcast channel.\n119\t    tx: broadcast::Sender<Vec<u8>>,\n120\t    /// When this query was initiated.\n121\t    started_at: Instant,\n122\t}\n123\t\n124\t/// Query coalescing cache.\n125\tpub struct QueryCoalescer {\n126\t    /// Fingerprint -> pending query state.\n127\t    pending: Arc<RwLock<HashMap<QueryFingerprint, PendingQuery>>>,\n128\t    /// Coalescing window in milliseconds.\n129\t    window_ms: u64,\n130\t    /// Max concurrent pending queries.\n131\t    max_pending: usize,\n132\t    /// Max subscribers per query.\n133\t    max_subscribers: usize,\n134\t}\n135\t\n136\timpl QueryCoalescer {\n137\t    /// Create a new query coalescer.\n138\t    pub fn new(window_ms: u64, max_pending: usize, max_subscribers: usize) -> Self {\n139\t        Self {\n140\t            pending: Arc::new(RwLock::new(HashMap::new())),\n141\t            window_ms,\n142\t            max_pending,\n143\t            max_subscribers,\n144\t        }\n145\t    }\n146\t\n147\t    /// Check if a query can be coalesced.\n148\t    ///\n149\t    /// Returns `Some(receiver)` if a matching query is in flight.\n150\t    /// Returns `None` if no matching query (caller should dispatch).\n151\t    pub async fn try_coalesce(\n152\t        &self,\n153\t        fingerprint: QueryFingerprint,\n154\t    ) -> Option<broadcast::Receiver<Vec<u8>>> {\n155\t        let mut pending = self.pending.write().await;\n156\t        self.prune_stale_locked(&mut pending);\n157\t\n158\t        if let Some(pq) = pending.get(&fingerprint) {\n159\t            if pq.tx.receiver_count() < self.max_subscribers {\n160\t                return Some(pq.tx.subscribe());\n161\t            }\n162\t        }\n163\t        None\n164\t    }\n165\t\n166\t    /// Register a new pending query.\n167\t    ///\n168\t    /// Returns a broadcast sender that the caller should notify on completion.\n169\t    pub async fn register(\n170\t        &self,\n171\t        fingerprint: QueryFingerprint,\n172\t    ) -> Result<broadcast::Sender<Vec<u8>>> {\n173\t        let mut pending = self.pending.write().await;\n174\t        self.prune_stale_locked(&mut pending);\n175\t\n176\t        if pending.len() >= self.max_pending {\n177\t            return Err(MiroirError::TooManyPendingQueries);\n178\t        }\n179\t\n180\t        let (tx, _) = broadcast::channel(self.max_subscribers);\n181\t        pending.insert(fingerprint, PendingQuery {\n182\t            tx: tx.clone(),\n183\t            started_at: Instant::now(),\n184\t        });\n185\t        Ok(tx)\n186\t    }\n187\t\n188\t    /// Unregister a pending query after completion.\n189\t    pub  ttl_secs,\n42\t        }\n43\t    }\n44\t\n45\t    /// Check for a duplicate write.\n46\t    ///\n47\t    /// Returns `Some(mtask_id)` if the key exists and body hash matches.\n48\t    /// Returns `Err` if the key exists but body hash differs.\n49\t    /// Returns `None` if the key is not found (caller should process).\n50\t    pub async fn check(&self, key: &str, body_hash: &str) -> Result<Option<String>> {\n51\t        let mut cache = self.cache.write().await;\n52\t        self.prune_expired_locked(&mut cache);\n53\t\n54\t        if let Some(entry) = cache.get(key) {\n55\t            if entry.body_hash == body_hash {\n56\t                return Ok(Some(entry.mtask_id.clone()));\n57\t            } else {\n58\t                return Err(MiroirError::IdempotencyKeyReused);\n59\t >= self.max_entries {\n71\t            // Remove oldest entry (first key)\n72\t            if let Some(k) = cache.keys().next().cloned() {\n73\t                cache.remove(&k);\n74\t            }\n75\t        }\n76\t\n77\t        let now = SystemTime::now()\n78\t            .duration_since(SystemTime::UNIX_EPOCH)\n79\t            .unwrap_or_default()\n80\t            .as_secs();\n81\t        }\n163\t        None\n164\t    }\n165\t\n166\t    /// Register a new pending query.\n167\t    ///\n168\t    /// Returns a broadcast sender that the caller should notify on completion.\n169\t    pub async fn register(\n170\t        &self,\n171\t        fingerprint: QueryFingerprint,\n172\t    ) -> Result<broadcast::Sender<Vec<u8>>> {\n173\t        let mut pending = \"mtask-1\".into()).await;\n243\t        cache.insert(\"key2\".into(), \"hash2\".into(), \"mtask-2\".into()).await;\n244\t        cache.insert(\"key3\".into(), \"hash3\".into(),     cache.insert(\"key4\".into(), \"hash4\".into(), \"mtask-4\".into()).await;\n251\t        assert_eq!(cache.size().await, 3);\n252\t        // At least one of the original keys should be entry.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct IdempotencyEntry {\n    /// SHA256 hash of the request body.\n    pub body_hash: String,\n    /// Miroir task ID returned to the client.\n    pub mtask_id: String,\n    /// Expiration timestamp.\n    pub expires_at: u64,\n}\n\n/// Idempotency cache for write deduplication.\npub struct IdempotencyCache {\n    /// key -> (body_hash, mtask_id, expires_at)\n    cache: Arc<RwLock<HashMap<String, IdempotencyEntry>>>,\n    /// Maximum entries (LRU cap).\n    max_entries: usize,\n    /// TTL in seconds.\n    ttl_secs: u64,\n}\n\nimpl IdempotencyCache {\n    /// Create a new idempotency cache.\n    pub fn new(max_entries: usize, ttl_secs: u64) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            cache: Arc::new(RwLock::new(HashMap::new())),\n            max_entries,\n            ttl_secs,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Check for a duplicate write.\n    ///\n    /// Returns `Some(mtask_id)` if the key exists and body hash matches.\n    /// Returns `Err` if the key exists but body hash differs.\n    /// Returns `None` if the key is not found (caller should process).\n    pub async fn check(&self, key: &str, body_hash: &str) -> Result<Option<String>> {\n        let mut cache = self.cache.write().await;\n        self.prune_expired_locked(&mut cache);\n\n        if let Some(entry) = cache.get(key) {\n            if entry.body_hash == body_hash {\n                return Ok(Some(entry.mtask_id.clone()));\n            } else {\n                return Err(MiroirError::IdempotencyKeyReused);\n            }\n        }\n        Ok(None)\n    }\n\n    /// Insert a new idempotency entry.\n    pub async fn insert(&self, key: String, body_hash: String, mtask_id: String) {\n        let mut cache = self.cache.write().await;\n        self.prune_expired_locked(&mut cache);\n\n        // Enforce max entries (simple FIFO for now)\n        if cache.len() >= self.max_entries {\n            // Remove oldest entry (first key)\n            if let Some(k) = cache.keys().next().cloned() {\n                cache.remove(&k);\n            }\n        }\n\n        let now = SystemTime::now()\n            .duration_since(SystemTime::UNIX_EPOCH)\n            .unwrap_or_default()\n            .as_secs();\n        let expires_at = now + self.ttl_secs;\n\n        cache.insert(key, IdempotencyEntry {\n            body_hash,\n            mtask_id,\n            expires_at,\n        });\n    }\n\n    /// Prune expired entries (must hold write lock).\n    fn prune_expired_locked(&self, cache: &mut HashMap<String, IdempotencyEntry>) {\n        let now = SystemTime::now()\n            .duration_since(SystemTime::UNIX_EPOCH)\n            .unwrap_or_default()\n            .as_secs();\n        cache.retain(|_, v| v.expires_at > now);\n    }\n\n    /// Get current cache size.\n    pub async fn size(&self) -> usize {\n        self.cache.read().await.len()\n    }\n}\n\n/// Fingerprint of a query for coalescing.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]\npub struct QueryFingerprint {\n    /// Index UID.\n    pub index: String,\n    /// Canonical JSON of search query.\n    pub query_json: String,\n    /// Settings version (include to avoid coalescing across settings changes).\n    pub settings_version: u64,\n}\n\n/// Pending query state for coalescing.\npub struct PendingQuery {\n    /// Response broadcast channel.\n    tx: broadcast::Sender<Vec<u8>>,\n    /// When this query was initiated.\n    started_at: Instant,\n}\n\n/// Query coalescing cache.\npub struct QueryCoalescer {\n    /// Fingerprint -> pending query state.\n    pending: Arc<RwLock<HashMap<QueryFingerprint, PendingQuery>>>,\n    /// Coalescing window in milliseconds.\n    window_ms: u64,\n    /// Max concurrent pending queries.\n    max_pending: usize,\n    /// Max subscribers per query.\n    max_subscribers: usize,\n}\n\nimpl QueryCoalescer {\n    /// Create a new query coalescer.\n    pub fn new(window_ms: u64, max_pending: usize, max_subscribers: usize) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            pending: Arc::new(RwLock::new(HashMap::new())),\n            window_ms,\n            max_pending,\n            max_subscribers,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Check if a query can be coalesced.\n    ///\n    /// Returns `Some(receiver)` if a matching query is in flight.\n    /// Returns `None` if no matching query (caller should dispatch).\n    pub async fn try_coalesce(\n        &self,\n        fingerprint: QueryFingerprint,\n    ) -> Option<broadcast::Receiver<Vec<u8>>> {\n        let mut pending = self.pending.write().await;\n        self.prune_stale_locked(&mut pending);\n\n        if let Some(pq) = pending.get(&fingerprint) {\n            if pq.tx.receiver_count() < self.max_subscribers {\n                return Some(pq.tx.subscribe());\n            }\n        }\n        None\n    }\n\n    /// Register a new pending query.\n    ///\n    /// Returns a broadcast sender that the caller should notify on completion.\n    pub async fn register(\n        &self,\n        fingerprint: QueryFingerprint,\n    ) -> Result<broadcast::Sender<Vec<u8>>> {\n        let mut pending = self.pending.write().await;\n        self.prune_stale_locked(&mut pending);\n\n        if pending.len() >= self.max_pending {\n            return Err(MiroirError::TooManyPendingQueries);\n        }\n\n        let (tx, _) = broadcast::channel(self.max_subscribers);\n        pending.insert(fingerprint, PendingQuery {\n            tx: tx.clone(),\n            started_at: Instant::now(),\n        });\n        Ok(tx)\n    }\n\n    /// Unregister a pending query after completion.\n    pub async fn unregister(&self, fingerprint: &QueryFingerprint) {\n        let mut pending = self.pending.write().await;\n        pending.remove(fingerprint);\n    }\n\n    /// Prune stale entries older than the window (must hold write lock).\n    fn prune_stale_locked(&self, pending: &mut HashMap<QueryFingerprint, PendingQuery>) {\n        let window = Duration::from_millis(self.window_ms);\n        let now = Instant::now();\n        pending.retain(|_, pq| now.duration_since(pq.started_at) < window);\n    }\n\n    /// Get current pending count.\n    pub async fn pending_count(&self) -> usize {\n        self.pending.read().await.len()\n    }\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_idempotency_miss_then_hit() {\n        let cache = IdempotencyCache::new(100, 3600);\n\n        // First check should miss.\n        let result = cache.check(\"key1\", \"hash1\").await.unwrap();\n        assert!(result.is_none());\n\n        // Insert.\n        cache.insert(\"key1\".into(), \"hash1\".into(), \"mtask-1\".into()).await;\n\n        // Second check should hit.\n        let result = cache.check(\"key1\", \"hash1\").await.unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(result, Some(\"mtask-1\".into()));\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_idempotency_conflict() {\n        let cache = IdempotencyCache::new(100, 3600);\n\n        cache.insert(\"key1\".into(), \"hash1\".into(), \"mtask-1\".into()).await;\n\n        // Different body hash should error.\n        let result = cache.check(\"key1\", \"hash2\").await;\n        assert!(matches!(result, Err(MiroirError::IdempotencyKeyReused)));\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_idempotency_max_entries() {\n        let cache = IdempotencyCache::new(3, 3600);\n\n        cache.insert(\"key1\".into(), \"hash1\".into(), \"mtask-1\".into()).await;\n        cache.insert(\"key2\".into(), \"hash2\".into(), \"mtask-2\".into()).await;\n        cache.insert(\"key3\".into(), \"hash3\".into(), \"mtask-3\".into()).await;\n\n        // At max.\n        assert_eq!(cache.size().await, 3);\n\n        // Adding a 4th should evict some entry to maintain max size.\n        cache.insert(\"key4\".into(), \"hash4\".into(), \"mtask-4\".into()).await;\n        assert_eq!(cache.size().await, 3);\n        // At least one of the original keys should be evicted.\n        let remaining = [\n            cache.check(\"key1\", \"hash1\").await.unwrap().is_some(),\n            cache.check(\"key2\", \"hash2\").await.unwrap().is_some(),\n            cache.check(\"key3\", \"hash3\").await.unwrap().is_some(),\n        ];\n        assert!(remaining.iter().filter(|&&x| x).count() < 3, \"expected at least one eviction\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_query_fingerprint_eq() {\n        let fp1 = QueryFingerprint {\n            index: \"products\".into(),\n            query_json: r#\"{\"q\":\"laptop\"}\"#.into(),\n            settings_version: 42,\n        };\n        let fp2 = QueryFingerprint {\n            index: \"products\".into(),\n            query_json: r#\"{\"q\":\"laptop\"}\"#.into(),\n            settings_version: 42,\n        };\n        let fp3 = QueryFingerprint {\n            index: \"products\".into(),\n            query_json: r#\"{\"q\":\"phone\"}\"#.into(),\n            settings_version: 42,\n        };\n        assert_eq!(fp1, fp2);\n        assert_ne!(fp1, fp3);\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_coalescer_miss_then_register() {\n        let coalescer = QueryCoalescer::new(50, 100, 10);\n        let fp = QueryFingerprint {\n            index: \"products\".into(),\n            query_json: r#\"{\"q\":\"test\"}\"#.into(),\n            settings_version: 1,\n        };\n\n        // Should not find a pending query.\n        assert!(coalescer.try_coalesce(fp.clone()).await.is_none());\n\n        // Register and get sender.\n        let _tx = coalescer.register(fp.clone()).await.unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(coalescer.pending_count().await, 1);\n\n        // Clean up.\n        coalescer.unregister(&fp).await;\n        assert_eq!(coalescer.pending_count().await, 0);\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_coalescer_hit() {\n        let coalescer = QueryCoalescer::new(50, 100, 10);\n        let fp = QueryFingerprint {\n            index: \"products\".into(),\n            query_json: r#\"{\"q\":\"test\"}\"#.into(),\n            settings_version: 1,\n        };\n\n        // Register.\n        let tx = coalescer.register(fp.clone()).await.unwrap();\n\n        // Should now find the pending query.\n        let rx = coalescer.try_coalesce(fp).await;\n        assert!(rx.is_some());\n\n        // Broadcast response.\n        let response = b\"test response\".to_vec();\n        tx.send(response.clone()).ok();\n\n        // Receiver should get it.\n        if let Some(mut rx) = rx {\n            let received = rx.recv().await.unwrap();\n            assert_eq!(received, response);\n        }\n    }\n}\n","numLines":330,"startLine":1,"totalLines":330}}}

Env override: `MIROIR_MASTER_KEY`.\n    pub master_key: String,\n    /// Key Miroir uses on Meilisearch nodes. Env override: `MIROIR_NODE_MASTER_KEY`.\n    pub node_master_key: String,\n\n    // --- Core topology ---\n    /// Total number of logical shards.\n    pub shards: u32,\n    /// Replication factor (intra-group replicas per shard). Production: 2.\n    pub replication_factor: u32,\n    /// Number of independent query pools. Default 1; production: 2.\n    pub replica_groups: u32,\n\n    // --- Sub-structs ---\n    pub nodes: Vec<NodeConfig>,\n    pub task_store: TaskStoreConfig,\n    pub admin: AdminConfig,\n    pub health: HealthConfig,\n    pub scatter: ScatterConfig,\n    pub rebalancer: RebalancerConfig,\n    pub server: ServerConfig,\n    pub connection_pool_per_node: ConnectionPoolConfig,\n    pub advanced::ReplicaSelectionConfig,\n    pub query_planner: advanced::QueryPlannerConfig,\n    pub settings_broadcast: advanced::SettingsBroadcastConfig,\n    pub settings_drift_check: advanced::SettingsDriftCheckConfig,\n    pub session_pinning: advanced::SessionPinningConfig,\n    pub aliases: advanced::AliasesConfig,\n    pub anti_entropy: advanced::AntiEntropyConfig,\n    pub dump_import: advanced::DumpImportConfig,\n    pub idempotency: advanced::IdempotencyConfig,\n    pub query_coalescing: advanced::QueryCoalescingConfig,\n    pub multi_search: advanced::MultiSearchConfig,\n    pub vector_search: advanced::VectorSearchConfig,\n    pub cdc: advanced::CdcConfig,\n    pub ttl: advanced::TtlConfig,\n    pub tenant_affinity: advanced::TenantAffinityConfig,\n    pub shadow: advanced::ShadowConfig,\n    pub ilm: advanced::IlmConfig,\n    pub canary_runner: advanced::CanaryRunnerConfig,\n    pub explain: advanced::ExplainConfig,\n    pub admin_ui: advanced::AdminUiConfig,\n    pub search_ui: advanced::SearchUiConfig,\n    pub tracing: advanced::TracingConfig,\n\n    // --- §14 horizontal scaling ---\n    pub peer_discovery: PeerDiscoveryConfig,\n    pub leader_election: LeaderElectionConfig,\n    pub hpa: HpaConfig,\n}\n\n/// Convenience alias.\npub type Config = MiroirConfig;\n\nimpl Default for MiroirConfig {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            master_key: String::new(),\n            node_master_key: String::new(),\n            shards: 64,\n            replication_factor: 2,\n            replica_groups: 1,\n            nodes: Vec::new(),\n            task_store: TaskStoreConfig::default(),\n            admin: AdminConfig::default(),\n            health: HealthConfig::default(),\n            scatter: ScatterConfig::default(),\n            rebalancer: RebalancerConfig::default(),\n            server: ServerConfig::default(),\n            connection_pool_per_node: ConnectionPoolConfig::default(),\n            task_registry: TaskRegistryConfig::default(),\n            resharding: advanced::ReshardingConfig::default(),\n            hedging: advanced::HedgingConfig::default(),\n            replica_selection: advanced::ReplicaSelectionConfig::default(),\n            query_planner: advanced::QueryPlannerConfig::default(),\n            settings_broadcast: advanced::SettingsBroadcastConfig::default(),\n            settings_drift_check: advanced::SettingsDriftCheckConfig::default(),\n            session_pinning: advanced::SessionPinningConfig::default(),\n            aliases: advanced::AliasesConfig::default(),\n            anti_entropy: advanced::AntiEntropyConfig::default(),\n            dump_import: advanced::DumpImportConfig::default(),\n            idempotency: advanced::IdempotencyConfig::default(),\n            query_coalescing: advanced::QueryCoalescingConfig::default(),\n            multi_search: advanced::MultiSearchConfig::default(),\n            vector_search: advanced::VectorSearchConfig::default(),\n            cdc: advanced::CdcConfig::default(),\n            ttl: advanced::TtlConfig::default(),\n            tenant_affinity: advanced::TenantAffinityConfig::default(),\n            shadow: advanced::ShadowConfig::default(),\n            ilm: advanced::IlmConfig::default(),\n            canary_runner: advanced::CanaryRunnerConfig::default(),\n            explain: advanced::ExplainConfig::default(),\n            admin_ui: advanced::AdminUiConfig::default(),\n            search_ui: advanced::SearchUiConfig::default(),\n            tracing: advanced::TracingConfig::default(),\n            peer_discovery: PeerDiscoveryConfig::default(),\n            leader_election: LeaderElectionConfig::default(),\n            hpa: HpaConfig::default(),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl MiroirConfig {\n    /// Validate cross-field constraints. Returns `Ok(())` or a `ConfigError`.\n    pub fn validate(&self) -> Result<(), ConfigError> {\n        validate::validate(self)\n    }\n\n    /// Layered loading: file → env overrides → CLI extensions.\n25\t///\n26\t/// This is a newtype wrapper around the 8-character hex request ID,\n27\t/// allowing handlers to extract it via `Request.extensions().get::<RequestId>()`.\n28\t#[derive(Clone, Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]\n29\tpub struct RequestId(pub String);\n30\t\n31\timpl RequestId {\n32\t    /// Create a new RequestId from a UUIDv7.\n33\t    ///\n34\t    /// Hashes the full UUIDv7 to produce an 8-character hex ID that is unique\n35\t    /// even for consecutive calls within the same millisecond.\n36\t    pub fn new() -> Self {\n37\t        let uuid = Uuid::now_v7();\n38\t        let bytes = uuid.as_bytes();\n39\t        // Hash the full UUID to ensure uniqueness even within the same millisecond\n40\t        let mut hasher = DefaultHasher::new();\n41\t        hasher.write(bytes);\n42\t        let hash = hasher.finish();\n43\t        // Take first 8 hex chars of 64-bit hash (32 bits is sufficient entropy)\n44\t        Self(format!(\"{:08x}\", hash as u32))\n45\t    }\n46\t\n47\t    /// Get the inner request ID string.\n48\t    pub fn as_str(&self) -> &str {\n49\t        &self.0\n50\t    }\n51\t\n52\t    /// Parse a RequestId from a string.\n53\t    pub fn parse(s: String) -> Option<Self> {\n54\t        if s.len() == 8 && s.chars().all(|c| c.is_ascii_hexdigit()) {\n55\t            Some(Self(s))\n56\t        } else {\n57\t            None\n58\t        }\n59\t    }\n60\t}\n61\t\n62\t/// Session ID wrapper type for read-your-writes session pinning (plan §13.6).\n63\t///\n64\t/// Extracted from the `X-Miroir-Session` header and stored in request extensions.\n65\t/// Handlers can access it via `Request.extensions().get::<SessionId>()`.\n66\t#[derive(Clone, Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]\n67\tpub the inner session ID string.\n77\t    pub fn as_str(&self) -> &str {\n78\t        &self.0\n79\t    }\n80\t\n81\t    /// Parse a SessionId from a string.\n82\t    ///\n83\t    /// Accepts any non-empty string (client-provided UUID or identifier).\n84\t    pub fn parse(s: String) -> Option<Self> {\n85\t        if !s.is_empty() && s.len() <= 256 {\n86\t            Some(Self(s))\n87\t        } else {\n88\t            None\n89\t        }\n90\t    }\n91\t}\n92\t\n93\tpub async fn request_id_middleware(\n94\t    mut req: Request,\n95\t    next: Next,\n96\t) -> Response {\n97\t    // Check for existing request ID in headers\n98\t    let request_id = req\n99\t        .headers()\n100\t        .get(\"x-request-id\")\n101\t        .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())\n102\t        .and_then(|s| RequestId::parse(s.to_string()))\n103\t        .unwrap_or_else(RequestId::new);\n104\t\n105\t    // Store in request extensions for handler access\n106\t    req.extensions_mut().insert(request_id.clone());\n107\t\n108\t    // Set X-Request-Id header on request (for telemetry_middleware to read)\n109\t    if let Ok(val) = HeaderValue::from_str(request_id.as_str()) {\n110\t        req.headers_mut().insert(\"x-request-id\", val);\n111\t    }\n112\t\n113\t    // Process the request\n114\t    let mut response = next.run(req).await;\n115\t\n116\t    // Add X-Request-Id header to response (override if exists)\n117\t    if let Ok(val) = HeaderValue::from_str(request_id.as_str()) {\n118\t        response.headers_mut().insert(\"x-request-id\", val);\n119\t    }\n120\t\n121\t    response\n122\t}\n123\t\n124\t/// Session pinning middleware (plan §13.6).\n125\t///\n126\t/// Extracts the `X-Miroir-Session` header and stores it in request extensions\n127\t/// for handlers to access via `Request.extensions().get::<SessionId>()`.\n128\tpub async fn session_pinning_middleware(\n129\t    mut req: Request,\n130\t    next: Next,\n131\t) -> Response {\n132\t    // Extract session ID from header if present\n133\t    let session_id = req\n134\t        .headers()\n135\t        .get(\"x-miroir-session\")\n136\t        .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())\n137\t        .and_then(|s| SessionId::parse(s.to_string()));\n138\t\n139\t    // Store in request extensions for handler combining metrics and pod_id for middleware.\n149\t#[derive(Clone)]\n150\tpub struct TelemetryState {\n151\t    pub metrics: Metrics,\n152\t    pub pod_id: String,\n153\t}\n154\t\n155\timpl TelemetryState {\n156\t    pub fn new(metrics: Metrics) -> Self {\n157\t        let pod_id = std::env::var(\"POD_NAME\").unwrap_or_else(|_| \"unknown\".to_string());\n158\t        Self { metrics, pod_id }\n159\t    }\n160\t}\n161\t\n162\t/// Global metrics registry shared across all middleware instances.\n163\tpub struct Metrics {\n164\t    registry: Registry,\n165\t\n166\t    // ── Request     // ── Request metrics ──\n416\t        let request_duration = HistogramVec::new(\n417\t            HistogramOpts::new(\"miroir_request_duration_seconds\", \"Request latency in seconds\")\n418\t                .buckets(vec![0.005, 0.01, 0.025, 0.05, 0.1, 0.25, 0.5, 1.0, 2.5, 5.0, 10.0]),\n419\t            &[\"method\", \"path_template\", \"status\"],\n420\t        )\n421\t        .expect(\"failed to create request_duration histogram\");\n422\t\n423\t        let requests_total = CounterVec::new(\n424\t            Opts::new(\"miroir_requests_total\", \"Total number of requests\"),\n425\t            &[\"method\", \"path_template\", \"status\"],\n426\t        )\n427\t        .expect(\"failed to create requests_total counter\");\n428\t\n429\t        let requests_in_flight = Gauge::with_opts(\n430\t            Opts::new(\"miroir_requests_in_flight\", \"Number of requests currently being processed\"),\n431\t        )\n432\t        .expect(\"failed to create requests_in_flight gauge\");\n433\t\n434\t        // ── Node health metrics ──\n435\t        let node_healthy = GaugeVec::new(\n436\t            Opts::new(\"miroir_node_healthy\", \"Health status of backend nodes (1=healthy, 0=unhealthy)\"),\n437\t            &[\"node_id\"],\n438\t        )\n439\t        .expect(\"failed to create node_healthy gauge\");\n440\t\n441\t        let node_request_duration = HistogramVec::new(\n442\t            HistogramOpts::new(\"miroir_node_request_duration_seconds\", \"Latency of individual node requests\")\n443\t                .buckets(vec![0.001, 0.005, 0.01, 0.025, 0.05, 0.1, 0.5, 1.0]),\n444\t            &[\"node_id\", \"operation\"],\n445\t        )\n446\t        .expect(\"failed to create node_request_duration histogram\");\n447\t\n448\t        let node_errors = CounterVec::new(\n449\t            Opts::new(\"miroir_node_errors_total\", \"Number of errors from backend nodes\"),\n450\t            &[\"node_id\", \"error_type\"],\n451\t        )\n452\t        .expect(\"failed to create node_errors counter\");\n453\t\n454\t        // ── Shard metrics ──\n455\t        let shard_coverage = Gauge::with_opts(\n456\t            Opts::new(\"miroir_shard_coverage\", \"Fraction of shards with at least one healthy replica\"),\n457\t        )\n458\t        .expect(\"failed to create shard_coverage gauge\");\n459\t\n460\t        let degraded_shards = Gauge::with_opts(\n461\t            Opts::new(\"miroir_degraded_shards_total\", \"Number of shards with reduced replica availability\"),\n462\t        )\n463\t        .expect(\"failed to create degraded_shards gauge\");\n464\t\n465\t        let shard_distribution = GaugeVec::new(\n466\t            Opts::new(\"miroir_shard_distribution\", \"Number of shards assigned to each node\"),\n467\t            &[\"node_id\"],\n468\t        )\n469\t        .expect(\"failed to create shard_distribution gauge\");\n470\t\n471\t        // ── Task metrics ──\n472\t        let task_processing_age = Histogram::with_opts(\n473\t            HistogramOpts::new(\"miroir_task_processing_age_seconds\", \"Time between task creation and processing start\")\n474\t                .buckets(vec![0.01, 0.05, 0.1, 0.25, 0.5, 1.0, 2.5, 5.0, 10.0, 30.0, 60.0]),\n475\t        )\n476\t        .expect(\"failed to create task_processing_age histogram\");\n477\t\n478\t        let tasks_total = CounterVec::new(\n479\t            Opts::new(\"miroir_tasks_total\", \"Total number of tasks by status\"),\n480\t            &[\"status\"],\n481\t        )\n482\t        .expect(\"failed to create tasks_total counter\");\n483\t\n484\t        let task_registry_size = Gauge::with_opts(\n485\t            Opts::new(\"miroir_task_registry_size\", \"Current number of tasks in the registry\"),\n486\t        )\n487\t        .expect(\"failed to create task_registry_size gauge\");\n488\t\n489\t        // ── Scatter-gather metrics ──\n490\t        let scatter_fan_out_size = Histogram::with_opts(\n491\t            HistogramOpts::new(\"miroir_scatter_fan_out_size\", \"Number of nodes in scatter operations\")\n492\t                .buckets(vec![1.0, 2.0, 3.0, 5.0, 10.0, 20.0, 50.0]),\n493\t        )\n494\t        .expect(\"failed to create scatter_fan_out_size histogram\");\n495\t\n496\t        let scatter_partial_responses = Counter::with_opts(\n497\t            Opts::new(\"miroir_scatter_partial_responses_total\", \"Number of scatter responses that were partial (some nodes failed)\"),\n498\t        )\n499\t        .expect(\"failed to create scatter_partial_responses counter\");\n500\t\n501\t        let scatter_retries = Counter::with_opts(\n502\t            Opts::new(\"miroir_scatter_retries_total\", \"Number of scatter retry attempts due to node failures\"),\n503\t        )\n504\t        .expect(\"failed to create scatter_retries counter\");\n505\t\n506\t        // ── Rebalancer metrics ──\n507\t        let rebalance_in_progress = Gauge::with_opts(\n508\t            Opts::new(\"miroir_rebalance_in_progress\", \"Whether a rebalance is currently running (1=yes, 0=no)\"),\n509\t        )\n510\t        .expect(\"failed to create rebalance_in_progress gauge\");\n511\t\n512\t        let rebalance_documents_migrated = Counter::with_opts(\n513\t            Opts::new(\"miroir_rebalance_documents_migrated_total\", \"Total number of documents migrated during rebalance\"),\n514\t        )\n515\t        .expect(\"failed to create rebalance_documents_migrated counter\");\n516\t\n517\t        let rebalance_duration = Histogram::with_opts(\n518\t            HistogramOpts::new(\"miroir_rebalance_duration_seconds\", \"Duration of rebalance operations\")\n519\t                .buckets(vec![1.0, 5.0, 10.0, 30.0, 60.0, 300.0, 600.0, 1800.0, 3600.0]),\n520\t        )\n521\t        .expect(\"failed to create rebalance_duration histogram\");\n522\t\n523\t        // Register all metrics\n524\t        macro_rules! reg {\n525\t            ($m:expr) => {\n526\t                registry.register(Box::new($m.clone())).expect(concat!(\"failed to register \", stringify!($m)));\n527\t            };\n528\t        }\n529\t\n530\t        reg!(request_duration);\n531\t        reg!(requests_total);\n532\t        reg!(requests_in_flight);\n533\t        reg!(node_healthy);\n534\t        reg!(node_request_duration);\n535\t        reg!(node_errors);\n536\t        reg!(shard_coverage);\n537\t        reg!(degraded_shards);\n538\t        reg!(shard_distribution);\n539\t        reg!(task_processing_age);\n540\t        reg!(tasks_total);\n541\t        reg!(task_registry_size);\n542\t        reg!(scatter_fan_out_size);\n543\t        reg!(scatter_partial_responses);\n544\t        reg!(scatter_retries);\n545\t        reg!(rebalance_in_progress);\n546\t        reg!(rebalance_documents_migrated);\n547\t        reg!(rebalance_duration);\n548\t\n549\t        // ── §13.11 Multi-search metrics (cardinality cap: top 100 tenants, rest bucketed) ──\n550\t        let (\n551\t            multisearch_queries_per_batch,\n552\t            multisearch_batches_total,\n553\t            multisearch_partial_failures_total,\n554\t            multisearch_tenant_session_pin_override_total,\n555\t        ) = if config.multi_search.enabled {\n556\t            let q = Histogram::with_opts(\n557\t                HistogramOpts::new(\"miroir_multisearch_queries_per_batch\", \"Number of queries in each multi-search batch\")\n558\t                    .buckets(vec![1.0, 2.0, 5.0, 10.0, 25.0, 50.0, 100.0]),\n559\t            ).expect(\"create multisearch_queries_per_batch\");\n560\t            let b = Counter::with_opts(\n561\t                Opts::new(\"miroir_multisearch_batches_total\", \"Total number of multi-search batches processed\"),\n562\t            ).expect(\"create multisearch_batches_total\");\n563\t            let p = Counter::with_opts(\n564\t                Opts::new(\"miroir_multisearch_partial_failures_total\", \"Number of multi-search batches with at least one query failure\"),\n565\t            ).expect(\"create multisearch_partial_failures_total\");\n566\t            let t = CounterVec::new(\n567\t                Opts::new(\"miroir_tenant_session_pin_override_total\", \"Session pin overrides triggered by multi-search tenant routing\"),\n568\t                &[\"tenant\"],\n569\t            ).expect(\"create multisearch_tenant_session_pin_override_total\");\n570\t            reg!(q); reg!(b); reg!(p); reg!(t);\n571\t            (Some(q), Some(b), Some(p), Some(t))\n572\t        } else {\n573\t            (None, None, None, None)\n574\t        };\n575\t\n576\t        // ── {\n1097\t        let encoder = TextEncoder::new();\n1098\t        let metric_families = self.registry.gather();\n1099\t        let mut buffer = Vec::new();\n1100\t        encoder.encode(&metric_families, &mut buffer)?;\n1101\t        Ok(String::from_utf8(buffer).map_err(|e| {\n1102\t            prometheus::Error::Msg(format!(\"failed to convert metrics to UTF-8: {}\", e))\n1103\t        })?)\n1104\t    }\n1105\t\n1106\t    pub fn admin_session_key_generated(&self) -> Gauge {\n1107\t        self.admin_session_key_generated.clone()\n1108\t    }\n1109\t\n1110\t    pub fn admin_session_revoked_total(&self) -> Counter {\n1111\t        self.admin_session_revoked_total.clone()\n1112\t    }\n1113\t}\n1114\t\n1115\t/// Generate a short request ID from UUIDv7.\n1116\t///\n1117\t/// UUIDv7 provides time-ordered unique IDs. We take the first 8 hex characters,\n1118\t/// hash them, and encode as hex for a short, URL-safe identifier.\n1119\tpub fn generate_request_id() -> String {\n1120\t    let uuid = Uuid::now_v7();\n1121\t    let uuid_str = uuid.simple().to_string();\n1122\t    // Take first 8 chars (enough entropy for uniqueness)\n1123\t    let prefix = &uuid_str[..8];\n1124\t\n1125\t    // Hash to get a consistent short representation\n1126\t    let mut hasher = DefaultHasher::new();\n1127\t    prefix.hash(&mut hasher);\n1128\t    let hash = hasher.finish();\n1129\t\n1130\t    // Encode as hex (16 chars = 64 bits)\n1131\t    format!(\"{:016x}\", hash)\n1132\t}\n1133\t\n1134\t/// Extension trait to add request ID extraction utilities.\n1135\tpub trait RequestIdExt {\n1136\t    fn get_request_id(&self) -> Option<String>;\n1137\t    fn set_request_id(&mut self, id: &str);\n1138\t}\n1139\t\n1140\timpl RequestIdExt for HeaderMap {\n1141\t    fn get_request_id(&self) -> Option<String> {\n1142\t        self.get(\"x-request-id\")\n1143\t            .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())\n1144\t            .map(|s| s.to_string())\n1145\t    }\n1146\t\n1147\t    fn set_request_id(&mut self, id: &str) {\n1148\t        if let Ok(val) = HeaderValue::from_str(id) {\n1149\t            self.insert(\"x-request-id\", val);\n1150\t        }\n1151\t    }\n1152\t}\n1153\t\n1154\t/// Guard that decrements the in-flight gauge when dropped.\n1155\t///\n1156\t/// This ensures that even if the handler panics, the in-flight count\n1157\t/// is accurately decremented.\n1158\tstruct InFlightGuard {\n1159\t    metrics: Metrics,\n1160\t    request_id: String,\n1161\t}\n1162\t\n1163\timpl InFlightGuard {\n1164\t    fn new(metrics: Metrics, request_id: String) -> Self {\n1165\t        metrics.requests_in_flight.inc();\n1166\t        tracing::trace!(\n1167\t            request_id = %request_id,\n1168\t            requests_in_flight = metrics.requests_in_flight.get(),\n1169\t            \"request started\"\n1170\t        );\n1171\t        Self { metrics, request_id }\n1172\t    }\n1173\t}\n1174\t\n1175\timpl Drop for InFlightGuard {\n1176\t    fn drop(&mut self) {\n1177\t        self.metrics.requests_in_flight.dec();\n1178\t        tracing::trace!(\n1179\t            request_id = %self.request_id,\n1180\t            requests_in_flight = self.metrics.requests_in_flight.get(),\n1181\t            \"request completed\"\n1182\t        );\n1183\t    }\n1184\t}\n1185\t\n1186\t/// Extract the path template from the matched route.\n1187\t///\n1188\t/// Axum's MatchedPath extractor provides the route template (e.g., \"/indexes/{uid}/search\")\n1189\t/// instead of the actual path (e.g., \"/indexes/products/search\"), avoiding high-cardinality labels.\n1190\tfn extract_path_template(request: &Request) -> String {\n1191\t    request\n1192\t        .extensions()\n1193\t        .get::<axum::extract::MatchedPath>()\n1194\t        .map(|mp| mp.as_str())\n1195\t        .unwrap_or_else(|| request.uri().path())\n1196\t        .to_string()\n1197\t}\n1198\t\n1199\t/// Main middleware that combines request ID injection, structured logging, and Prometheus metrics.\n1200\t///\n1201\t/// IMPORTANT: This middleware must be applied AFTER request_id_middleware in the layer stack\n1202\t/// (i.e., its layer() call must come BEFORE request_id_middleware's layer() call).\n1203\t/// This ensures the request_id header is already set when this middleware runs.\n1204\tpub async fn telemetry_middleware(\n1205\t    State(telemetry): State<TelemetryState>,\n1206\t    mut req: Request,\n1207\t    next: Next,\n1208\t) -> Response {\n1209\t    let start = Instant::now();\n1210\t    let method = req.method().clone();\n1211\t    let path_template = extract_path_template(&req);\n1212\t    let metrics = telemetry.metrics.clone();\n1213\t    let pod_id = telemetry.pod_id.clone();\n1214\t\n1215\t    // Extract request ID from header (set by request_id_middleware)\n1216\t    // The header must already exist because request_id_middleware runs first.\n1217\t    let request_id = req\n1218\t        .headers()\n1219\t        .get_request_id()\n1220\t        .expect(\"request_id header must be set by request_id_middleware\");\n1221\t    req.headers_mut().set_request_id(&request_id);\n1222\t\n1223\t    // Create span for structured logging with pod_id included.\n1224\t    // Note: raw path is intentionally omitted to avoid logging index names\n1225\t    // (which may contain customer identifiers). Use path_template instead.\n1226\t    let span = info_span!(\n1227\t        \"request\",\n1228\t        request_id = %request_id,\n1229\t        pod_id = %pod_id,\n1230\t        method = %method,\n1231\t        path_template = %path_template,\n1232\t    );\n1233\t\n1234\t    let _guard = span.enter();\n1235\t\n1236\t    // Track in-flight requests\n1237\t    let in_flight = InFlightGuard::new(metrics.clone(), request_id.clone());\n1238\t\n1239\t    let response = next.run(req).await;\n1240\t\n1241\t    drop(in_flight);\n1242\t\n1243\t    let status = response.status();\n1244\t    let status_u16 = status.as_u16();\n1245\t    let duration = start.elapsed();\n1246\t\n1247\t    // Record Prometheus metrics\n1248\t    metrics\n1249\t        .request_duration\n1250\t        .with_label_values(&[method.as_str(), &path_template, &status_u16.to_string()])\n1251\t        .observe(duration.as_secs_f64());\n1252\t    metrics\n1253\t        .requests_total\n1254\t        .with_label_values(&[method.as_str(), &path_template, &status_u16.to_string()])\n1255\t        .inc();\n1256\t\n1257\t    // Structured log entry (plan §10 shape)\n1258\t    // Base fields: timestamp (from tracing-subscriber), level, message, duration_ms\n1259\t    // Additional fields (index, node_count, estimated_hits, degraded)\n1260\t    // are added by request handlers via the tracing span.\n1261\t    let message = format!(\"{} {}\", method, status);\n1262\t    if status.is_server_error() {\n1263\t        tracing::error!(\n1264\t            target: \"miroir.request\",\n1265\t            pod_id = %pod_id,\n1266\t            request_id = %request_id,\n1267\t            message = %message,\n1268\t            duration_ms = duration.as_millis() as u64,\n1269\t            status = status_u16,\n1270\t            method = %method,\n1271\t            path_template = %path_template,\n1272\t        );\n1273\t    } else if status.is_client_error() {\n1274\t        tracing::warn!(\n1275\t            target: \"miroir.request\",\n1276\t            pod_id = %pod_id,\n1277\t            request_id = %request_id,\n1278\t            message = %message,\n1279\t            duration_ms = duration.as_millis() as u64,\n1280\t            status = status_u16,\n1281\t            method = %method,\n1282\t            path_template = %path_template,\n1283\t        );\n1284\t    } else {\n1285\t        tracing::info!(\n1286\t            target: \"miroir.request\",\n1287\t            pod_id = %pod_id,\n1288\t            request_id = %request_id,\n1289\t            message = %message,\n1290\t            duration_ms = duration.as_millis() as u64,\n1291\t            status = status_u16,\n1292\t            method = %method,\n1293\t            path_template = %path_template,\n1294\t        );\n1295\t    }\n1296\t\n1297\t    // Ensure request ID is in response headers\n1298\t    let mut response = response;\n1299\t    if !response.headers().contains_key(\"x-request-id\") {\n1300\t        if let Ok(val) = HeaderValue::from_str(&request_id) {\n1301\t            response.headers_mut().insert(\"x-request-id\", val);\n1302\t        }\n1303\t    }\n1304\t\n1305\t    response\n1306\t}\n1307\t\n1308\t/// Create the metrics router for the :9090 server.\n1309\tpub fn metrics_router() -> Router<Metrics> {\n1310\t    Router::new().route(\"/metrics\", get(metrics_handler))\n1311\t}\n1312\t\n1313\t/// Handler that returns Prometheus metrics in text exposition format.\n1314\tasync fn metrics_handler(State(metrics): State<Metrics>) -> Response {\n1315\t    let body = match metrics.encode_metrics() {\n1316\t        Ok(metrics) => metrics,\n1317\t        Err(e) => {\n1318\t            tracing::error!(error = %e, \"failed to encode metrics\");\n1319\t            format!(\"# ERROR: failed to encode metrics: {}\\n\", e)\n1320\t        }\n1321\t    };\n1322\t\n1323\t    let mut response = charset=utf-8\"),\n1327\t    );\n1328\t    response\n1329\t}\n1330\t\n1331\t/// Accessor methods for metrics that can be used by other parts of the application.\n1332\timpl Metrics {\n1333\t    // ── Structured logging, request IDs, and Prometheus metrics middleware.\n\nuse std::time::Instant;\n\nuse axum::{\n    extract::{Request, State},\n    http::{HeaderMap, HeaderValue, StatusCode},\n    middleware::Next,\n    response::{Response, IntoResponse},\n    Router,\n    routing::get,\n};\nuse miroir_core::config::MiroirConfig;\nuse prometheus::{\n    Counter, CounterVec, Encoder, Gauge, GaugeVec, Histogram, HistogramOpts, HistogramVec, Opts,\n    Registry, TextEncoder,\n};\nuse tracing::info_span;\nuse uuid::Uuid;\nuse hex;\nuse std::collections::hash_map::DefaultHasher;\nuse std::hash::{Hash, Hasher};\n\n/// Request ID wrapper type for storing in axum Request extensions.\n///\n/// This is a newtype wrapper around the 8-character hex request ID,\n/// allowing handlers to extract it via `Request.extensions().get::<RequestId>()`.\n#[derive(Clone, Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]\npub struct RequestId(pub String);\n\nimpl RequestId {\n    /// Create a new RequestId from a UUIDv7.\n    ///\n    /// Hashes the full UUIDv7 to produce an 8-character hex ID that is unique\n    /// even for consecutive calls within the same millisecond.\n    pub fn new() -> Self {\n        let uuid = Uuid::now_v7();\n        let bytes = uuid.as_bytes();\n        // Hash the full UUID to ensure uniqueness even within the same millisecond\n        let mut hasher = DefaultHasher::new();\n        hasher.write(bytes);\n        let hash = hasher.finish();\n        // Take first 8 hex chars of 64-bit hash (32 bits is sufficient entropy)\n        Self(format!(\"{:08x}\", hash as u32))\n    }\n\n    /// Get the inner request ID string.\n    pub fn as_str(&self) -> &str {\n        &self.0\n    }\n\n    /// Parse a RequestId from a string.\n    pub fn parse(s: String) -> Option<Self> {\n        if s.len() == 8 && s.chars().all(|c| c.is_ascii_hexdigit()) {\n            Some(Self(s))\n        } else {\n            None\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Session ID wrapper type for read-your-writes session pinning (plan §13.6).\n///\n/// Extracted from the `X-Miroir-Session` header and stored in request extensions.\n/// Handlers can access it via `Request.extensions().get::<SessionId>()`.\n#[derive(Clone, Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]\npub struct SessionId(pub String);\n\nimpl Default for SessionId {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self(String::new())\n    }\n}\n\nimpl SessionId {\n    /// Get the inner session ID string.\n    pub fn as_str(&self) -> &str {\n        &self.0\n    }\n\n    /// Parse a SessionId from a string.\n    ///\n    /// Accepts any non-empty string (client-provided UUID or identifier).\n    pub fn parse(s: String) -> Option<Self> {\n        if !s.is_empty() && s.len() <= 256 {\n            Some(Self(s))\n        } else {\n            None\n        }\n    }\n}\n\npub async fn request_id_middleware(\n    mut req: Request,\n    next: Next,\n) -> Response {\n    // Check for existing request ID in headers\n    let request_id = req\n        .headers()\n        .get(\"x-request-id\")\n        .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())\n        .and_then(|s| RequestId::parse(s.to_string()))\n        .unwrap_or_else(RequestId::new);\n\n    // Store in request extensions for handler access\n    req.extensions_mut().insert(request_id.clone());\n\n    // Set X-Request-Id header on request (for telemetry_middleware to read)\n    if let Ok(val) = HeaderValue::from_str(request_id.as_str()) {\n        req.headers_mut().insert(\"x-request-id\", val);\n    }\n\n    // Process the request\n    let mut response = next.run(req).await;\n\n    // Add X-Request-Id header to response (override if exists)\n    if let Ok(val) = HeaderValue::from_str(request_id.as_str()) {\n        response.headers_mut().insert(\"x-request-id\", val);\n    }\n\n    response\n}\n\n/// Session pinning middleware (plan §13.6).\n///\n/// Extracts the `X-Miroir-Session` header and stores it in request extensions\n/// for handlers to access via `Request.extensions().get::<SessionId>()`.\npub async fn session_pinning_middleware(\n    mut req: Request,\n    next: Next,\n) -> Response {\n    // Extract session ID from header if present\n    let session_id = req\n        .headers()\n        .get(\"x-miroir-session\")\n        .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())\n        .and_then(|s| SessionId::parse(s.to_string()));\n\n    // Store in request extensions for handler access\n    if let Some(sid) = session_id {\n        req.extensions_mut().insert(sid);\n    }\n\n    next.run(req).await\n}\n\n\n/// Telemetry state combining metrics and pod_id for middleware.\n#[derive(Clone)]\npub struct TelemetryState {\n    pub metrics: Metrics,\n    pub pod_id: String,\n}\n\nimpl TelemetryState {\n    pub fn new(metrics: Metrics) -> Self {\n        let pod_id = std::env::var(\"POD_NAME\").unwrap_or_else(|_| \"unknown\".to_string());\n        Self { metrics, pod_id }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Global metrics registry shared across all middleware instances.\npub struct Metrics {\n    registry: Registry,\n\n    // ── Request metrics ──\n    request_duration: HistogramVec,\n    requests_total: CounterVec,\n    requests_in_flight: Gauge,\n\n    // ── Node health metrics ──\n    node_healthy: GaugeVec,\n    node_request_duration: HistogramVec,\n    node_errors: CounterVec,\n\n    // ── Shard metrics ──\n    shard_coverage: Gauge,\n    degraded_shards: Gauge,\n    shard_distribution: GaugeVec,\n\n    // ── Task metrics ──\n    task_processing_age: Histogram,\n    tasks_total: CounterVec,\n    task_registry_size: Gauge,\n\n    // ── Scatter-gather metrics ──\n    scatter_fan_out_size: Histogram,\n    scatter_partial_responses: Counter,\n    scatter_retries: Counter,\n\n    // ── Rebalancer metrics ──\n    rebalance_in_progress: Gauge,\n    rebalance_documents_migrated: Counter,\n    rebalance_duration: Histogram,\n\n    // ── §13.11 Multi-search metrics (feature-gated) ──\n    multisearch_queries_per_batch: Option<Histogram>,\n    multisearch_batches_total: Option<Counter>,\n    multisearch_partial_failures_total: Option<Counter>,\n    multisearch_tenant_session_pin_override_total: Option<CounterVec>,\n\n    // ── §13.12 Vector search metrics (feature-gated) ──\n    vector_search_over_fetched_total: Option<Counter>,\n    vector_merge_strategy: Option<CounterVec>,\n    vector_embedder_drift_total: Option<Counter>,\n\n    // ── §13.13 CDC metrics (feature-gated) ──\n    cdc_events_published_total: Option<CounterVec>,\n    cdc_lag_seconds: Option<GaugeVec>,\n    cdc_buffer_bytes: Option<GaugeVec>,\n    cdc_dropped_total: Option<CounterVec>,\n    cdc_events_suppressed_total: Option<CounterVec>,\n\n    // ── §13.14 TTL metrics (feature-gated) ──\n    ttl_documents_expired_total: Option<CounterVec>,\n    ttl_sweep_duration_seconds: Option<HistogramVec>,\n    ttl_pending_estimate: Option<GaugeVec>,\n\n    // ── §13.15 Tenant affinity metrics (feature-gated) ──\n    tenant_queries_total: Option<CounterVec>,\n    tenant_pinned_groups: Option<GaugeVec>,\n    tenant_fallback_total: Option<CounterVec>,\n\n    // ── §13.16 Shadow traffic metrics (feature-gated) ──\n    shadow_diff_total: Option<CounterVec>,\n    shadow_kendall_tau: Option<Gauge>,\n    shadow_latency_delta_seconds: Option<Histogram>,\n    shadow_errors_total: Option<CounterVec>,\n\n    // ── §13.17 ILM metrics (feature-gated) ──\n    rollover_events_total: Option<CounterVec>,\n    rollover_active_indexes: Option<GaugeVec>,\n    rollover_documents_expired_total: Option<CounterVec>,\n    rollover_last_action_seconds: Option<GaugeVec>,\n\n    // ── §13.18 Canary metrics (feature-gated) ──\n    canary_runs_total: Option<CounterVec>,\n    canary_latency_ms: Option<HistogramVec>,\n    canary_assertion_failures_total: Option<CounterVec>,\n\n    // ── §13.19 Admin UI metrics (feature-gated) ──\n    admin_ui_sessions_total: Option<Counter>,\n    admin_ui_action_total: Option<CounterVec>,\n    admin_ui_destructive_action_total: Option<CounterVec>,\n\n    // ── §13.20 Explain metrics (feature-gated) ──\n    explain_requests_total: Option<Counter>,\n    explain_warnings_total: Option<CounterVec>,\n    explain_execute_total: Option<Counter>,\n\n    // ── §13.21 Search UI metrics (feature-gated) ──\n    search_ui_sessions_total: Option<Counter>,\n    search_ui_queries_total: Option<CounterVec>,\n    search_ui_zero_hits_total: Option<CounterVec>,\n    search_ui_click_through_total: Option<CounterVec>,\n    search_ui_p95_ms: Option<GaugeVec>,\n\n    // ── §14.9 Resource-pressure metrics (always present) ──\n    memory_pressure: Gauge,\n    cpu_throttled_seconds_total: Counter,\n    request_queue_depth: Gauge,\n    background_queue_depth: GaugeVec,\n    peer_pod_count: Gauge,\n    leader: GaugeVec,\n    owned_shards_count: Gauge,\n\n    // ── Admin session sealing metrics (always present) ──\n    admin_session_key_generated: Gauge,\n    admin_session_revoked_total: Counter,\n\n    // ── §13.5 Two-phase settings broadcast metrics (always present) ──\n    settings_broadcast_phase: GaugeVec,\n    settings_hash_mismatch_total: Counter,\n    settings_drift_repair_total: CounterVec,\n    settings_version: GaugeVec,\n    settings_divergence_alert_total: Counter,\n    frozen_indexes: GaugeVec,\n\n    // ── §13.7 Alias metrics (always present) ──\n    alias_resolutions_total: CounterVec,\n    alias_flips_total: CounterVec,\n\n    // ── §13.6 Session pinning metrics (always present) ──\n    session_active_count: Gauge,\n    session_pin_enforced_total: CounterVec,\n    session_wait_duration_seconds: Histogram,\n    session_wait_timeout_total: CounterVec,\n\n    // ── §13.8 Anti-entropy metrics (always present) ──\n    antientropy_shards_scanned_total: Counter,\n    antientropy_mismatches_found_total: Counter,\n    antientropy_docs_repaired_total: Counter,\n    antientropy_last_scan_completed_seconds: Gauge,\n\n    // ── §13.3 Adaptive replica selection metrics (always present) ──\n    replica_selection_score: GaugeVec,\n    replica_selection_exploration_total: Counter,\n\n    // ── §13.10 Idempotency metrics (always present) ──\n    idempotency_hits_total: CounterVec,\n    idempotency_cache_size: Gauge,\n\n    // ── §13.10 Query coalescing metrics (always present) ──\n    query_coalesce_subscribers_total: Counter,\n    query_coalesce_hits_total: Counter,\n}\n\nimpl Clone for Metrics {\n    fn clone(&self) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            registry: self.registry.clone(),\n            request_duration: self.request_duration.clone(),\n            requests_total: self.requests_total.clone(),\n            requests_in_flight: self.requests_in_flight.clone(),\n            node_healthy: self.node_healthy.clone(),\n            node_request_duration: self.node_request_duration.clone(),\n            node_errors: self.node_errors.clone(),\n            shard_coverage: self.shard_coverage.clone(),\n            degraded_shards: self.degraded_shards.clone(),\n            shard_distribution: self.shard_distribution.clone(),\n            task_processing_age: self.task_processing_age.clone(),\n            tasks_total: self.tasks_total.clone(),\n            task_registry_size: self.task_registry_size.clone(),\n            scatter_fan_out_size: self.scatter_fan_out_size.clone(),\n            scatter_partial_responses: self.scatter_partial_responses.clone(),\n            scatter_retries: self.scatter_retries.clone(),\n            rebalance_in_progress: self.rebalance_in_progress.clone(),\n            rebalance_documents_migrated: self.rebalance_documents_migrated.clone(),\n            rebalance_duration: self.rebalance_duration.clone(),\n            multisearch_queries_per_batch: self.multisearch_queries_per_batch.clone(),\n            multisearch_batches_total:       tenant_queries_total: self.tenant_queries_total.clone(),\n            tenant_pinned_groups: self.tenant_pinned_groups.clone(),\n            tenant_fallback_total: self.tenant_fallback_total.clone(),\n            shadow_diff_total: self.shadow_diff_total.clone(),\n            shadow_kendall_tau: self.shadow_kendall_tau.clone(),\n            shadow_latency_delta_seconds: self.shadow_latency_delta_seconds.clone(),\n            shadow_errors_total: self.shadow_errors_total.clone(),\n            rollover_events_total:          canary_latency_ms: self.canary_latency_ms.clone(),\n            canary_assertion_failures_total: self.canary_assertion_failures_total.clone(),\n            admin_ui_sessions_total: self.admin_ui_destructive_action_total.clone(),\n            explain_requests_total: self.explain_requests_total.clone(),\n            explain_warnings_total: self.explain_warnings_total.clone(),\n            explain_execute_total: self.explain_execute_total.clone(),\n            search_ui_sessions_total: self.search_ui_sessions_total.clone(),\n            search_ui_queries_total: self.search_ui_queries_total.clone(),\n            search_ui_zero_hits_total: self.search_ui_zero_hits_total.clone(),\n            search_ui_click_through_total: self.search_ui_click_through_total.clone(),\n            search_ui_p95_ms: self.search_ui_p95_ms.clone(),\n            memory_pressure: self.memory_pressure.clone(),\n            cpu_throttled_seconds_total: self.cpu_throttled_seconds_total.clone(),\n            request_queue_depth: self.request_queue_depth.clone(),\n            background_queue_depth: self.background_queue_depth.clone(),\n            peer_pod_count: self.peer_pod_count.clone(),\n            leader: self.leader.clone(),\n            owned_shards_count: self.owned_shards_count.clone(),\n            admin_session_key_generated: self.admin_session_key_generated.clone(),\n            admin_session_revoked_total:  settings_divergence_alert_total: self.settings_divergence_alert_total.clone(),\n            frozen_indexes: self.frozen_indexes.clone(),\n            alias_resolutions_total: self.alias_resolutions_total.clone(),\n            alias_flips_total: self.alias_flips_total.clone(),\n            session_active_count: self.session_active_count.clone(),\n
