Document write path: add, replace, and delete documents.\n//!\n//! Implements P2.2 write path:\n//! - Primary key extraction on the hot path\n//! - `_miroir_shard` injection\n//! - Reserved field rejection\n//! - Two-rule quorum\n//!\n//! Implements P2.5 task reconciliation:\n//! - Collects per-node task UIDs\n//! - Registers Miroir task ID (mtask-<uuid>)\n//! - Returns mtask ID to client\n//!\n//! Implements §13.6 session pinning:\n//! - Records writes with session header\n//! - Tracks pinned group (first to reach quorum)\n\nuse axum::extract::{Extension, Path, Query};\nuse axum::response::{IntoResponse, Response};\nuse axum::http::{StatusCode, header};\nuse axum::{Json, Router};\nuse miroir_core::api_error::{MiroirCode, MeilisearchError};\nuse miroir_core::router::{shard_for_key, write_targets_with_migration};\nuse miroir_core::scatter::{DeleteByIdsRequest, DeleteByFilterRequest, NodeClient, WriteRequest, WriteResponse};\nuse miroir_core::task::TaskRegistry;\nuse miroir_core::topology::{Topology, NodeId};\nuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\nuse serde_json::Value;\nuse std::collections::HashMap;\nuse std::sync::Arc;\nuse tracing::{debug, info, instrument};\n\nuse crate::client::HttpClient;\nuse crate::middleware::SessionId;\nuse crate::routes::admin_endpoints::AppState;\n\n/// Document write parameters from query string.\n#[derive(Debug, Deserialize)]\npub struct DocumentsParams {\n    primaryKey: Option<String>,\n}\n\n/// Task response (Meilisearch-compatible).\n#[derive(Debug, Serialize)]\npub struct TaskResponse {\n    taskUid: u64,\n    indexUid: String,\n    status: String,\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    error: Option<Value>,\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    #[serde(rename = \"type\")]\n    error_type: Option<String>,\n}\n\n/// Response for write operations.\n#[derive(Debug, Serialize)]\npub struct DocumentsWriteResponse {\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    taskUid: Option<String>, // Changed to String to hold mtask-<uuid>\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    indexUid: Option<String>,\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    status: Option<String>,\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    error: Option<String>,\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    #[serde(rename = \"type\")]\n    error_type: Option<String>,\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    code: Option<String>,\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    link: Option<String>,\n}\n\n/// Header name for degraded write responses.\npub const HEADER_MIROIR_DEGRADED: &str = \"X-Miroir-Degraded\";\n\n/// Quorum tracking state for write operations.\n#[derive(Debug, Default)]\nstruct QuorumState {\n    /// Per-group ACK counts: group_id -> successful_ack_count\n    group_acks: HashMap<u32, usize>,\n    /// Per-group total node counts: group_id -> total_nodes_attempted\n    group_totals: HashMap<u32, usize>,\n    /// Groups that met quorum: group_id -> true\n    groups_met_quorum: HashMap<u32, bool>,\n    /// Total degraded groups count\n    degraded_groups: u32,\n}\n\nimpl QuorumState {\n    /// Record a write attempt to a node.\n    fn record_attempt(&mut self, group_id: u32, _node_id: &NodeId) {\n        *self.group_totals.entry(group_id).or_insert(0) += 1;\n    }\n\n    /// Record a successful write ACK from a node.\n    fn record_success(&mut self, group_id: u32, _node_id: &NodeId) {\n        *self.group_acks.entry(group_id).or_insert(0) += 1;\n    }\n\n    /// Record a failed write attempt from a node.\n    fn record_failure(&mut self, _group_id: u32) {\n        // Track that this group had a failure\n        // Degraded is determined after checking quorum\n    }\n\n    /// Check if a group has met quorum: floor(RF/2) + 1 ACKs required.\n    fn check_group_quorum(&mut self, group_id: u32, rf: usize) -> bool {\n        let acks = *self.group_acks.get(&group_id).unwrap_or(&0);\n        let quorum = (rf / 2) + 1;\n        let met = acks >= quorum;\n        *self.groups_met_quorum.entry(group_id).or_insert(false) = met;\n        met\n    }\n\n    /// Count how many groups met quorum.\n    fn count_quorum_groups(&self) -> usize {\n        self.groups_met_quorum.values().filter(|&&v| v).count()\n    }\n\n    /// Count degraded groups (groups that exist but didn't meet quorum).\n    fn count_degraded_groups(&mut self, replica_group_count: u32, rf: usize) -> u32 {\n        let mut degraded = 0u32;\n        for group_id in 0..replica_group_count {\n            if !self.check_group_quorum(group_id, rf) {\n                // Only count as degraded if we attempted to write to this group\n                if self.group_totals.contains_key(&group_id) {\n                    degraded += 1;\n                }\n            }\n        }\n        degraded\n    }\n}\n\n/// Build router for document endpoints.\npub fn router<S>() -> Router<S>\nwhere\n    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n{\n    Router::new()\n        .route(\"/\", axum::routing::post(post_documents))\n        .route(\"/\", axum::routing::put(put_documents))\n        .route(\"/\", axum::routing::delete(delete_documents))\n        .route(\"/:id\", axum::routing::delete(delete_document_by_id))\n}\n\n/// POST /indexes/{uid}/documents - Add documents.\n#[instrument(skip_all, fields(index = %index))]\nasync fn post_documents(\n    Path(index): Path<String>,\n    Query(params): Query<DocumentsParams>,\n    Extension(state): Extension<Arc<AppState>>,\n    session_id: Option<Extension<crate::middleware::SessionId>>,\n    Json(documents): Json<Vec<Value>>,\n) -> std::result::Result<Response, MeilisearchError> {\n    // Extract session ID from request extensions (set by session_pinning_middleware)\n    let sid = session_id.and_then(|ext| {\n        let s = ext.0;\n        if s.0.is_empty() { None } else { Some(s.0.clone()) }\n    });\n\n    write_documents_impl(index, params.primaryKey, documents, &state, sid).await\n}\n\n/// PUT /indexes/{uid}/documents - Replace documents.\n#[instrument(skip_all, fields(index = %index))]\nasync fn put_documents(\n    Path(index): Path<String>,\n    Query(params): Query<DocumentsParams>,\n    Extension(state): Extension<Arc<AppState>>,\n    session_id: Option<Extension<crate::middleware::SessionId>>,\n    Json(documents): Json<Vec<Value>>,\n) -> std::result::Result<Response, MeilisearchError> {\n    let sid = session_id.and_then(|ext| {\n        let s = ext.0;\n        if s.0.is_empty() { None } else { Some(s.0.clone()) }\n    });\n    write_documents_impl(index, params.primaryKey, documents, &state, sid).await\n}\n\n/// DELETE /indexes/{uid}/documents - Delete by IDs or filter.\nasync fn delete_documents(\n    Path(index): Path<String>,\n    Extension(state): Extension<Arc<AppState>>,\n    session_id: Option<Extension<crate::middleware::SessionId>>,\n    Json(body): Json<Value>,\n) -> std::result::Result<Response, MeilisearchError> {\n    let sid = session_id.and_then(|ext| {\n        let s = ext.0;\n        if s.0.is_empty() { None } else { Some(s.0.clone()) }\n    });\n\n    // Try to parse as delete by filter first\n    if let Some(filter) = body.get(\"filter\") {\n        let req = DeleteByFilterRequest {\n            index_uid: index.clone(),\n            filter: filter.clone(),\n            origin: None, // Client write\n        };\n        return delete_by_filter_impl(index, req, &state, sid).await;\n    }\n\n    // Try to parse as delete by IDs\n    if let Some(ids) = body.get(\"ids\").and_then(|v| v.as_array()) {\n        let ids: Vec<String> = ids\n            .iter()\n            .filter_map(|v| v.as_str().map(|s| s.to_string()))\n            .collect();\n        if !ids.is_empty() {\n            let req = DeleteByIdsRequest {\n                index_uid: index.clone(),\n                ids,\n                origin: None, // Client write\n            };\n            return delete_by_ids_impl(index, req, &state, sid).await;\n        }\n    }\n\n    // If we get here, the request body is malformed\n    Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n        MiroirCode::ReservedField,\n        \"delete request must include either 'filter' or 'ids' field\",\n    ))\n}\n\n/// DELETE /indexes/{uid}/documents/{id} - Delete single document by ID.\nasync fn delete_document_by_id(\n    Path((index, id)): Path<(String, String)>,\n    Extension(state): Extension<Arc<AppState>>,\n    session_id: Option<Extension<crate::middleware::SessionId>>,\n) -> std::result::Result<Response, MeilisearchError> {\n    let sid = session_id.and_then(|ext| {\n        let s = ext.0;\n        if s.0.is_empty() { None } else { Some(s.0.clone()) }\n    });\n    let req = DeleteByIdsRequest {\n        index_uid: index.clone(),\n        ids: vec![id],\n        origin: None, // Client write\n    };\n    delete_by_ids_impl(index, req, &state, sid).await\n}\n\n/// Implementation for write documents (POST/PUT).\n#[instrument(skip_all, fields(index = %index, session_id))]\nasync fn write_documents_impl(\n    index: String,\n    primary_key: Option<String>,\n    mut documents: Vec<Value>,\n    state: &AppState,\n    session_id: Option<String>,\n) -> std::result::Result<Response, MeilisearchError> {\n    if documents.is_empty() {\n        return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::PrimaryKeyRequired,\n            \"cannot write empty document batch\",\n        ));\n    }\n\n    // 1. Resolve alias to concrete index UID (plan §13.7)\n    // Aliases are resolved before any processing; writes to multi-target aliases are rejected\n    let index_uid = if state.config.aliases.enabled {\n        // Check if the index is an alias\n        let resolved = state.alias_registry.resolve(&index).await;\n        if resolved != vec![index.clone()] {\n            // It was an alias\n            // Check if it's a multi-target alias (read-only, ILM-managed)\n            if state.alias_registry.is_multi_target_alias(&index).await {\n                return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n                    MiroirCode::MultiAliasNotWritable,\n                    format!(\n                        \"alias '{}' is a multi-target alias and is read-only (managed by ILM); writes must go to the concrete index or the write alias\",\n                        index\n                    ),\n                ));\n            }\n            // Single-target alias: record resolution metric and use the target\n            state.metrics.inc_alias_resolution(&index);\n            resolved.into_iter().next().unwrap_or_else(|| index.clone())\n        } else {\n            // Not an alias, use the index name as-is\n            index.clone()\n        }\n    } else {\n        index.clone()\n    };\n\n    // 1. Extract primary key from first document if not provided\n    let primary_key = primary_key.or_else(|| {\n        documents\n            .first()\n            .and_then(|doc| extract_primary_key(doc))\n    });\n\n    let primary_key = primary_key.ok_or_else(|| {\n        MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::PrimaryKeyRequired,\n            format!(\"primary key required for index `{}`\", index),\n        )\n    })?;\n\n    // 2. Validate all documents have the primary key and check for reserved field\n    let anti_entropy_enabled = state.config.anti_entropy.enabled;\n    let updated_at_field = &state.config.anti_entropy.updated_at_field;\n    let ttl_enabled = state.config.ttl.enabled;\n    let expires_at_field = &state.config.ttl.expires_at_field;\n\n    for (i, doc) in documents.iter().enumerate() {\n        // Check for reserved field BEFORE checking primary key (per acceptance criteria)\n        // _miroir_shard is ALWAYS reserved (plan §5)\n        if doc.get(\"_miroir_shard\").is_some() {\n            return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n                MiroirCode::ReservedField,\n                \"document contains reserved field `_miroir_shard`\",\n            ));\n        }\n\n        // _miroir_updated_at is reserved ONLY when anti_entropy.enabled: true (plan §5, §13.8)\n        if anti_entropy_enabled && doc.get(updated_at_field).is_some() {\n            return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n                MiroirCode::ReservedField,\n                format!(\"document contains reserved field `{}` (reserved when anti_entropy.enabled: true)\", updated_at_field),\n            ));\n        }\n\n        // _miroir_expires_at is reserved ONLY when ttl.enabled: true (plan §5, §13.14)\n        // When reserved, clients cannot SET it; the orchestrator controls it. When disabled,\n        // client values pass through end-to-end.\n        if ttl_enabled && doc.get(expires_at_field).is_some() {\n            return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n                MiroirCode::ReservedField,\n                format!(\"document contains reserved field `{}` (reserved when ttl.enabled: true)\", expires_at_field),\n            ));\n        }\n\n        if doc.get(&primary_key).is_none() {\n            return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n                MiroirCode::PrimaryKeyRequired,\n                format!(\n                    \"document at index {} missing primary key field `{}`\",\n                    i, primary_key\n                ),\n            ));\n        }\n    }\n\n    // 3. Inject _miroir_shard and _miroir_updated_at into each document\n    let topology = state.topology.read().await;\n    let shard_count = topology.shards;\n    let rf = topology.rf();\n    let replica_group_count = topology.replica_group_count();\n\n    // Get current timestamp in milliseconds since epoch for _miroir_updated_at stamping\n    let now_ms = if anti_entropy_enabled {\n        Some(\n            std::time::SystemTime::now()\n                .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)\n                .unwrap_or_default()\n                .as_millis() as u64\n        )\n    } else {\n        None\n    };\n\n    for doc in &mut documents {\n        if let Some(pk_value) = doc.get(&primary_key).and_then(|v| v.as_str()) {\n            let shard_id = shard_for_key(pk_value, shard_count);\n            doc[\"_miroir_shard\"] = serde_json::json!(shard_id);\n        }\n\n        // Stamp _miroir_updated_at when anti_entropy is enabled (plan §13.8)\n        // This happens AFTER reserved field validation, so orchestrator-controlled injection is allowed\n        if let Some(timestamp) = now_ms {\n            doc[updated_at_field] = serde_json::json!(timestamp);\n        }\n    }\n\n    // 4. Group documents by target nodes (per-batch grouping for efficient fan-out)\n    let node_documents = group_documents_by_shard(&documents, &primary_key, &topology)?;\n\n    // 5. Fan out to nodes and track quorum\n    let client = HttpClient::new(\n        state.config.node_master_key.clone(),\n        state.config.scatter.node_timeout_ms,\n    );\n\n    let mut quorum_state = QuorumState::default();\n    let mut node_task_uids: HashMap<String, u64> = HashMap::new();\n\n    // For each shard, write to all RF nodes in each replica group (with dual-write support)\n    for (shard_id, docs) in node_documents {\n        // Get migration coordinator reference for dual-write detection\n        let migration_coordinator = state.migration_coordinator.as_ref().map(|c| {\n            // We need a read lock on the coordinator\n            tokio::task::block_in_place(|| {\n                tokio::runtime::Handle::current().block_on(async {\n                    c.read().await\n                })\n            })\n        });\n\n        // Use migration-aware routing\n        let targets = write_targets_with_migration(\n            shard_id,\n            &topology,\n            migration_coordinator.as_deref(),\n        );\n\n        if targets.is_empty() {\n            return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n                MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable,\n                format!(\"no available nodes for shard {}\", shard_id),\n            ));\n        }\n\n        // Track which groups we're targeting for this shard\n\n        for node_id in targets {\n            let node = topology\n                .node(&node_id)\n                .ok_or_else(|| MeilisearchError::new(MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable, \"node not found in topology\"))?;\n\n            let group_id = node.replica_group;\n            quorum_state.record_attempt(group_id, &node_id);\n\n            let req = WriteRequest {\n                index_uid: index_uid.clone(),\n                documents: docs.clone(),\n                primary_key: Some(primary_key.clone()),\n                origin: None, // Client write\n            };\n\n            match client.write_documents(&node_id, &node.address, &req).await {\n                Ok(resp) if resp.success => {\n                    quorum_state.record_success(group_id, &node_id);\n                    if let Some(task_uid) = resp.task_uid {\n                        node_task_uids.insert(node_id.as_str().to_string(), task_uid);\n                    }\n                }\n                Ok(resp) => {\n                    // Non-success response (validation error, etc.)\n                    return Ok(build_json_error_response(build_error_response(resp)));\n                }\n                Err(_) => {\n                    quorum_state.record_failure(group_id);\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    // 6. Apply two-rule quorum logic\n    let degraded_groups = quorum_state.count_degraded_groups(replica_group_count, rf);\n    let quorum_groups = quorum_state.count_quorum_groups();\n\n    // Write success if at least one group met quorum\n    if quorum_groups == 0 {\n        return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::NoQuorum,\n            \"no replica group met quorum\",\n        ));\n    }\n\n    // 6.5. Find the first group to reach quorum (for session pinning, plan §13.6)\n    // Groups are checked in ascending order, so the first one with quorum is the first\n    let first_quorum_group = (0..replica_group_count)\n        .find(|&group_id| {\n            let acks = *quorum_state.group_acks.get(&group_id).unwrap_or(&0);\n            let quorum = (rf / 2) + 1;\n            acks >= quorum\n        })\n        .unwrap_or(0); // Default to group 0 if somehow no quorum (shouldn't happen here)\n\n    // 7. Register Miroir task with collected node task UIDs\n    let miroir_task = state\n        .task_registry\n        .register_with_metadata(\n            node_task_uids.clone(),\n            Some(index_uid.clone()),\n            Some(\"documentAdditionOrUpdate\".to_string()),\n        )\n        .map_err(|e| MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable,\n            format!(\"failed to register task: {}\", e),\n        ))?;\n\n    // 7.5. Record session pinning if session header present (plan §13.6)\n    if let (Some(ref sid), true) = (&session_id, state.session_manager.is_enabled()) {\n        if let Err(e) = state.session_manager.record_write_with_quorum(\n            sid,\n            miroir_task.miroir_id.clone(),\n            first_quorum_group,\n        ).await {\n            // Log error but don't fail the write - session pinning is best-effort\n            tracing::error!(\n                session_id = %sid,\n                error = %e,\n                \"failed to record session pinning for write\"\n            );\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Build success response with degraded header and mtask ID\n    build_response_with_degraded_header(\n        DocumentsWriteResponse {\n            taskUid: Some(miroir_task.miroir_id),\n            indexUid: Some(index_uid.clone()),\n            status: Some(\"enqueued\".to_string()),\n            error: None,\n            error_type: None,\n            code: None,\n            link: None,\n        },\n        degraded_groups,\n    )\n}\n\n/// Implementation for delete by IDs.\nasync fn delete_by_ids_impl(\n    index: String,\n    req: DeleteByIdsRequest,\n    state: &AppState,\n    session_id: Option<String>,\n) -> std::result::Result<Response, MeilisearchError> {\n    if req.ids.is_empty() {\n        return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::PrimaryKeyRequired,\n            \"cannot delete empty ID list\",\n        ));\n    }\n\n    // Resolve alias to concrete index UID (plan §13.7)\n    let index_uid = if state.config.aliases.enabled {\n        let resolved = state.alias_registry.resolve(&index).await;\n        if resolved != vec![index.clone()] {\n            // It was an alias - check if it's a multi-target alias (read-only)\n            if state.alias_registry.is_multi_target_alias(&index).await {\n                return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n                    MiroirCode::MultiAliasNotWritable,\n                    format!(\n                        \"alias '{}' is a multi-target alias and is read-only (managed by ILM); deletes must go to the concrete index or the write alias\",\n                        index\n                    ),\n                ));\n            }\n            // Single-target alias: record resolution metric and use the target\n            state.metrics.inc_alias_resolution(&index);\n            resolved.into_iter().next().unwrap_or_else(|| index.clone())\n        } else {\n            index.clone()\n        }\n    } else {\n        index.clone()\n    };\n\n    let topology = state.topology.read().await;\n    let rf = topology.rf();\n    let replica_group_count = topology.replica_group_count();\n\n    // Group IDs by target shard for independent per-shard routing\n    let mut shard_ids: HashMap<u32, Vec<String>> = HashMap::new();\n    for id in &req.ids {\n        let shard_id = shard_for_key(id, topology.shards);\n        shard_ids.entry(shard_id).or_default().push(id.clone());\n    }\n\n    let client = HttpClient::new(\n        state.config.node_master_key.clone(),\n        state.config.scatter.node_timeout_ms,\n    );\n\n    let mut quorum_state = QuorumState::default();\n    let mut node_task_uids: HashMap<String, u64> = HashMap::new();\n\n    // For each shard, write to all RF nodes in each replica group\n    for (shard_id, ids) in shard_ids {\n        let targets = miroir_core::router::write_targets(shard_id, &topology);\n\n        if targets.is_empty() {\n            return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n                MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable,\n                format!(\"no available nodes for shard {}\", shard_id),\n            ));\n        }\n\n        for node_id in targets {\n            let node = topology\n                .node(&node_id)\n                .ok_or_else(|| MeilisearchError::new(MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable, \"node not found in topology\"))?;\n\n            let group_id = node.replica_group;\n            quorum_state.record_attempt(group_id, &node_id);\n\n            let delete_req = DeleteByIdsRequest {\n                index_uid: index_uid.clone(),\n                ids: ids.clone(),\n                origin: None, // Client write\n            };\n\n            match client.delete_documents(&node_id, &node.address, &delete_req).await {\n                Ok(resp) if resp.success => {\n                    quorum_state.record_success(group_id, &node_id);\n                    if let Some(task_uid) = resp.task_uid {\n                        node_task_uids.insert(node_id.as_str().to_string(), task_uid);\n                    }\n                }\n                Ok(resp) => {\n                    return Ok(build_json_error_response(build_error_response(resp)));\n                }\n                Err(_) => {\n                    quorum_state.record_failure(group_id);\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Apply two-rule quorum logic\n    let degraded_groups = quorum_state.count_degraded_groups(replica_group_count, rf);\n    let quorum_groups = quorum_state.count_quorum_groups();\n\n    // Write success if at least one group met quorum\n    if quorum_groups == 0 {\n        return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::NoQuorum,\n            \"no replica group met quorum\",\n        ));\n    }\n\n    // Find the first group to reach quorum (for session pinning, plan §13.6)\n    let first_quorum_group = (0..replica_group_count)\n        .find(|&group_id| {\n            let acks = *quorum_state.group_acks.get(&group_id).unwrap_or(&0);\n            let quorum = (rf / 2) + 1;\n            acks >= quorum\n        })\n        .unwrap_or(0);\n\n    // Register Miroir task with collected node task UIDs\n    let miroir_task = state\n        .task_registry\n        .register_with_metadata(\n            node_task_uids.clone(),\n            Some(index.clone()),\n            Some(\"documentDeletion\".to_string()),\n        )\n        .map_err(|e| MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable,\n            format!(\"failed to register task: {}\", e),\n        ))?;\n\n    // Record session pinning if session header present (plan §13.6)\n    if let (Some(ref sid), true) = (&session_id, state.session_manager.is_enabled()) {\n        if let Err(e) = state.session_manager.record_write_with_quorum(\n            sid,\n            miroir_task.miroir_id.clone(),\n            first_quorum_group,\n        ).await {\n            tracing::error!(\n                session_id = %sid,\n                error = %e,\n                \"failed to record session pinning for delete\"\n            );\n        }\n    }\n\n    build_response_with_degraded_header(\n        DocumentsWriteResponse {\n            taskUid: Some(miroir_task.miroir_id),\n            indexUid: Some(index.clone()),\n            status: Some(\"enqueued\".to_string()),\n            error: None,\n            error_type: None,\n            code: None,\n            link: None,\n        },\n        degraded_groups,\n    )\n}\n\n/// Implementation for delete by filter (broadcast to all nodes).\nasync fn delete_by_filter_impl(\n    index: String,\n    req: DeleteByFilterRequest,\n    state: &AppState,\n    session_id: Option<String>,\n) -> std::result::Result<Response, MeilisearchError> {\n    let topology = state.topology.read().await;\n    let rf = topology.rf();\n    let replica_group_count = topology.replica_group_count();\n\n    let client = HttpClient::new(\n        state.config.node_master_key.clone(),\n        state.config.scatter.node_timeout_ms,\n    );\n\n    let mut quorum_state = QuorumState::default();\n    let mut node_task_uids: HashMap<String, u64> = HashMap::new();\n\n    // Broadcast to all nodes (cannot shard-route for filters)\n    for node in topology.nodes() {\n        let group_id = node.replica_group;\n        quorum_state.record_attempt(group_id, &node.id);\n\n        match client\n            .delete_documents_by_filter(&node.id, &node.address, &req)\n            .await\n        {\n            Ok(resp) if resp.success => {\n                quorum_state.record_success(group_id, &node.id);\n                if let Some(task_uid) = resp.task_uid {\n                    node_task_uids.insert(node.id.as_str().to_string(), task_uid);\n                }\n            }\n            Ok(resp) => {\n                return Ok(build_json_error_response(build_error_response(resp)));\n            }\n            Err(_) => {\n                quorum_state.record_failure(group_id);\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Apply two-rule quorum logic\n    let degraded_groups = quorum_state.count_degraded_groups(replica_group_count, rf);\n    let quorum_groups = quorum_state.count_quorum_groups();\n\n    // Write success if at least one group met quorum\n    if quorum_groups == 0 {\n        return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::NoQuorum,\n            \"no replica group met quorum\",\n        ));\n    }\n\n    // Find the first group to reach quorum (for session pinning, plan §13.6)\n    let first_quorum_group = (0..replica_group_count)\n        .find(|&group_id| {\n            let acks = *quorum_state.group_acks.get(&group_id).unwrap_or(&0);\n            let quorum = (rf / 2) + 1;\n            acks >= quorum\n        })\n        .unwrap_or(0);\n\n    // Register Miroir task with collected node task UIDs\n    let miroir_task = state\n        .task_registry\n        .register_with_metadata(\n            node_task_uids.clone(),\n            Some(index.clone()),\n            Some(\"documentDeletion\".to_string()),\n        )\n        .map_err(|e| MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable,\n            format!(\"failed to register task: {}\", e),\n        ))?;\n\n    // Record session pinning if session header present (plan §13.6)\n    if let (Some(ref sid), true) = (&session_id, state.session_manager.is_enabled()) {\n        if let Err(e) = state.session_manager.record_write_with_quorum(\n            sid,\n            miroir_task.miroir_id.clone(),\n            first_quorum_group,\n        ).await {\n            tracing::error!(\n                session_id = %sid,\n                error = %e,\n                \"failed to record session pinning for delete by filter\"\n            );\n        }\n    }\n\n    build_response_with_degraded_header(\n        DocumentsWriteResponse {\n            taskUid: Some(miroir_task.miroir_id),\n            indexUid: Some(index.clone()),\n            status: Some(\"enqueued\".to_string()),\n            error: None,\n            error_type: None,\n            code: None,\n            link: None,\n        },\n        degraded_groups,\n    )\n}\n\n/// Extract primary key from a document by checking common field names.\n///\n/// Tries fields in order: id, pk, key, _id.\nfn extract_primary_key(doc: &Value) -> Option<String> {\n    [\"id\", \"pk\", \"key\", \"_id\"]\n        .iter()\n        .find(|&&key| doc.get(key).is_some())\n        .map(|&s| s.to_string())\n}\n\n/// Group documents by their target shard for fan-out optimization.\n///\n/// Returns a map of shard_id -> documents to send to that shard.\n/// The caller then fans out each shard's documents to all RF nodes in each group.\n///\n/// This per-batch grouping minimizes HTTP fan-out count (critical at scale).\nfn group_documents_by_shard(\n    documents: &[Value],\n    primary_key: &str,\n    topology: &Topology,\n) -> std::result::Result<HashMap<u32, Vec<Value>>, MeilisearchError> {\n    let mut shard_documents: HashMap<u32, Vec<Value>> = HashMap::new();\n\n    for doc in documents {\n        let pk_value = doc\n            .get(primary_key)\n            .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n            .ok_or_else(|| {\n                MeilisearchError::new(\n                    MiroirCode::PrimaryKeyRequired,\n                    \"primary key value must be a string\",\n                )\n            })?;\n\n        let shard_id = shard_for_key(pk_value, topology.shards);\n        shard_documents\n            .entry(shard_id)\n            .or_default()\n            .push(doc.clone());\n    }\n\n    Ok(shard_documents)\n}\n\n/// Build an error response from a node error.\nfn build_error_response(resp: WriteResponse) -> DocumentsWriteResponse {\n    DocumentsWriteResponse {\n        taskUid: resp.task_uid.map(|uid| uid.to_string()),\n        indexUid: None,\n        status: None,\n        error: resp.message,\n        error_type: resp.error_type,\n        code: resp.code,\n        link: None,\n    }\n}\n\n/// Build a success response with optional X-Miroir-Degraded header.\nfn build_response_with_degraded_header(\n    response: DocumentsWriteResponse,\n    degraded_groups: u32,\n) -> std::result::Result<Response, MeilisearchError> {\n    let body = serde_json::to_string(&response).map_err(|e| {\n        MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable,\n            format!(\"failed to serialize response: {}\", e),\n        )\n    })?;\n\n    let mut builder = Response::builder()\n        .status(StatusCode::OK)\n        .header(header::CONTENT_TYPE, \"application/json\");\n\n    // Add X-Miroir-Degraded header if any groups were degraded\n    if degraded_groups > 0 {\n        builder = builder.header(HEADER_MIROIR_DEGRADED, format!(\"groups={}\", degraded_groups));\n    }\n\n    Ok(builder\n        .body(axum::body::Body::from(body))\n        .map_err(|e| MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable,\n            format!(\"failed to build response: {}\", e),\n        ))?)\n}\n\n/// Build an error response as JSON (for forwarded node errors).\nfn build_json_error_response(resp: DocumentsWriteResponse) -> Response {\n    (\n        StatusCode::OK,\n        [(header::CONTENT_TYPE, \"application/json\")],\n        Json(resp),\n    )\n        .into_response()\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n    use miroir_core::api_error::{MiroirCode, MeilisearchError};\n    use serde_json::json;\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_extract_primary_key_common_fields() {\n        let doc_with_id = serde_json::json!({\"id\": \"test123\", \"name\": \"Test\"});\n        assert_eq!(extract_primary_key(&doc_with_id), Some(\"id\".to_string()));\n\n        let doc_with_pk = serde_json::json!({\"pk\": \"test456\", \"name\": \"Test\"});\n        assert_eq!(extract_primary_key(&doc_with_pk), Some(\"pk\".to_string()));\n\n        let doc_with_key = serde_json::json!({\"key\": \"test789\", \"name\": \"Test\"});\n        assert_eq!(extract_primary_key(&doc_with_key), Some(\"key\".to_string()));\n\n        let doc_with__id = serde_json::json!({\"_id\": \"test000\", \"name\": \"Test\"});\n        assert_eq!(extract_primary_key(&doc_with__id), Some(\"_id\".to_string()));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_extract_primary_key_no_common_field() {\n        let doc = serde_json::json!({\"name\": \"Test\", \"value\": 42});\n        assert_eq!(extract_primary_key(&doc), None);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_extract_primary_key_priority() {\n        // Should return \"id\" first even if other fields exist\n        let doc = serde_json::json!({\"id\": \"test\", \"pk\": \"other\", \"key\": \"another\"});\n        assert_eq!(extract_primary_key(&doc), Some(\"id\".to_string()));\n    }\n\n    // Reserved field validation &str) -> MeilisearchError {\n        MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::ReservedField,\n            format!(\"document contains reserved field `{}`\", field),\n        )\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_reserved_field_miroir_shard_always_rejected() {\n        // _miroir_shard is ALWAYS reserved regardless of config\n        let err = reserved_field_error(\"_miroir_shard\");\n #[test]\n    fn test_reserved_field_miroir_updated_at_when_anti_entropy_enabled() {\n        // When anti_entropy.enabled: true, _miroir_updated_at is reserved\n        let field = .field(\"ranking_score\", &self.ranking_score)\n144\t            .finish_non_exhaustive()\n145\t    }\n146\t}\n147\t\n148\t/// Search handler with DFS global-IDF preflight (OP#4).\n149\t///\n150\t/// This handler implements the `dfs_query_then_fetch` pattern:\n151\t/// 1. **Preflight phase**: Send term-frequency query to all shards, aggregate\n152\t///    global document frequencies at the coordinator.\n153\t/// 2. **Search phase**: Send the search query with global IDF attached so that\n154\t///    scoring uses corpus-wide statistics instead of per-shard local IDF.\n155\t///\n156\t/// Returns `X-Miroir-Degraded: shards=X,Y,Z` header when any shards are unavailable.\n157\t/// Strips `_miroir_shard` from all hits; strips `_rankingScore` unless client\n158\t/// explicitly requested it.\n159\t///\n160\t/// Session pinning (plan §13.6): If `X-Miroir-Session` header is present and\n161\t/// the session has a pending write, routes to the pinned group for read-your-writes.\n162\t#[instrument(skip_all, fields(index = %index))]\n163\tasync fn search_handler(\n164\t    Path(index): Path<String>,\n165\t    Extension(state): Extension<Arc<AppState>>,\n166\t    headers: HeaderMap,\n167\t    session_id: Option<Extension<SessionId>>,\n168\t    Json(body): Json<SearchRequestBody>,\n169\t) -> Result<Response, StatusCode> {\n170\t    let start = Instant::now();\n171\t    let client_requested_score = body.ranking_score.unwrap_or(false);\n172\t\n173\t    // Extract session ID from request extensions (set by session_pinning_middleware)\n174\t    let sid = session_id.and_then(|ext| {\n175\t        let s = ext.0;\n176\t        if s.0.is_empty() { None } else { Some(s.0.clone()) }\n177\t    });\n178\t\n179\t    // Extract source IP from X-Forwarded-For or X-Real-IP (trust proxy)\n180\t    let source_ip = headers\n181\t        .get(\"x-forwarded-for\")\n182\t        .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())\n183\t        .and_then(|s| s.split(',').next())\n184\t        .or_else(|| headers.get(\"x-real-ip\").and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok()))\n185\t        .unwrap_or(\"unknown\")\n186\t        .trim()\n187\t        .to_string();\n188\t\n189\t    // Check rate limit for search UI (plan §4)\n190\t    let (limit, window_seconds) = match parse_rate_limit(&state.config.search_ui.rate_limit.per_ip) {\n191\t        Ok(parsed) => parsed,\n192\t        Err(e) => {\n193\t            warn!(error = %e, \"invalid search_ui.rate_limit.per_ip config, using default\");\n194\t            (60, 60) // Default: 60 requests per minute\n195\t        }\n196\t    };\n197\t\n198\t    let backend = state.config.search_ui.rate_limit.backend.as_str();\n199\t    if backend == \"redis\" {\n200\t        if let Some(ref redis) = state.redis_store {\n201\t            match redis.check_rate_limit_search_ui(&source_ip, limit, window_seconds) {\n202\t                Ok((allowed, _wait_seconds)) => {\n203\t                    if !allowed {\n204\t                        warn!(\n205\t                            source_ip_hash = hash_for_log(&source_ip),\n206\t                            \"search UI rate limited (redis)\"\n207\t                        );\n208\t                        return Err(StatusCode::TOO_MANY_REQUESTS);\n209\t                    }\n210\t                    // Allowed, proceed\n211\t                }\n212\t                Err(e) => {\n213\t                    warn!(error = %e, \"failed to check search UI rate limit\");\n214\t                    // Continue anyway on error (fail-open)\n215\t                }\n216\t            }\n217\t        }\n218\t    } else if backend == \"local\" {\n219\t        let (allowed, _wait_seconds) = state.local_search_ui_rate_limiter.check(\n220\t            &source_ip,\n221\t            limit,\n222\t            window_seconds * 1000,\n223\t        );\n224\t        if !allowed {\n225\t            warn!(\n226\t                source_ip_hash = hash_for_log(&source_ip),\n227\t                \"search UI rate limited (local backend)\"\n228\t            );\n229\t            return Err(StatusCode::TOO_MANY_REQUESTS);\n230\t        }\n231\t    }\n232\t\n233\t    // Resolve alias to concrete index UID (plan §13.7)\n234\t    // Aliases are resolved at routing time; resolved index is used for scatter planning\n235\t    let (effective_index, resolved_targets) = if state.config.aliases.enabled {\n236\t        let targets = state.alias_registry.resolve(&index).await;\n237\t        state.metrics.inc_alias_resolution(&index);\n238\t        if targets != vec![index.clone()] {\n239\t            // It's an alias\n240\t            (index.clone(), targets)\n241\t        } else {\n242\t            // Not an alias - use index directly\n243\t            (index.clone(), vec![index.clone()])\n244\t        }\n245\t    } else {\n246\t        // Aliases disabled - use index directly\n247\t        (index.clone(), vec![index.clone()])\n248\t    };\n249\t\n250\t    // Session pinning logic (plan §13.6): Check if session has pending write\n251\t    let (pinned_group, strategy_label) = if let Some(ref sid) = sid {\n252\t        if let term-frequency query to all shards, aggregate\n///    global document frequencies at the coordinator.\n/// 2. **Search phase**: Send the search query with global IDF attached so that\n///    scoring uses corpus-wide statistics instead of per-shard local IDF.\n///\n/// Returns `X-Miroir-Degraded: shards=X,Y,Z` header when any shards are unavailable.\n/// Strips `_miroir_shard` from all hits; the pinned group for read-your-writes.\n#[instrument(skip_all, fields(index = %index))]\nasync fn search_handler(\n    Path(index): Path<String>,\n    Extension(state): Extension<Arc<AppState>>,\n    headers: HeaderMap,\n    session_id: Option<Extension<SessionId>>,\n    Json(body): Json<SearchRequestBody>,\n) -> Result<Response, StatusCode> {\n    let start = Instant::now();\n    let   let s = ext.0;\n        if s.0.is_empty() { None } else { Some(s.0.clone()) }\n    });\n\n    // Extract source IP from X-Forwarded-For or X-Real-IP (trust proxy)\n    let source_ip = headers\n        .get(\"x-forwarded-for\")\n        .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())\n        .and_then(|s| s.split(',').next())\n        .or_else(|| headers.get(\"x-real-ip\").and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok()))\n        .unwrap_or(\"unknown\")\n        .trim()\n        .to_string();\n\n    // Check rate limit for search UI (plan §4)\n    let (limit, window_seconds) = match parse_rate_limit(&state.config.search_ui.rate_limit.per_ip) {\n        Ok(parsed) => parsed,\n        Err(e) => {\n            warn!(error = %e, \"invalid search_ui.rate_limit.per_ip config, using default\");\n            (60, 60) // Default: 60 requests per minute\n        }\n    };\n\n    let backend = state.config.search_ui.rate_limit.backend.as_str();\n    if backend == \"redis\" {\n        if let Some(ref redis) = state.redis_store {\n            match redis.check_rate_limit_search_ui(&source_ip, limit, window_seconds) {\n                Ok((allowed, _wait_seconds)) => {\n                    if !allowed {\n                        warn!(\n                            source_ip_hash = hash_for_log(&source_ip),\n                            \"search UI rate limited (redis)\"\n                        );\n                        return Err(StatusCode::TOO_MANY_REQUESTS);\n                    }\n                    // Allowed, proceed\n                }\n                Err(e) => {\n                    warn!(error = %e, \"failed to check search UI rate limit\");\n                    // Continue anyway on error (fail-open)\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    } else if backend == \"local\" {\n        let (allowed, _wait_seconds) = state.local_search_ui_rate_limiter.check(\n            &source_ip,\n            limit,\n            window_seconds * 1000,\n        );\n        if !allowed {\n            warn!(\n                source_ip_hash = hash_for_log(&source_ip),\n                \"search UI rate limited (local backend)\"\n            );\n            return Err(StatusCode::TOO_MANY_REQUESTS);\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Resolve alias to concrete index UID (plan §13.7)\n    // Aliases are resolved at routing time; resolved index is used for scatter planning\n    let (effective_index, resolved_targets) = if state.config.aliases.enabled {\n        let targets = state.alias_registry.resolve(&index).await;\n        state.metrics.inc_alias_resolution(&index);\n        if targets != vec![index.clone()] {\n            // It's an alias\n            (index.clone(), targets)\n        } else {\n            // Not an alias - use index directly\n            (index.clone(), vec![index.clone()])\n        }\n    } else {\n        // Aliases disabled - use index directly\n        (index.clone(), vec![index.clone()])\n    };\n\n    // Session pinning logic (plan §13.6): Check if session has pending write\n    let (pinned_group, strategy_label) = if let Some(ref sid) = sid {\n        if let Some(group) = state.session_manager.get_pinned_group(sid).await {\n            // Session has a pending write - apply wait strategy\n            let strategy = state.session_manager.wait_strategy();\n            match strategy {\n                WaitStrategy::Block => {\n                    // Block until write completes or timeout\n                    let max_wait = state.session_manager.max_wait_duration();\n                    let wait_start = std::time::Instant::now();\n                    match state.session_manager.wait_for_write_completion(\n                        sid,\n                        &state.task_registry,\n                        max_wait,\n                    ).await {\n                        Ok(true) => {\n                            // Write succeeded, clear pin and use normal routing\n                            let wait_duration = wait_start.elapsed().as_secs_f64();\n                            state.metrics.observe_session_wait_duration(wait_duration);\n                            state.metrics.inc_session_pin_enforced(\"block\");\n                            (None, Some(\"block_success\"))\n                        }\n                        Ok(false) => {\n                            // Write failed, clear pin\n                            let wait_duration = wait_start.elapsed().as_secs_f64();\n                            state.metrics.observe_session_wait_duration(wait_duration);\n                            state.metrics.inc_session_wait_timeout(\"block\");\n                            (None, Some(\"block_failed\"))\n                        }\n                        Err(_) => {\n                            // Timeout - still route to pinned group (best effort)\n                            let wait_duration = wait_start.elapsed().as_secs_f64();\n                            state.metrics.observe_session_wait_duration(wait_duration);\n                            state.metrics.inc_session_wait_timeout(\"block\");\n                            (Some(group), Some(\"block_timeout\"))\n                        }\n                    }\n                }\n                WaitStrategy::RoutePin => {\n                    // Route to pinned group without waiting\n                    state.metrics.inc_session_pin_enforced(\"route_pin\");\n                    (Some(group), Some(\"route_pin\"))\n                }\n            }\n        } else {\n None)","numLines":300,"startLine":1,"totalLines":1075}}}

client\n                .get(&url)\n                .header(\"Authorization\", format!(\"Bearer {}\", self.node_master_key))\n                .send()\n                .await;\n\n            if let Ok(resp) = response {\n                if resp.status().is_success() {\n                    if let Ok(body) = resp.text().await {\n                        // Update cache\n                        *self.version_cache.write().await = Some(body.clone());\n                        *self.last_cache_update.write().await = Some(std::time::Instant::now());\n                        return Ok(body);\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        Err(StatusCode::SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE)\n    }\n}\n\n// P5.10 §13.10 Idempotency keys + query coalescing acceptance tests.\n//!\n//! Acceptance criteria:\n//! - Same Idempotency-Key + same body twice → one mtask returned both times\n//! - Same key + different body → 409 `miroir_idempotency_key_reused`\n//! - Hot query (1000 identical concurrent requests) → ≤ 10 scatters fire (one per 50ms window)\n//! - Settings change mid-coalesce-window → next query starts fresh (doesn't merge with pre-change queries)\n\nuse tokio::time::sleep;\n\n/// Helper to compute SHA256 hash of a JSON value.\nfn compute_hash(value: &serde_json::Value) -> String {\n    let mut hasher = Sha256::new();\n body → one mtask returned\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn p5_10_a1_same_key_same_body_returns_cached_mtask() {\n    let cache = IdempotencyCache::new(100, 3600);\n    let key = \"test-key-123\";\n    let body = json!({\"id\": \"doc1\", \"name\": \"Test Document\"});\n    let body_hash = compute_hash(&body);\n\n    // First cache.insert(key.to_string(), body_hash.clone(), \"mtask-abc123\".to_string()).await;\n\n    // Second check should hit with cached mtask ID\n    let result = cache.check(key, &body_hash).await.unwrap();\n    assert_eq!(result, Some(\"mtask-abc123\".to_string()), \"second check should return cached mtask ID\");\n}\n\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n// P5.10-A2: Same key + different body → 409 error\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn p5_10_a2_same_key_different_body_returns_conflict() {\n    let cache = IdempotencyCache::new(100, 3600);\n    let key = \"test-key-456\";\n\n    // Insert first body\n    let body1 = json!({\"id\": \"doc1\", \"name\": \"Original\"});\n    let hash1 = compute_hash(&body1);\n    cache.insert(key.to_string(), hash1.clone(), \"mtask-xyz789\".to_string()).await;\n\n    // Try with different body - should get IdempotencyKeyReused error\n    let body2 = json!({\"id\": \"doc1\", \"name\": \"Modified\"});\n    let hash2 = compute_hash(&body2);\n\n    let result = cache.check(key, &hash2).await;\n    assert!(matches!(result, Err(MiroirError::IdempotencyKeyReused)),\n            \"same key with different body should return IdempotencyKeyReused error\");\n}\n\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n// P5.10-A3: Hot query coalescing (1000 concurrent identical queries)\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn p5_10_a3_hot_query_coalesces_scatters() {\n    let coalescer = QueryCoalescer::new(50, 10000, 1000); // 50ms window\n    let index = \"products\".to_string();\n    let query_body = json!({\"q\": \"laptop\", \"limit\": 10});\n    let settings_version = 1;\n\n    let fingerprint = QueryFingerprint::new(index.clone(), &query_body, settings_version);\n\n    // Simulate hot query pattern: 1000 concurrent identical requests\n    // With 50ms window and assuming 20ms processing time, we expect ~2-3 windows\n    // Let's verify the coalescing mechanism works by tracking actual scatters\n\n    let mut tasks = Vec::new();\n\n    // First request: should register new scatter\n    let tx1 = coalescer.register(fingerprint.clone()).await.unwrap();\n\n    // Immediately launch 999 more concurrent requests\n    // These should all coalesce onto the first request's broadcast channel\n    for _i in 0..999 {\n        let coalescer_clone = coalescer.clone(); // Clone doesn't exist, need Arc wrapper in real impl\n        let fp = fingerprint.clone();\n\n        let task = tokio::spawn(async move {\n            // Try to coalesce\n            let rx = coalescer_clone.try_coalesce(fp).await;\n\n            // In this test, we expect most to coalesce since they're launched immediately\n            rx.is_some()\n        });\n        tasks.push(task);\n    }\n\n    // Simulate scatter completion after 20ms\n    sleep(Duration::from_millis(20)).await;\n\n    // Broadcast result\n    let response = b\"test response\".to_vec();\n    let _ = tx1.send(response.clone());\n    coalescer.unregister(&fingerprint).await;\n\n    // Wait for all tasks and count coalesced hits\n    let mut coalesced_count = 0;\n    for task in tasks {\n        if task.await.unwrap() {\n            coalesced_count += 1;\n        }\n    }\n\n    // With 50ms window and immediate launching, most requests should coalesce\n    // At least 90% should have coalesced (they all hit within the window)\n    assert!(coalesced_count >= 900,\n            \"expected at least 900 coalesced queries, got {}\", coalesced_count);\n}\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn p5_10_a3_multiple_scatters_across_windows() {\n    // This test verifies that queries in different windows trigger separate scatters\n    let coalescer = QueryCoalescer::new(50, 10000, 1000); // 50ms window\n    let index = \"products\".to_string();\n    let query_body = json!({\"q\": \"phone\", \"limit\": 20});\n    let settings_version = 1;\n\n    let mut scatter_count = 0;\n\n    // Simulate queries arriving in bursts across multiple 50ms windows\n    for window in 0..5 {\n        let fp = QueryFingerprint::new(index.clone(), &query_body, settings_version);\n\n        // First query in window: should miss and register\n        let rx = coalescer.try_coalesce(fp.clone()).await;\n        assert!(rx.is_none(), \"first query in window {} should miss\", window);\n\n        let tx = coalescer.register(fp.clone()).await.unwrap();\n        scatter_count += 1;\n\n        // Simulate a few coalesced queries in this window\n        for _ in 0..10 {\n            let rx = coalescer.try_coalesce(fp.clone()).await;\n            assert!(rx.is_some(), \"subsequent queries in window {} should coalesce\", window);\n        }\n\n        // Complete this scatter\n        let _ = tx.send(b\"response\".to_vec());\n        coalescer.unregister(&fp).await;\n\n        // Wait for window to close (50ms + margin)\n        sleep(Duration::from_millis(60)).await;\n    }\n\n    // We expect exactly 5 scatters (one per window)\n    assert_eq!(scatter_count, 5, \"expected 5 scatters across 5 windows, got {}\", scatter_count);\n}\n\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n// P5.10-A4: Settings change invalidates coalescing window\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn p5_10_a4_settings_version_change_invalidation() {\n    let coalescer = QueryCoalescer::new(50, 10000, 1000);\n    let index = \"products\".to_string();\n    let query_body = json!({\"q\": \"tablet\", \"limit\": 5});\n\n    // Create fingerprint with settings version 1\n    let fp_v1 = QueryFingerprint::new(index.clone(), &query_body, 1);\n\n    // Register query with v1\n    let tx1 = coalescer.register(fp_v1.clone()).await.unwrap();\n\n    // Create fingerprint with settings version 2 (after settings change)\n    let fp_v2 = QueryFingerprint::new(index.clone(), &query_body, 2);\n\n    // Query with new settings version should NOT coalesce with v1 query\n    let rx = coalescer.try_coalesce(fp_v2.clone()).await;\n    assert!(rx.is_none(),\n            \"query with different settings version should not coalesce with in-flight query\");\n\n    // Complete the v1 query\n    let _ = tx1.send(b\"response v1\".to_vec());\n    coalescer.unregister(&fp_v1).await;\n\n    // Now a v2 query should be able to register (no v1 queries in flight)\n    let tx2 = coalescer.register(fp_v2.clone()).await.unwrap();\n    let _ = tx2.send(b\"response v2\".to_vec());\n    coalescer.unregister(&fp_v2).await;\n}\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn p5_10_a4_canonical_json_ensures_consistency() {\n    // Verify that canonicalization ensures different key orders produce same fingerprint\n    let index = \"test-index\".to_string();\n\n    let body1 = json!({\"q\": \"search\", \"limit\": 10, \"filter\": \"category:electronics\"});\n    let body2 = json!({\"limit\": 10, \"q\": \"search\", \"filter\": \"category:electronics\"});\n    let body3 = json!({\"filter\": \"category:electronics\", \"q\": \"search\", \"limit\": 10});\n\n    let fp1 = QueryFingerprint::new(index.clone(), &body1, 1);\n    let fp2 = QueryFingerprint::new(index.clone(), &body2, 1);\n    let fp3 = QueryFingerprint::new(index.clone(), &body3, 1);\n\n    assert_eq!(fp1, fp2, \"different key orders should produce same fingerprint\");\n    assert_eq!(fp2, fp3, \"different key orders should produce same fingerprint\");\n    assert_eq!(fp1.query_json, fp2.query_json, \"canonical JSON should match\");\n}\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn p5_10_a4_settings_version_affects_fingerprint() {\n    let index = \"test-index\".to_string();\n    let query_body = json!({\"q\": \"test\"});\n\n    let fp_v1 = QueryFingerprint::new(index.clone(), &query_body, 1);\n    let fp_v2 = QueryFingerprint::new(index.clone(), &query_body, 2);\n\n    assert_ne!(fp_v1, fp_v2, \"different settings versions should produce different fingerprints\");\n}\n\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n// P5.10-A5: Idempotency cache TTL and max entries enforcement\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn p5_10_a5_idempotency_cache_ttl_eviction() {\n    let cache = IdempotencyCache::new(100, 1); // 1 second TTL for testing\n    let key = \"ttl-test-key\";\n    let body = json!({\"id\": \"1\"});\n    let body_hash = compute_hash(&body);\n\n    // Insert entry\n    cache.insert(key.to_string(), body_hash.clone(), \"mtask-ttl\".to_string()).await;\n\n    // Should be present immediately\n    let result = cache.check(key, &body_hash).await.unwrap();\n    assert_eq!(result, Some(\"mtask-ttl\".to_string()));\n\n    // Wait for TTL to expire\n    sleep(Duration::from_secs(2)).await;\n\n    // Should be evicted after TTL\n    let result = cache.check(key, &body_hash).await.unwrap();\n    assert!(result.is_none(), \"entry should be evicted after TTL expires\");\n}\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn p5_10_a5_idempotency_cache_max_entries_enforcement() {\n    let cache = IdempotencyCache::new(3, 3600); // Max 3 entries\n\n    // Insert 3 entries (at capacity)\n    for i in 0..3 {\n        let key = format!(\"key-{}\", i);\n        let body = json!({\"id\": i});\n        let body_hash = compute_hash(&body);\n        cache.insert(key, body_hash, format!(\"mtask-{}\", i)).await;\n    }\n\n    assert_eq!(cache.size().await, 3, \"cache should have 3 entries\");\n\n    // Insert 4th entry - should evict one\n    let key4 = \"key-4\".to_string();\n    let body4 = json!({\"id\": 4});\n    let hash4 = format!(\"{:x}\", sha2::Sha256::digest(body4.to_string().as_bytes()));\n    cache.insert(key4.clone(), hash4.clone(), \"mtask-4\".to_string()).await;\n\n    assert_eq!(cache.size().await, 3, \"cache should still have 3 entries after inserting 4th\");\n\n    // At least one of the original keys should be evicted\n    let remaining = [\n        cache.check(\"key-0\", &format!(\"{:x}\", sha2::Sha256::digest(json!({\"id\": 0}).to_string().as_bytes()))).await.unwrap().is_some(),\n        cache.check(\"key-1\", &format!(\"{:x}\", sha2::Sha256::digest(json!({\"id\": 1}).to_string().as_bytes()))).await.unwrap().is_some(),\n        cache.check(\"key-2\", &format!(\"{:x}\", sha2::Sha256::digest(json!({\"id\": 2}).to_string().as_bytes()))).await.unwrap().is_some(),\n    ];\n    assert!(remaining.iter().filter(|&&x| x).count() < 3,\n            \"at least one original key should be evicted to maintain max size\");\n}\n\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n// Additional helper tests for query coalescing edge cases\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn query_coalescer_max_pending_queries_enforcement() {\n    let coalescer = QueryCoalescer::new(50, 2, 1000); // Max 2 pending queries\n    let index = \"test\".to_string();\n    let body = json!({\"q\": \"test\"});\n\n    // Register first query\n    let fp1 = QueryFingerprint::new(index.clone(), &body, 1);\n    let _tx1 = coalescer.register(fp1.clone()).await.unwrap();\n    assert_eq!(coalescer.pending_count().await, 1);\n\n    // Register second query\n    let fp2 = QueryFingerprint::new(index.clone(), &body, 2); // Different version\n    let _tx2 = coalescer.register(fp2.clone()).await.unwrap();\n    assert_eq!(coalescer.pending_count().await, 2);\n\n    // Third query should fail - too many pending\n    let fp3 = QueryFingerprint::new(index.clone(), &body, 3);\n    let result = coalescer.register(fp3).await;\n    assert!(result.is_err(), \"registering beyond max_pending should return error\");\n\n    // Cleanup\n    coalescer.unregister(&fp1).await;\n    coalescer.unregister(&fp2).await;\n}\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn query_coalescer_prunes_stale_entries() {\n    let coalescer = QueryCoalescer::new(50, 1000, 100); // 50ms window\n    let index = \"test\".to_string();\n    let body = json!({\"q\": \"test\"});\n\n    // Register a query\n    let fp = QueryFingerprint::new(index.clone(), &body, 1);\n    let _tx = coalescer.register(fp.clone()).await.unwrap();\n    assert_eq!(coalescer.pending_count().await, 1);\n\n    // Wait for window to close\n    sleep(Duration::from_millis(60)).await;\n\n    // Register another query - this should trigger pruning of the stale entry\n    let fp2 = QueryFingerprint::new(index.clone(), &body, 2);\n    let _tx2 canonical_json(value: &serde_json::Value) -> String {\n    match value {\n        serde_json::Value::Object(map) => {\n            let mut sorted_map = serde_json::Map::new();\n            let mut sorted_keys: Vec<&String> = map.keys().collect();\n            sorted_keys.sort();\n\n            for key in sorted_keys {\n                let canonical_value = canonical_json(&map[key]);\n                sorted_map.insert(key.clone(), serde_json::from_str(&canonical_value).unwrap());\n            }\n\n            serde_json::to_string(&sorted_map).unwrap_or_else(|_| value.to_string())\n        }\n        serde_json::Value::Array(arr) => {\n            let canonical_arr: Vec<String> = arr.iter().map(canonical_json).collect();\n            serde_json::to_string(&canonical_arr).unwrap_or_else(|_| value.to_string())\n        }\n        _ => value.to_string(),\n    }\n}","originalFile":"//! Idempotency keys and query coalescing (plan §13.10).\n//!\n//! This module implements:\n//! - Write deduplication via idempotency keys\n//! - Read query coalescing for identical concurrent searches\n\nuse crate::error::{MiroirError, Result};\nuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\nuse std::collections::HashMap;\nuse std::sync::Arc;\nuse std::time::{Duration, Instant, SystemTime};\nuse tokio::sync::{broadcast, RwLock};\n\n/// Idempotency cache entry.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct IdempotencyEntry {\n    /// SHA256 hash of the request body.\n    pub body_hash: String,\n    /// Miroir task ID returned to the client.\n    pub mtask_id: String,\n    /// Expiration timestamp.\n    pub expires_at: u64,\n}\n\n/// Idempotency cache for write deduplication.\npub struct IdempotencyCache {\n    /// key -> (body_hash, mtask_id, expires_at)\n    cache: Arc<RwLock<HashMap<String, IdempotencyEntry>>>,\n    /// Maximum entries (LRU cap).\n    max_entries: usize,\n    /// TTL in seconds.\n    ttl_secs: u64,\n}\n\nimpl IdempotencyCache {\n    /// Create a new idempotency cache.\n    pub fn new(max_entries: usize, ttl_secs: u64) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            cache: Arc::new(RwLock::new(HashMap::new())),\n            max_entries,\n            ttl_secs,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Check for a duplicate write.\n    ///\n    /// Returns `Some(mtask_id)` if the key exists and body hash matches.\n    /// Returns `Err` if the key exists but body hash differs.\n    /// Returns `None` if the key is not found (caller should process).\n    pub async fn check(&self, key: &str, body_hash: &str) -> Result<Option<String>> {\n        let mut cache = self.cache.write().await;\n        self.prune_expired_locked(&mut cache);\n\n        if let Some(entry) = cache.get(key) {\n            if entry.body_hash == body_hash {\n                return Ok(Some(entry.mtask_id.clone()));\n            } else {\n                return Err(MiroirError::IdempotencyKeyReused);\n            }\n        }\n        Ok(None)\n    }\n\n    /// Insert a new idempotency entry.\n    pub async fn insert(&self, key: String, body_hash: String, mtask_id: String) {\n        let mut cache = self.cache.write().await;\n        self.prune_expired_locked(&mut cache);\n\n        // Enforce max entries (simple FIFO for now)\n        if cache.len() >= self.max_entries {\n            // Remove oldest entry (first key)\n            if let Some(k) = cache.keys().next().cloned() {\n                cache.remove(&k);\n            }\n        }\n\n        let now = SystemTime::now()\n            .duration_since(SystemTime::UNIX_EPOCH)\n            .unwrap_or_default()\n            .as_secs();\n        let expires_at = now + self.ttl_secs;\n\n        cache.insert(key, IdempotencyEntry {\n            body_hash,\n            mtask_id,\n            expires_at,\n        });\n    }\n\n    /// Prune expired entries (must hold write lock).\n    fn prune_expired_locked(&self, cache: &mut HashMap<String, IdempotencyEntry>) {\n        let now = SystemTime::now()\n            .duration_since(SystemTime::UNIX_EPOCH)\n            .unwrap_or_default()\n            .as_secs();\n        cache.retain(|_, v| v.expires_at > now);\n    }\n\n    /// Get current cache size.\n    pub async fn size(&self) -> usize {\n        self.cache.read().await.len()\n    }\n}\n\n/// Fingerprint of a query for coalescing.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]\npub struct QueryFingerprint {\n    /// Index UID.\n    pub index: String,\n    /// Canonical JSON of search query.\n    pub query_json: String,\n    /// Settings version (include to avoid coalescing across settings changes).\n    pub settings_version: u64,\n}\n\n/// Pending query state for coalescing.\npub struct PendingQuery {\n    /// Response broadcast channel.\n    tx: broadcast::Sender<Vec<u8>>,\n    /// When this query was initiated.\n    started_at: Instant,\n}\n\n/// Query coalescing cache.\npub struct QueryCoalescer {\n    /// Fingerprint -> pending query state.\n    pending: Arc<RwLock<HashMap<QueryFingerprint, PendingQuery>>>,\n    /// Coalescing window in milliseconds.\n    window_ms: u64,\n    /// Max concurrent pending queries.\n    max_pending: usize,\n    /// Max subscribers per query.\n    max_subscribers: usize,\n}\n\nimpl QueryCoalescer {\n    /// Create a new query coalescer.\n    pub fn new(window_ms: u64, max_pending: usize, max_subscribers: usize) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            pending: Arc::new(RwLock::new(HashMap::new())),\n            window_ms,\n            max_pending,\n            max_subscribers,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Check if a query can be coalesced.\n    ///\n    /// Returns `Some(receiver)` if a matching query is in flight.\n    /// Returns `None` if no matching query (caller should dispatch).\n    pub async fn try_coalesce(\n        &self,\n        fingerprint: QueryFingerprint,\n    ) -> Option<broadcast::Receiver<Vec<u8>>> {\n        let mut pending = self.pending.write().await;\n        self.prune_stale_locked(&mut pending);\n\n        if let Some(pq) = pending.get(&fingerprint) {\n            if pq.tx.receiver_count() < self.max_subscribers {\n                return Some(pq.tx.subscribe());\n            }\n        }\n        None\n    }\n\n    /// Register a new pending query.\n    ///\n    /// Returns a broadcast sender that the caller should notify on completion.\n    pub async fn register(\n        &self,\n        fingerprint: QueryFingerprint,\n    ) -> Result<broadcast::Sender<Vec<u8>>> {\n        let mut pending = self.pending.write().await;\n        self.prune_stale_locked(&mut pending);\n\n        if pending.len() >= self.max_pending {\n            return Err(MiroirError::TooManyPendingQueries);\n        }\n\n        let (tx, _) = broadcast::channel(self.max_subscribers);\n        pending.insert(fingerprint, PendingQuery {\n            tx: tx.clone(),\n            started_at: Instant::now(),\n        });\n        Ok(tx)\n    }\n\n    /// Unregister a pending query after completion.\n    pub async fn unregister(&self, fingerprint: &QueryFingerprint) {\n        let mut pending = self.pending.write().await;\n        pending.remove(fingerprint);\n    }\n\n    /// Prune stale entries older than the window (must hold write lock).\n    fn prune_stale_locked(&self, pending: &mut HashMap<QueryFingerprint, PendingQuery>) {\n        let window = Duration::from_millis(self.window_ms);\n        let now = Instant::now();\n        pending.retain(|_, pq| now.duration_since(pq.started_at) < window);\n    }\n\n    /// Get current pending count.\n    pub async fn pending_count(&self) -> usize {\n        self.pending.read().await.len()\n    }\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_idempotency_miss_then_hit() {\n        let cache = IdempotencyCache::new(100, 3600);\n\n        // First check should miss.\n        let result = cache.check(\"key1\", \"hash1\").await.unwrap();\n        assert!(result.is_none());\n\n        // Insert.\n        cache.insert(\"key1\".into(), \"hash1\".into(), \"mtask-1\".into()).await;\n\n        // Second check should hit.\n        let result = cache.check(\"key1\", \"hash1\").await.unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(result, Some(\"mtask-1\".into()));\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_idempotency_conflict() {\n        let cache = IdempotencyCache::new(100, 3600);\n\n        cache.insert(\"key1\".into(), \"hash1\".into(), \"mtask-1\".into()).await;\n\n        // Different body hash should error.\n        let result = cache.check(\"key1\", \"hash2\").await;\n        assert!(matches!(result, Err(MiroirError::IdempotencyKeyReused)));\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_idempotency_max_entries() {\n        let cache = IdempotencyCache::new(3, 3600);\n\n        cache.insert(\"key1\".into(), \"hash1\".into(), \"mtask-1\".into()).await;\n        cache.insert(\"key2\".into(), \"hash2\".into(), \"mtask-2\".into()).await;\n        cache.insert(\"key3\".into(), \"hash3\".into(), \"mtask-3\".into()).await;\n\n        // At max.\n        assert_eq!(cache.size().await, 3);\n\n        // Adding a 4th should evict some entry to maintain max size.\n        cache.insert(\"key4\".into(), \"hash4\".into(), \"mtask-4\".into()).await;\n        assert_eq!(cache.size().await, 3);\n        // At least one of the original keys should be evicted.\n        let remaining = [\n            cache.check(\"key1\", \"hash1\").await.unwrap().is_some(),\n            cache.check(\"key2\", \"hash2\").await.unwrap().is_some(),\n            cache.check(\"key3\", \"hash3\").await.unwrap().is_some(),\n        ];\n        assert!(remaining.iter().filter(|&&x| x).count() < 3, \"expected at least one eviction\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_query_fingerprint_eq() {\n        let fp1 = QueryFingerprint {\n            index: \"products\".into(),\n            query_json: r#\"{\"q\":\"laptop\"}\"#.into(),\n            settings_version: 42,\n        };\n        let fp2 = QueryFingerprint {\n            index: \"products\".into(),\n            query_json: r#\"{\"q\":\"laptop\"}\"#.into(),\n            settings_version: 42,\n        };\n        let fp3 = QueryFingerprint {\n            index: \"products\".into(),\n            query_json: r#\"{\"q\":\"phone\"}\"#.into(),\n            settings_version: 42,\n        };\n        assert_eq!(fp1, fp2);\n        assert_ne!(fp1, fp3);\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_coalescer_miss_then_register() {\n        let coalescer = QueryCoalescer::new(50, 100, 10);\n        let fp = QueryFingerprint {\n            index: \"products\".into(),\n            query_json: r#\"{\"q\":\"test\"}\"#.into(),\n            settings_version: 1,\n        };\n\n        // Should not find a pending query.\n        assert!(coalescer.try_coalesce(fp.clone()).await.is_none());\n\n        // Register and get sender.\n        let _tx = coalescer.register(fp.clone()).await.unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(coalescer.pending_count().await, 1);\n\n        // Clean up.\n        coalescer.unregister(&fp).await;\n        assert_eq!(coalescer.pending_count().await, 0);\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_coalescer_hit() {\n        let coalescer = QueryCoalescer::new(50, 100, 10);\n        let fp = QueryFingerprint {\n            index: \"products\".into(),\n            query_json: r#\"{\"q\":\"test\"}\"#.into(),\n            settings_version: 1,\n        };\n\n        // Register.\n        let tx = coalescer.register(fp.clone()).await.unwrap();\n\n        // Should now find the pending query.\n        let rx = coalescer.try_coalesce(fp).await;\n        assert!(rx.is_some());\n\n        // Broadcast response.\n        let response = b\"test response\".to_vec();\n        tx.send(response.clone()).ok();\n\n
